blocks:
Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ ٱتَّقُوا۟ رَبَّكُمُ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَكُم مِّن نَّفْسٍ وَٰحِدَةٍ وَخَلَقَ مِنْهَا زَوْجَهَا وَبَثَّ مِنْهُمَا رِجَالًا كَثِيرًا وَنِسَآءً ۚ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ٱلَّذِى تَسَآءَلُونَ بِهِۦ وَٱلْأَرْحَامَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ عَلَيْكُمْ رَقِيبًا (۱)
O mankind! Be wary of your Lord who created you from a single soul, and created its mate from it, and from the two of them scattered numerous men and women. Be wary of Allah, in whose Name you adjure one another and [of severing ties with] blood relations. Indeed Allah is watchful over you. (1)

Key Points

| Amir al-Mu'minin Ali عليه السلام said: Maintain kinship ties (Silat al-Rahim), even if it is only by giving a greeting (Salam). Allah said: «و اتّقوا اللّه... و الارحام...» (225)

225) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 150.

Messages

1- The beginning of the Sūrah concerning the family starts with a recommendation for piety (Taqwa), meaning the foundation of the family rests upon piety, and observing it is obligatory upon everyone. «یا ایّها الناس اتّقوا»

2- The creation and the nurturing of humanity are both in the hands of Allah; therefore, reverence and obedience must also be directed towards Him. «اتّقوا ربكم الّذى خلقكم»

3- All human beings are of one kind; thus, every type of racial, linguistic, regional, etc., discrimination is forbidden. «خلقكم من نفس واحدة»

4- Men and women share unity in creation, and neither has superiority over the other based on gender. «خلق منها زوجها»

5- All human beings throughout history originate from one father and one mother. «بثّ منهما رجالاً...» There are two types of narrations regarding the marriage of Adam’s children. Those narrations that are consistent with the Quran state that Adam’s children married one another. (226)

6- In educational matters, repetition is a fundamental principle. «اتّقوا ربّكم... اتّقوا اللّه»

7- Observing the rights of family and relatives is a prerequisite for piety (Taqwa). «اتّقوا اللّه... والارحام» Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The kinship of those attached to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله must be observed with affection (Mawaddah). (227)

226) Tafsir al-Mizan.

227) Bihar, Vol. 23, p. 257.

Explanations

| Portrait of Surah An-Nisa This Sūrah has one hundred and seventy-six verses and was revealed in Medina. Its content includes the call to faith and justice, taking lessons from previous nations, severing friendly relations with the enemies of Allah, supporting orphans, rulings on marriage and inheritance, the necessity of obeying the Divine Leader, migration (Hijrah), striving (Jihad) in the way of Allah, and more. Since the first thirty-five verses of this Sūrah concern family matters, it has been named Sūrat An-Nisa (The Women).

Ayah

وَءَاتُوا۟ ٱلْيَتَـٰمَىٰٓ أَمْوَٰلَهُمْ ۖ وَلَا تَتَبَدَّلُوا۟ ٱلْخَبِيثَ بِٱلطَّيِّبِ ۖ وَلَا تَأْكُلُوٓا۟ أَمْوَٰلَهُمْ إِلَىٰٓ أَمْوَٰلِكُمْ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ حُوبًا كَبِيرًا (۲)
Give the orphans their property, and do not replace the good with the bad, and do not eat up their property [by mingling it] with your own property, for that is indeed a great sin. (2)

Messages

1- The wealth of the orphans must be returned to them, even if they themselves do not know or forget it. «آتوا الیتامى أموالهم»

2- Islam is the protector of the deprived and weak class. «الیتامى»

3- Orphan children have the right of ownership. «اموالهم»

4- Preventing, exchanging, or diminishing the orphan's property is a great sin. «انّه كان حوباً كبیراً»

5- Sins have major and minor categories. «حوباً كبیراً»

Ayah

وَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَلَّا تُقْسِطُوا۟ فِى ٱلْيَتَـٰمَىٰ فَٱنكِحُوا۟ مَا طَابَ لَكُم مِّنَ ٱلنِّسَآءِ مَثْنَىٰ وَثُلَـٰثَ وَرُبَـٰعَ ۖ فَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَلَّا تَعْدِلُوا۟ فَوَٰحِدَةً أَوْ مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَـٰنُكُمْ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ أَدْنَىٰٓ أَلَّا تَعُولُوا۟ (۳)
If you fear that you may not deal justly with the orphans, then marry [other] women that you like, two, three, or four. But if you fear that you may not treat them fairly, then [marry only] one, or [marry from among] your slave-women. That makes it likelier that you will not be unfair. (3)

Key Points

Some men would bring female orphans into their homes for guardianship and care, then marry them with a low dowry and easily divorce them. The Ayah was revealed stating that if you fear injustice, marry other women. The previous Ayah was about observing justice regarding the property of orphans; this Ayah commands justice in marrying female orphans. Regarding the wisdom of polygyny, the following points can be mentioned: A: Male casualties in wars and accidents are higher, leading to women becoming widows. B: Young men are less willing to marry widows. C: Not all widows and single women possess sufficient piety and self-control. D: Women have an excuse (menstruation) for a period each month, and marital relations are suspended. The result is that, in order to protect the rights of widows, a man may take a second wife under certain conditions so that the problem of both parties is solved. Question: In this Ayah, the issue of injustice is expressed as doubtful: «ان خفتم الا تقسطوا» (if you fear that you will not be just), but in Ayah 129 of the same Surah, it says: «لن تستطیعوا» (you will never be able), meaning you do not have the power for it. How can these two Ayahs be reconciled? Answer: In material matters such as clothing, housing, food, and cohabitation, you must be just, and if you fear you will not be just, set up a different plan for yourselves. However, in Ayah 129, He says: you will never have the power for justice, which refers to justice in affection and heartfelt inclinations, because affection is not under human control so that one can divide it equally. This answer is based on a Hadith from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام.[228]

228) Wasā’il, Vol. 21, p. 345.

Messages

  1. Islam is the protector of the deprived, especially orphans, and specifically female orphans, particularly concerning chastity and marriage, and avoiding their exploitation. «...فى الیتامى»

  2. It is not necessary to be certain of injustice to refrain from marrying orphans; the possibility and fear of it are sufficient. «ان خفتم»

  3. In choosing a spouse, one may seek orphans, unless there is a fear of injustice. «ان خفتم... فانكحوا»

  4. In choosing a spouse, heartfelt inclination is a principle. «طاب لكم»

  5. Islam generally agrees with polygyny for men. «فانكحوا... مثنى و ثلاث و رباع فان خفتم... فواحدة»

  6. Polygyny is both limited to four wives and conditional upon observance (of justice).[229] «فانكحوا... رباع فان خفتم... فواحدة»

229) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 439; Kāfī, Vol. 5, p. 363.

Ayah

وَءَاتُوا۟ ٱلنِّسَآءَ صَدُقَـٰتِهِنَّ نِحْلَةً ۚ فَإِن طِبْنَ لَكُمْ عَن شَىْءٍ مِّنْهُ نَفْسًا فَكُلُوهُ هَنِيٓـًٔا مَّرِيٓـًٔا (٤)
Give women their dowries, handing it over to them; but if they remit anything of it of their own accord, then consume it as [something] lawful and wholesome. (4)

Key Points

The word niḥlah, according to Raghib Isfahani, derives from naḥl, meaning honeybee. Since the honeybee gives honey to people without expecting anything in return, a gift is also called niḥlah. Of course, some commentators have taken niḥlah to mean dayn (debt). Meaning, the mahr (dower) is a definite debt and obligation upon the man. It is stated in a hadith: Allocate your best wealth for three expenditures: 1. Mahr (dower). 2. Hajj. 3. Shroud (Kafan). If you spend the best of your wealth on the mahr, your children will become righteous. (230)

(230) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan concerning the Ayah.

Messages

1- Paying the mahr (dower) to the woman is her right and is obligatory. «و آتوا النساء...»

2- The mahr is not the price of the woman, but rather a sign of the man's sincerity in his affection and love for his wife. «صدقاتهن»

3- The woman is the owner of her mahr. The father and relatives of the woman do not have the right to take her mahr for themselves. (231) «آتوا النّساء صدقاتهن»

4- The mahr is not the price of the woman, but rather the man's gift to his wife. «نحلة»

5- The woman is free and independent in receiving or forgiving the mahr. «فان طبن لكم»

6- Pleasant wealth is wealth that its owner gives willingly and with satisfaction. «فان طبن... هنیئاً»

7- Outward consent is not sufficient. Inner consent is necessary. Gifts given under duress, coercion, or out of obligation (social pressure) are not valid. «طبن... نفساً»

8- Dower (mahr) and gift (hibah) are means of ownership. «صدقاتهنّ، فَان طبن لكم عن شى ء»

9- Women should not forgive their entire mahr under the influence of emotions. «شى ء منه»

(231) Because in the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah) and among some tribes, the father or relatives would take the woman's mahr, Allah, in the Quran, first mentioned the recipient and said: «و اتوا النساء» (Give the dower to the women themselves), and did not say: «واتوا صدقات النساء» (Pay the dowers of the women), which would have allowed the recipient to be anyone.

Ayah

وَلَا تُؤْتُوا۟ ٱلسُّفَهَآءَ أَمْوَٰلَكُمُ ٱلَّتِى جَعَلَ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمْ قِيَـٰمًا وَٱرْزُقُوهُمْ فِيهَا وَٱكْسُوهُمْ وَقُولُوا۟ لَهُمْ قَوْلًا مَّعْرُوفًا (٥)
Do not give the feeble-minded your property, which Allah has assigned you to manage: provide for them out of it and clothe them, and speak to them honourable words. (5)

Key Points

In narrations (Riwāyāt), drunkards and transgressors (fāsiq) are called "sufahā'" (the foolish/incompetent).[232] Therefore, public funds should not be placed in the hands of such individuals, because wealth is the pillar (qawām) of society, and entrusting any financial position or responsibility to these individuals is a betrayal of society. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said to a person: Protect your wealth, because wealth is the cause of the stability (qawām) of your religion. He then recited this Ayah: «و لاتؤتوا السفهاء اموالكم التى جعل اللّه لكم قیاماً...»[233] Imam Baqir عليه السلام said regarding the meaning of «السفهاء»: The intent is someone whom you do not trust.[234]

232) كافى، ج 5، ص 299. 233) وسائل، ج 7، ص 126. 234) وسائل، ج 16، ص 369.

Messages

1- The incompetent (safīh) is prohibited from disposing of his own wealth. «لاتؤتوا السفهاء»

2- Wealth that causes the establishment of society should not be placed at the disposal of the incompetent. «اموالكم التى... قیاماً»

3- Even the control of the incompetent over his own wealth is an economic blow to society, and society has a right over the personal wealth of individuals. «اموالكم» instead of «اموالهم»

4- In interactions, attention must be paid to the economic interests of society and the intellectual growth of individuals, not to fleeting emotions or pity. «لاتؤتوا السفهاء اموالكم»

5- Wealth and riches are the stability (qawām) of life and the lever of the system. «جعل اللّه لكم قیاماً»

6- Capital should not remain stagnant. The wealth of orphans and the incompetent must also be placed in the cycle of production and profitability, and their lives should be sustained by its income, not by the principal wealth itself. «وارزقوهم فیها» instead of «منها»

7- Attention must be paid to the spiritual dimensions and personality of the deprived. «قولوا لهم قولاً معروفا»

8- Prohibiting individuals from disposing of their wealth generates a reaction. The solution is good conduct and kind speech. «قولاً معروفا»

Ayah

وَٱبْتَلُوا۟ ٱلْيَتَـٰمَىٰ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا بَلَغُوا۟ ٱلنِّكَاحَ فَإِنْ ءَانَسْتُم مِّنْهُمْ رُشْدًا فَٱدْفَعُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِمْ أَمْوَٰلَهُمْ ۖ وَلَا تَأْكُلُوهَآ إِسْرَافًا وَبِدَارًا أَن يَكْبَرُوا۟ ۚ وَمَن كَانَ غَنِيًّا فَلْيَسْتَعْفِفْ ۖ وَمَن كَانَ فَقِيرًا فَلْيَأْكُلْ بِٱلْمَعْرُوفِ ۚ فَإِذَا دَفَعْتُمْ إِلَيْهِمْ أَمْوَٰلَهُمْ فَأَشْهِدُوا۟ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ حَسِيبًا (٦)
Test the orphans when they reach the age of marriage. Then if you discern in them maturity, deliver to them their property. And do not consume it lavishly and hastily lest they should grow up. As for him who is well-off, let him be abstemious, and as for him who is poor, let him eat in an honourable manner. And when you deliver to them their property, take witnesses over them, and Allah suffices as reckoner. (6)

Messages

Regulations for the disbursement of orphans' property

1- Test the orphans before puberty regarding their economic maturity, and help them grow by providing training and teaching them methods of trade and transaction. «وابتلوا الیتامى...»

2- The orphan has the right to dispose of his property under the supervision of his guardian. The requirement for acting upon «وابتلوا» is that the orphan should manage the property under the supervision of his guardian so that he may be tested.

3- In order to take possession of capital, economic and social maturity is necessary, in addition to sexual maturity. «اذابلغوالنكاح فان آنستم منهم رشداً»

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The meaning of «آنستم رشدا» is that he must be able to safeguard his property. [235]

4- Conjecture and speculation are not sufficient when handing over the orphan's property to him. You must be certain of his maturity. «آنستم منهم رشداً»

5- Ownership is not always accompanied by the permission to dispose of property. The orphan is the owner, but until he attains maturity, he does not have the right to dispose of it. «فَان انستم منهم رُشداً»

6- Affluent individuals should perform social services without expectation of reward. «من كان غنیاً فلیستعفف»

7- When taking compensation for services, consider the customary limit. «فلیأكل بالمعروف»

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The meaning of «فلیاكل بالمعروف» is the amount necessary to satisfy one's hunger. [236]

8- Safeguard both the orphans' property and your own reputation by taking witnesses and preventing future disputes and accusations. «فاشهدواعلیهم»

9- The testimony of people is for preserving worldly honor, and the testimony of Allah is for the honor of the Hereafter. «وكفى باللّه حسیباً»

10- Taking witnesses in society ends disputes, but the reckoning of the Day of Judgment remains firm. «وكفى باللّه حسیباً»

235) Man La Yahduruhu al-Faqih, Vol. 4, p. 223.

236) Kafi, Vol. 5, p. 130.

Ayah

لِّلرِّجَالِ نَصِيبٌ مِّمَّا تَرَكَ ٱلْوَٰلِدَانِ وَٱلْأَقْرَبُونَ وَلِلنِّسَآءِ نَصِيبٌ مِّمَّا تَرَكَ ٱلْوَٰلِدَانِ وَٱلْأَقْرَبُونَ مِمَّا قَلَّ مِنْهُ أَوْ كَثُرَ ۚ نَصِيبًا مَّفْرُوضًا (۷)
Men have a share in the heritage left by parents and near relatives, and women have a share in the heritage left by parents and near relatives, whether it be little or much, a share ordained [by Allah]. (7)

Messages

1- Women, like men, have the right to inheritance, and religion safeguards their rights. «للرّجال نصیب... للنّساء نصیب»

2- Inheritance is one of the means of ownership. «للرّجال نصیبٌ»

3- The relative who is closer has priority in inheritance. «الاقربون»

4- The just division of the inheritance is important, not its amount. «قَلّ منه او كثر»

5- The share of inheritance is unchangeable. «نصیباً مفروضا»

Ayah

وَإِذَا حَضَرَ ٱلْقِسْمَةَ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ وَٱلْيَتَـٰمَىٰ وَٱلْمَسَـٰكِينُ فَٱرْزُقُوهُم مِّنْهُ وَقُولُوا۟ لَهُمْ قَوْلًا مَّعْرُوفًا (۸)
And when the division is attended by relatives, the orphans and the needy, provide for them out of it, and speak to them honourable words. (8)

Key Points

The fact that the word «اولواالقربى» (relatives) appears alongside «الیتامى» (the orphans) and «المساكین» (the needy) indicates that the intended relatives are those who are poor and do not naturally inherit. The emotional recommendation at the end of the verse also confirms this meaning.

Messages

1- Pay attention to the glances, presence, and natural expectations of the deprived. «واذا حضر القسمة»

2- Do not divide inherited wealth secretly, so that others may also have the possibility of being present. «حَضر القسمة اولوا القربى»

3- In dividing the inheritance, besides those who have a specified share, also think of the deprived and the orphans. «نصیباً مفروضاً... فارزقوهم منه»

4- Prevent grudges and jealousies and strengthen family ties through financial gifts and sweet words. «فارزقوهم ... قولوا»

5- Material gifts should be accompanied by spiritual affection and emotions. «قولوا لهم قولاً معروفاً»

Ayah

وَلْيَخْشَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَوْ تَرَكُوا۟ مِنْ خَلْفِهِمْ ذُرِّيَّةً ضِعَـٰفًا خَافُوا۟ عَلَيْهِمْ فَلْيَتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَلْيَقُولُوا۟ قَوْلًا سَدِيدًا (۹)
Let those fear [the result of mistreating orphans] who, were they to leave behind weak offspring, would be concerned on their account. So let them be wary of Allah, and let them speak upright words. (9)

Key Points

According to narrations (Riwāyāt), consuming the property of the orphan has both worldly and otherworldly repercussions. In this world, it results in harm to one's children, which is mentioned in this Ayah, and in the Hereafter, it brings the torment of Hellfire, which is mentioned in the subsequent Ayah. [237] It is possible that the intent of the Ayah is to prohibit unbalanced charity (infāq) and bequests (waṣiyyahs), meaning: beware lest you dedicate or donate all your wealth while having young and weak children, thereby abandoning your offspring to poverty and misery after your death. [238] And it is possible that the address of the Ayah is directed at those who have developmentally delayed children, so that they may secure their future through planning. [239] There is a connection between generations in the attainment of good (khayr) or evil (sharr), because the effects of an action are not limited to our lifespan. The evil of a father's deed reaches the child, and conversely, the offspring benefits from the effects of the father's good deed. The story of Moses and Khidr and the repair of the wall that contained the orphans' treasure is an example of this, concerning which the Quran states: «كان أبوهماصالحاً» [240]

237) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 238) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān. 239) Tafsir Kabir Fakhr al-Razi. 240) Kahf, 82.

Messages

1- One must put oneself in the place of others to better understand their pains. We should treat the orphans of others as we would like our own orphans to be treated. «تركوا من خلفهم ذرّيّةً ضعافاً»

2- Whoever lights a fire, the smoke goes into his own eyes. Today's oppression of the orphans of others becomes a tradition (sunnah) in society and tomorrow it will affect our own orphans. «وليخش الّذين لو تركوا...»

3- In methods of propagation (tablīgh), emotions and innate dispositions (fiṭriyyāt) must also be utilized. «ذرّيّة ضعافاً»

4- Neither treachery regarding the orphans' property nor harshness in speech toward them. «فليتّقوااللّه وليقولوا...»

5- Orphans, besides clothing and food, also need love, affection, and guidance (irshād). «وليقولوا قولاً سديداً»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ أَمْوَٰلَ ٱلْيَتَـٰمَىٰ ظُلْمًا إِنَّمَا يَأْكُلُونَ فِى بُطُونِهِمْ نَارًا ۖ وَسَيَصْلَوْنَ سَعِيرًا (۱٠)
Indeed those who consume the property of orphans wrongfully, only ingest fire into their bellies, and soon they will enter the Blaze. (10)

Key Points

Allamah Tabataba'i considered this verse proof of the embodiment of deeds on the Day of Resurrection, because the Quran states: the property of the orphan turns into fire and burns the one who consumes it.

Messages

1- The true face of consuming the orphan's property in this world will become manifest in the form of consuming fire on the Day of Resurrection. «یأكلون اموال الیتامى... یأكلون... نارا»

2- Being present in the homes of orphans and consuming from their property is permissible, provided it does not cause them harm or loss. «یأكلون... ظلما»

Ayah

يُوصِيكُمُ ٱللَّهُ فِىٓ أَوْلَـٰدِكُمْ ۖ لِلذَّكَرِ مِثْلُ حَظِّ ٱلْأُنثَيَيْنِ ۚ فَإِن كُنَّ نِسَآءً فَوْقَ ٱثْنَتَيْنِ فَلَهُنَّ ثُلُثَا مَا تَرَكَ ۖ وَإِن كَانَتْ وَٰحِدَةً فَلَهَا ٱلنِّصْفُ ۚ وَلِأَبَوَيْهِ لِكُلِّ وَٰحِدٍ مِّنْهُمَا ٱلسُّدُسُ مِمَّا تَرَكَ إِن كَانَ لَهُۥ وَلَدٌ ۚ فَإِن لَّمْ يَكُن لَّهُۥ وَلَدٌ وَوَرِثَهُۥٓ أَبَوَاهُ فَلِأُمِّهِ ٱلثُّلُثُ ۚ فَإِن كَانَ لَهُۥٓ إِخْوَةٌ فَلِأُمِّهِ ٱلسُّدُسُ ۚ مِنۢ بَعْدِ وَصِيَّةٍ يُوصِى بِهَآ أَوْ دَيْنٍ ۗ ءَابَآؤُكُمْ وَأَبْنَآؤُكُمْ لَا تَدْرُونَ أَيُّهُمْ أَقْرَبُ لَكُمْ نَفْعًا ۚ فَرِيضَةً مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًا (۱۱)
Allah enjoins you concerning your children: for the male shall be the like of the share of two females, and if there be [two or] more than two females, then for them shall be two-thirds of what he leaves; but if she be alone, then for her shall be a half; and for each of his parents a sixth of what he leaves, if he has children; but if he has no children, and his parents are his [sole] heirs, then it shall be a third for his mother; but if he has brothers, then a sixth for his mother, after [paying off] any bequest he may have made or any debt [he may have incurred]. Your parents and your children—you do not know which of them is likelier to be beneficial for you. This is an ordinance from Allah. Indeed Allah is all-knowing, all-wise. (11)

Key Points

Although the inheritance received by a woman is half that of a man, her utilization (or benefit) is double that of a man. Suppose the total amount is 30, of which the daughter takes 10 and the son takes 20. But because the daughter has no financial obligations, she saves her share and benefits from the son's share (which was 20) for living expenses. Thus, she has secured her needs from the man's share and kept her own share saved. Therefore, the daughter receives half in acquisition, but is fully secured in utilization. In a hadith from Imam Jawad عليه السلام, it is stated: No individual or social expense is the responsibility of the woman; furthermore, she receives a mahr (dowry) upon marriage, while the man both pays the mahr and is responsible for the woman's expenses. The woman receives her share of inheritance without any responsibility and has the right to save it, but in utilization, she benefits from the man's share itself. (241)

The rulings of inheritance are also stated in the Torah (242), and it is mentioned in the Gospel that Christ said: I have not come to change the rulings of the Torah. The Arabs of the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah) deprived women and children of inheritance, but Islam established a share of inheritance for every child and spouse, commensurate with their needs. The inheritance reaching the child is an incentive for greater activity. If a person knows that his wealth will not reach his child, he will not show much motivation. In France, when the law of inheritance was abolished, economic activities weakened. The greater share of the deceased's children compared to His parents is perhaps because the parents are passing their final years of life and have fewer needs. If the deceased had no brothers, the mother's share is one-third, and if he did have brothers, it is one-sixth, and the remainder is for the father. Perhaps this is because the expenses of the deceased's brothers are usually the responsibility of the father, not the mother. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Paying the debt of the deceased takes precedence over executing his will (243), just as his will should not be carried out if it is against the Sharia (Islamic law). (244) A person was worried because his wife had given birth to a daughter. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام recited this part of the ayah for him: «لاتدرون ایّهم اقرب لكم نفعاً» (245)

241) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn. 242) Torah, Book of Numbers, Chapter 27, verses 8-11. 243) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn. 244) Bihar, Vol. 100, p. 201 and Tafsir Qummi, Vol. 1, p. 65. 245) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

  1. Islam is the religion of Fitrah (innate nature). Just as the existence of the child is the continuation of the parents' existence, and their physical and spiritual characteristics are transferred to the child, wealth must also be transferred to the children. «یوصیكم اللّه فى اولادكم»

  2. The will (wasiyyah), which is the person's own right, takes precedence over the rights of the heirs. «من بعد وصیّة»

  3. The rights of the people (creditors) take precedence over the rights of the heirs and relatives. «من بعد... دین»

  4. In the difference in the share of inheritance among relatives, there are hidden wisdoms that we do not know. The share of inheritance is based on the real interests of humanity, even if man himself is heedless. «لاتدرون ایهم اقرب لكم نفعاً»

  5. Knowledge and wisdom are two necessary conditions for legislation. The rulings of inheritance are based on Divine knowledge and wisdom. «فریضة من اللّه ...حكیماً علیما»

Ayah

۞ وَلَكُمْ نِصْفُ مَا تَرَكَ أَزْوَٰجُكُمْ إِن لَّمْ يَكُن لَّهُنَّ وَلَدٌ ۚ فَإِن كَانَ لَهُنَّ وَلَدٌ فَلَكُمُ ٱلرُّبُعُ مِمَّا تَرَكْنَ ۚ مِنۢ بَعْدِ وَصِيَّةٍ يُوصِينَ بِهَآ أَوْ دَيْنٍ ۚ وَلَهُنَّ ٱلرُّبُعُ مِمَّا تَرَكْتُمْ إِن لَّمْ يَكُن لَّكُمْ وَلَدٌ ۚ فَإِن كَانَ لَكُمْ وَلَدٌ فَلَهُنَّ ٱلثُّمُنُ مِمَّا تَرَكْتُم ۚ مِّنۢ بَعْدِ وَصِيَّةٍ تُوصُونَ بِهَآ أَوْ دَيْنٍ ۗ وَإِن كَانَ رَجُلٌ يُورَثُ كَلَـٰلَةً أَوِ ٱمْرَأَةٌ وَلَهُۥٓ أَخٌ أَوْ أُخْتٌ فَلِكُلِّ وَٰحِدٍ مِّنْهُمَا ٱلسُّدُسُ ۚ فَإِن كَانُوٓا۟ أَكْثَرَ مِن ذَٰلِكَ فَهُمْ شُرَكَآءُ فِى ٱلثُّلُثِ ۚ مِنۢ بَعْدِ وَصِيَّةٍ يُوصَىٰ بِهَآ أَوْ دَيْنٍ غَيْرَ مُضَآرٍّ ۚ وَصِيَّةً مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَلِيمٌ (۱۲)
For you shall be a half of what your wives leave, if they have no children; but if they have children, then for you shall be a fourth of what they leave, after [paying off] any bequest they may have made or any debt [they may have incurred]. And for them [it shall be] a fourth of what you leave, if you have no children; but if you have children, then for them shall be an eighth of what you leave, after [paying off] any bequest you may have made or any debt [you may have incurred]. If a man or woman is inherited by siblings and has a brother or a sister, then each of them shall receive a sixth; but if they are more than that, then they shall share in one third, after [paying off] any bequest he may have made or any debt [he may have incurred] without prejudice. [This is] an enjoinment from Allah, and Allah is all-knowing, all-forbearing. (12)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the inheritance of children and parents was discussed. In this Ayah, the inheritance of the wife, husband, and maternal siblings is discussed. However, the rulings of inheritance must be implemented with the help of narrations (Ahadith), as they have many branches and details that are not mentioned in the verses of the Qur'an. The word "Kalalah" (كلاله) appears twice in the Qur'an: once in this Ayah and once in the last Ayah of this very Surah. This word linguistically means encompassing or surrounding—the relatives who surround a person and his children. Ikleel (اَكلیل) is also a crown that surrounds the head. Kull (كُلّ) is a number that encompasses other numbers. This word has two meanings in the discussion of inheritance: 1. The maternal siblings of the deceased, which is used in this Ayah. 2. The full siblings (paternal and maternal) or paternal siblings of the deceased, which is intended in the last Ayah. Although the payment of debts takes precedence over the execution of the will, because executing the will is practically more difficult, Allah mentioned the execution of the will first in the Ayah, followed by the payment of debts. (246) It is stated in a Hadith: Willing away one-third of the wealth is excessive; try to ensure that your heirs receive a greater share of the inheritance so that all are self-sufficient. Causing harm through a will is among the major sins (Kaba'ir). And acting upon a will that harms an heir is not obligatory. (247) If a man had several wives, the one-eighth or one-fourth share of the inheritance is divided equally among them. The debts and liabilities of the deceased include both Divine debts, such as Hajj, Khums, Zakat, and expiation (Kaffarah), and debts owed to people. (248)

246) Tafsir Safi. 247) Tafsir Kabir by Fakhr Razi. 248) Tafsir Kabir by Fakhr Razi.

Messages

  1. Paying people's debts is so important that it has been mentioned four times in these two Ayahs. «من بعد وصیة... اودین» (249)

  2. Having a child—even if from another spouse—reduces the share of both the wife and the husband by half, so that the children also receive a share from the deceased, whether the child is a daughter or a son. «فان كان لهنّ ولد»

  3. In making a will, the condition of the heirs must be observed so that they are not harmed. «غیر مضارّ»

  4. Islam rejects not only causing harm during life but also causing harm after death. «غیر مضارّ»

  5. The Divine distribution of inheritance is both wise, and if you transgress, Allah is Forbearing in His wrath and does not hasten (punishment). «وصیّةً من اللّه واللّه علیم حلیم»

249) It is stated in Ahadith: On the Day of Judgment, all the sins of the martyr will be forgiven, except his debt and liability. Mizan al-Hikmah, Hadith 9776.

Ayah

تِلْكَ حُدُودُ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَمَن يُطِعِ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ يُدْخِلْهُ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ (۱۳)
These are Allah’s bounds, and whoever obeys Allah and His Apostle, He shall admit him to gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them [forever]. That is the great success. (13)

Messages

1- The rulings of inheritance are among the limits set by Allah, so one must not transgress their boundary. «تلك حدوداللّه»

2- Obedience to the Messenger of Allah is equivalent to obedience to Allah. «یطع اللَّه و رسوله»

3- Felicity and salvation depend on moving along the Divine path, not on gaining a larger share of the inheritance. «ذلك الفوز»

Ayah

وَمَن يَعْصِ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ وَيَتَعَدَّ حُدُودَهُۥ يُدْخِلْهُ نَارًا خَـٰلِدًا فِيهَا وَلَهُۥ عَذَابٌ مُّهِينٌ (۱٤)
But whoever disobeys Allah and His Apostle and transgresses the bounds set by Allah, He shall make him enter a Fire, to remain in it [forever], and there will be a humiliating punishment for him. (14)

Messages

1- Those who do not act upon the will, or do not pay the debt of the deceased, or try to seize the property by denying the right or not giving the share of some heirs, will be eternally in divine punishment and wrath. «تلك حدود اللّه... تعدّ حدود اللّه»

2- The criterion for felicity and wretchedness is obedience or disobedience towards Allah and His Messenger, and nothing else. «من یطع اللّه ... و من یعص اللّه»

3- Disobedience to the Messenger of Allah is disobedience to Allah. «یعص اللَّه و رسوله»

4- Continuous disobedience is the cause of eternity (in Hellfire). «من یعص اللّه... یدخله ناراً خاداً فیها» Ya'ṣi (یعص) is a present tense verb (فعل مضارع) and signifies continuity.

5- Those who transgress the rights of others will suffer eternal punishment, ranked alongside the disbelievers (Kuffar). «خالداً فیها»

6- The wrath of Allah includes both physical punishment and spiritual humiliation. «عذاب مهین»

Ayah

وَٱلَّـٰتِى يَأْتِينَ ٱلْفَـٰحِشَةَ مِن نِّسَآئِكُمْ فَٱسْتَشْهِدُوا۟ عَلَيْهِنَّ أَرْبَعَةً مِّنكُمْ ۖ فَإِن شَهِدُوا۟ فَأَمْسِكُوهُنَّ فِى ٱلْبُيُوتِ حَتَّىٰ يَتَوَفَّىٰهُنَّ ٱلْمَوْتُ أَوْ يَجْعَلَ ٱللَّهُ لَهُنَّ سَبِيلًا (۱٥)
Should any of your women commit an indecent act, produce against them four witnesses from yourselves, and if they testify, detain them in [their] houses until death finishes them, or Allah decrees a course for them. (15)

Key Points

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The meaning of the "other way" that Allah establishes is the divine prescribed punishments (Hudud al-Ilahi), stoning (stoning to death), and lashing (whipping). [250] Some have considered the Ayah to include musahaqah (lesbianism) and female homosexuality, and they have narrated a tradition from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله in Majma' al-Bayan that confirms this. [251] In a tradition, we read that the issue of confining the adulteress woman to the house was also practiced in the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah), and Islam initially accepted it, but after Islam gained strength, the matter shifted from confinement to the execution of the prescribed punishment (Hadd). [252]

250) Tafsir Burhan, Vol. 1, p. 353. 251) Tafsir Rahnuma. 252) Wasa'il, Vol. 28, p. 61.

Messages

1- To protect the honor and dignity of others, Islam has stipulated four witnesses for proving adultery (Zina). Even if only three witnesses testify, all three are lashed. However, for proving murder, two witnesses suffice. «اربعة منكم»

2- The witness to adultery must be both male and Muslim, so that the necessary precision is achieved in proving the crime. «اربعة منكم»

3- Testifying to adultery is not obligatory. «فاِن شهدوا»

4- In judgment, we should not rely solely on our own knowledge; testimony is necessary. «فان شهدوا فامسكوهنّ»

5- The punishment for the adulteress woman is confinement in the "house," not in public prisons, where she might learn more corruption. «فامسكوهنّ فى البیوت»

6- Confining the woman to the house is so that she does not corrupt members of society, remains deprived of marriage, and finds no opportunity for pleasure. «فأمسكوهنّ»

7- For the health and purification of society, the confinement of the offender is necessary. «فأمسكوهنّ»

8- Life imprisonment was the punishment for married women who committed adultery. «حتى یتوفّاهنّ الموت» Of course, this ruling was temporary until the punishment of lashing and stoning was introduced. «أو یجعل اللّه لهنّ سبیلا»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذَانِ يَأْتِيَـٰنِهَا مِنكُمْ فَـَٔاذُوهُمَا ۖ فَإِن تَابَا وَأَصْلَحَا فَأَعْرِضُوا۟ عَنْهُمَآ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ تَوَّابًا رَّحِيمًا (۱٦)
Should two among you commit it, chastise them both; but if they repent and reform, let them alone. Indeed Allah is all-clement, all-merciful. (16)

Key Points

Various meanings have been stated for this Ayah in the exegeses, but the meaning mentioned above is taken from Tafsir Namūneh (Exemplary Exegesis). Of course, another meaning also comes to mind, which is:

Whenever two men decide to commit fāḥishah (an indecent act, sodomy), but only perform the preliminary actions, in such a way that there is certainty of its commission in the future, you should discipline, chastise (ta'zīr), and threaten them, based on the principle of Nahy 'anil-Munkar (forbidding the evil). If they become ashamed, desist, and compensate for the past, you too should forgive them, for Allah is Oft-Forgiving (Acceptor of repentance) and Merciful.

According to this meaning, the word “al-ladhān” (اللّذان) refers to two men, not one man and one woman (where the dual form would be used by way of taghlīb—predominance), and “ādhūhumā” (آذوهما) means annoyance and discipline, not the execution of the prescribed punishment (ḥadd). Repentance (tawbah) and reform (iṣlāḥ) also mean genuine desistance, not merely repenting before the judge issues a verdict. “Ya’tiyānihā” (یأتیانها) can also be taken to mean performing the imminent preliminaries of the indecent act (faḥshā). It is common in prevailing custom to attribute an action to someone who is on the verge of committing it. For instance, a person who has invited people to a party and is busy preparing the preliminaries is said to be "hosting a party" or "having guests." (Wallāhu al-ʿĀlim - And Allah knows best).

The repentance of the adulterer and the waiving of the prescribed punishment (ḥadd) concerning him is valid only until the matter has not reached the court, the establishment of witnesses, and the issuance of a verdict. Otherwise, after the verdict is issued, repentance does not prevent its execution.

Ayah

إِنَّمَا ٱلتَّوْبَةُ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ لِلَّذِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ ٱلسُّوٓءَ بِجَهَـٰلَةٍ ثُمَّ يَتُوبُونَ مِن قَرِيبٍ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ يَتُوبُ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ ۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًا (۱۷)
[Acceptance of] repentance by Allah is only for those who commit evil out of ignorance and then repent promptly. It is such whose repentance Allah will accept, and Allah is all-knowing, all-wise. (17)

Key Points

This Ayah points to a corner of the conditions for the acceptance of repentance (Tawbah). Among them are: A: The sin must be committed out of ignorance and inattention to the consequences of the sin, not out of disbelief (Kufr) or obstinacy. B: He repents quickly, before the sin encompasses him, or becomes his habit/trait, or before divine wrath and punishment arrive. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Every sin that a person commits, even if it is done knowingly, is in reality (an act of) ignorance, because he has placed himself in danger of divine wrath.[۲۵۳]

۲۵۳) تفسیر برهان، ج ۱، ص ۳۵۴.

Messages

1- Accepting genuine repentance is among the rights that Allah has placed upon Himself for the people. «التوبة على اللّه»

2- Knowledge that is not steadfast against desires and instincts is ignorance. «یعملون... بجهالة»

3- Repentance is easy until the sin becomes numerous. In this Ayah, we read: «یعملون السوء» which refers to committing a single sin, but in the next Ayah, we read: «یعملون السیّئات» which refers to numerous sins, from which repentance is difficult.

4- Allah encourages sinners toward immediate repentance. «یتوبون من قریب»

5- Speed in repentance is the key to its acceptance. «یتوبون من قریب»

6- Repentance must be genuine; if we merely pretend to repent, Allah knows. «كان اللَّه علیماً»

7- Accepting the excuse of sinners who are not obstinate is in accordance with wisdom. «علیماً حكیما»

Ayah

وَلَيْسَتِ ٱلتَّوْبَةُ لِلَّذِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ ٱلسَّيِّـَٔاتِ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا حَضَرَ أَحَدَهُمُ ٱلْمَوْتُ قَالَ إِنِّى تُبْتُ ٱلْـَٔـٰنَ وَلَا ٱلَّذِينَ يَمُوتُونَ وَهُمْ كُفَّارٌ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَعْتَدْنَا لَهُمْ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا (۱۸)
But [acceptance of] repentance is not for those who go on committing misdeeds: when death approaches any of them, he says, ‘I repent now.’ Nor is it for those who die while they are faithless. For such We have prepared a painful punishment. (18)

Messages

1- Repentance (Tawbah) is valuable when performed willingly and freely, not when under duress or facing danger. «اذا حضر احدهم الموت قال انّى تبت الآن»

2- Insistence upon sin deprives a person of the success (Tawfīq) of repentance. The word «یعملون» signifies continuity, and the word «سیّئات» means the multiplicity and repetition of sin.

3- Repentance must not be delayed, because the time of death is unknown, and repentance at the moment of witnessing death is not accepted. «اذا حضر احدهم الموت...»

4- At the time of danger and death, the innate nature (Fitrah) of man's knowledge of God becomes manifest. «انّى تُبت الآن»

5- Dying as a disbeliever and having a bad end nullifies and renders void all good deeds, including repentance. «یموتون و هم كفّار»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا يَحِلُّ لَكُمْ أَن تَرِثُوا۟ ٱلنِّسَآءَ كَرْهًا ۖ وَلَا تَعْضُلُوهُنَّ لِتَذْهَبُوا۟ بِبَعْضِ مَآ ءَاتَيْتُمُوهُنَّ إِلَّآ أَن يَأْتِينَ بِفَـٰحِشَةٍ مُّبَيِّنَةٍ ۚ وَعَاشِرُوهُنَّ بِٱلْمَعْرُوفِ ۚ فَإِن كَرِهْتُمُوهُنَّ فَعَسَىٰٓ أَن تَكْرَهُوا۟ شَيْـًٔا وَيَجْعَلَ ٱللَّهُ فِيهِ خَيْرًا كَثِيرًا (۱۹)
O you who have faith! It is not lawful for you to inherit women forcibly, and do not press them to take away part of what you have given them, unless they commit a gross indecency. Consort with them in an honourable manner; and should you dislike them, maybe you dislike something while Allah invests it with an abundant good. (19)

Key Points

Contrary to the tradition of the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah), where sometimes women themselves were inherited, this verse may indicate that only property is inheritable, and the wife of the deceased is not anyone's inheritance. She herself is free to choose another husband after the death of her spouse.

Messages

1- Islam defends the rights of women. «لایحلّ لكم ان ترثوا النساء كَرهاً»

2- Taking back the dowry (Mahr) by force is forbidden (Haram). «لاتعضلوهنّ لتذهبوا ببعض ما آتیتموهنّ»

3- A man has the right to impose restrictions only if the woman is guilty of manifest immorality. «لاتعضلوهنّ... الاّ ان یأتین بفاحشة مبیّنة»

4- Women must be treated kindly. «عاشروهنّ بالمعروف»

5- The man is the cornerstone of family life and must be patient with difficulties while maintaining good conduct. The address of the verse is to the man: «لاتعضلوهنّ... عاشروهنّ»

6- Much good (Khayr) is found amidst the unpleasantness of life. «یجعل اللَّه فیه خیراً...»

7- Resolving family problems and having forbearance and patience for the happiness of the children is better than separation and divorce. «فیه خیراً كثیر»

8- Our good and evil are not always aligned with our desires. Perhaps we dislike something, but Allah has placed abundant good in it, because man is not aware of all his own interests. «عَسى ان تكرهوا... فیه خیراً كثیرا»

Ayah

وَإِنْ أَرَدتُّمُ ٱسْتِبْدَالَ زَوْجٍ مَّكَانَ زَوْجٍ وَءَاتَيْتُمْ إِحْدَىٰهُنَّ قِنطَارًا فَلَا تَأْخُذُوا۟ مِنْهُ شَيْـًٔا ۚ أَتَأْخُذُونَهُۥ بُهْتَـٰنًا وَإِثْمًا مُّبِينًا (۲٠)
If you desire to take a wife in place of another, and you have given one of them a quintal [of gold], do not take anything away from it. Would you take it by way of calumny and flagrant sin?! (20)

Key Points

Qintar (قِنطار) means a large amount of wealth, which, like a bridge (qantara), is a means of benefit/utilization. During the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah), some men who wanted to marry another wife would slander the first wife so that she would be pressured to waive her mahr (dowry) in exchange for the husband divorcing her. Then, the husband would use that returned mahr to marry another wife. The Ayah condemns this custom of the Age of Ignorance.

Messages

1- Remarriage is permissible in Islam. «استبدال زوج مكان زوج»

2- Divorce is in the hands of the man. «اردتم استبدال زوج»

3- A large mahr (dowry) is acceptable, although a small mahr is recommended. «آتیتم احداهنّ قنطاراً»

4- Human ownership is not limited within the framework of Divine laws. «قنطاراً»

5- Islam is the protector of women's rights and prohibits a second marriage at the cost of violating the rights of the first wife. «فلا تأخذوا منه شیئاً»

6- A woman has the right of ownership, and the mahr must be delivered to her completely and without reduction. «فلاتأخذوا منه شیئا»

7- If a person rightfully acquires ownership, their property cannot be taken from them, even if it is a large amount. «لاتأخذوا...»

8- One of the worst forms of oppression is taking people's property accompanied by justification, slander, and damaging their reputation. «أتأخذونه بهتاناً واِثماً»

Ayah

وَكَيْفَ تَأْخُذُونَهُۥ وَقَدْ أَفْضَىٰ بَعْضُكُمْ إِلَىٰ بَعْضٍ وَأَخَذْنَ مِنكُم مِّيثَـٰقًا غَلِيظًا (۲۱)
How could you take it back, when you have known each other, and they have taken from you a solemn covenant? (21)

Messages

1- In forbidding evil (Nahy 'anil Munkar), human emotions should also be utilized. You who have had complete relations with your spouse for a long time and have enjoyed yourselves, why are you now unjustly taking back her dowry? «قد افضى بعضكم الى بعض»

2- During life's difficulties, one should remember the pleasures that preceded them. «قد أفضى...»

3- In expressing matters related to conjugal relations, we should observe propriety. «افضى»

4- The dowry (Mahr) is a right for women that is stipulated in exchange for cohabitation. «قدافضى »

5- The marriage contract is a firm covenant. «میثاقاً غلیظاً»

6- Taking back the dowry is a breach of covenant. «وكیف تأخذونه... واخذن منكم میثاقا غلیظا»

Ayah

وَلَا تَنكِحُوا۟ مَا نَكَحَ ءَابَآؤُكُم مِّنَ ٱلنِّسَآءِ إِلَّا مَا قَدْ سَلَفَ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ فَـٰحِشَةً وَمَقْتًا وَسَآءَ سَبِيلًا (۲۲)
Do not marry any of the women whom your fathers had married, excluding what is already past. That is indeed an indecency, an outrage and an evil course. (22)

Key Points

It was a custom of the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah) that whenever a man died, his sons would marry their stepmother (the father's wife). One of the Ansar named Abu Qays passed away. His son proposed marriage to his stepmother. The woman said, "I must seek clarification from the Prophet." When she inquired, this Ayah was revealed, prohibiting marriage with the "father's wife."

Messages

1- Islam is the religion of nature (Fitrah). Since man is naturally repulsed by marrying his mother or stepmother, it has been forbidden in Islam. «لا تنكحوا ما نكح آباؤكم»

2- "Father's wives" (stepmothers) are considered mothers. Therefore, instead of harboring resentment and incompatibility, they should act "motherly" toward the children. «لا تنكحوا ما نكح آباؤكم»

3- Children must preserve the sanctity of the father in marriage. «لا تنكحوا ما نكح آباؤكم»

4- Children should view the father's wife through the lens of a "mother." «ما نكح آباؤكم»

5- Islam overlooks past actions.[254] Marriages with stepmothers that occurred before this ruling are forgiven. «الاّ ما قد سلف»

6- For any statement, a reason and proof must be provided, either generally or in detail. «انه كان فاحشة و مقتاً وساء سبیلاً»

7- Marrying a stepmother also opens the door to marrying other forbidden relatives (Maharim). «ساء سبیلاً»

254) «الاسلام یجُبّ ما قبله» Islam covers (wipes out) what preceded it.

Ayah

حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمْ أُمَّهَـٰتُكُمْ وَبَنَاتُكُمْ وَأَخَوَٰتُكُمْ وَعَمَّـٰتُكُمْ وَخَـٰلَـٰتُكُمْ وَبَنَاتُ ٱلْأَخِ وَبَنَاتُ ٱلْأُخْتِ وَأُمَّهَـٰتُكُمُ ٱلَّـٰتِىٓ أَرْضَعْنَكُمْ وَأَخَوَٰتُكُم مِّنَ ٱلرَّضَـٰعَةِ وَأُمَّهَـٰتُ نِسَآئِكُمْ وَرَبَـٰٓئِبُكُمُ ٱلَّـٰتِى فِى حُجُورِكُم مِّن نِّسَآئِكُمُ ٱلَّـٰتِى دَخَلْتُم بِهِنَّ فَإِن لَّمْ تَكُونُوا۟ دَخَلْتُم بِهِنَّ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ وَحَلَـٰٓئِلُ أَبْنَآئِكُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ مِنْ أَصْلَـٰبِكُمْ وَأَن تَجْمَعُوا۟ بَيْنَ ٱلْأُخْتَيْنِ إِلَّا مَا قَدْ سَلَفَ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا (۲۳)
Forbidden to you are your mothers, your daughters and your sisters, your paternal aunts and your maternal aunts, your brother’s daughters and your sister’s daughters, your [foster-]mothers who have suckled you and your sisters through fosterage, your wives’ mothers, and your stepdaughters who are under your care [born] of the wives whom you have gone into—but if you have not gone into them there is no sin upon you—and the wives of your sons who are from your own loins, and that you should marry two sisters at one time—excluding what is already past; indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful— (23)

Key Points

This verse mentions groups of women who are mahram (unmarriageable kin) to a man, and whom it is forbidden to marry. Mahramiyyah (the state of being mahram) and the prohibition of marriage are established through three means: 1. Birth (consanguineous relationship) 2. Marriage (affinal relationship) 3. Breastfeeding (foster relationship). The prohibition of marrying mahram relatives existed in previous religions as well. [255] Marriage with non-mahram individuals is a factor in expanding family connections. Simultaneous marriage to two sisters usually leads them to personal and sexual jealousy and rivalry, and their affection turns into enmity. Perhaps the prohibition is for this reason. «و ان تجمعوا بین الاختین...»

255) Torah, Book of Leviticus, Chapter 18, verses 6 - 23.

Messages

  1. The authority to make all things lawful (halal) or unlawful (haram) (foods, drinks, transactions, and marriages...) belongs solely to Allah. «حرّمت علیكم»

  2. Mother's milk, just like birth from a mother, is one of the causes of mahramiyyah. We must be careful in choosing a wet nurse. «امّهاتكم اللاتى ارضعنكم و اخواتكم من الرضاعة»

  3. Look upon the daughters of your wife's previous husband who live in your house as your own daughters, and be their guardian/educator. «و ربائبكم اللاتى فى حجوركم»

  4. The law is not retroactive. «الا ما قد سلف» (Those who married two sisters before the prohibition was announced bear no penalty, although after this law is declared, they must keep one sister and release the other.)

Ayah

۞ وَٱلْمُحْصَنَـٰتُ مِنَ ٱلنِّسَآءِ إِلَّا مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَـٰنُكُمْ ۖ كِتَـٰبَ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ ۚ وَأُحِلَّ لَكُم مَّا وَرَآءَ ذَٰلِكُمْ أَن تَبْتَغُوا۟ بِأَمْوَٰلِكُم مُّحْصِنِينَ غَيْرَ مُسَـٰفِحِينَ ۚ فَمَا ٱسْتَمْتَعْتُم بِهِۦ مِنْهُنَّ فَـَٔاتُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَّ فَرِيضَةً ۚ وَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِيمَا تَرَٰضَيْتُم بِهِۦ مِنۢ بَعْدِ ٱلْفَرِيضَةِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًا (۲٤)
and married women, excepting your slave-women. This is Allah’s ordinance for you. As to others than these, it is lawful for you to seek [temporary union with them] with your wealth, in wedlock, not in license. For the enjoyment you have had from them thereby, give them their dowries, by way of settlement, and there is no sin upon you in what you may agree upon after the settlement. Indeed Allah is all-knowing, all-wise. (24)

Key Points

The word muḥṣanāt (مُحصَنات), linguistically, means that which is in a ḥiṣn (fortress) or sanctuary and is prevented from the access of others. The root of this word is used in the Quran regarding three groups: 1- Chaste and virtuous women. As Allah says concerning Lady Maryam (Mary): «التى احصنت فرجها» (256) 2- Married women. Because the husband undertakes the role of protecting the woman's chastity. The meaning intended by «المحصنات من النساء» in the current Ayah is this very meaning. 3- Free women who are not prisoners of war and are not slave-girls. This is because captivity causes the veils of a woman's chastity and modesty to be torn, and immodesty was common among slave-girls. The meaning intended by «المحصنات المؤمنات» in the next Ayah is this one.

The word musāfiḥīn (مُسفِحین) is derived from sifāḥ (سفاح), meaning fornication/adultery (zina).

Captivity for disbelieving women is equivalent to their divorce from their husbands. This is similar to a woman embracing faith; if her husband continues in disbelief, her conversion separates her from her husband.

Marriage to a married woman is forbidden in Islam, regardless of her religion or nation. However, since captivity is equivalent to divorce, the captive woman must observe the ‘iddah (waiting period) after captivity for the duration of one menstrual cycle, or until childbirth if she is pregnant. During the ‘iddah, no marital relations with her are permissible. This arrangement for marital relations, whether as a wife or a slave-girl, is better than the plan of returning them to the disbelievers or abandoning them without guardianship.

The word mut‘ah (مُتعة), like the words Hajj, Ṣalāt, Ribā (usury), and Ghanīmah (spoils), has a legal (Shar‘ī) meaning, not merely a linguistic one. Therefore, according to Allamah [Tabataba'i] in Tafsir al-Mizan, there is no doubt that the intent of the phrase «استمتعتم» is Mut‘ah (temporary marriage).

The payment of the wage, «آتوهنّ اجورهنّ» (give them their wages), in Mut‘ah is related to the enjoyment, «فما استمتعتم» (for that which you have enjoyed). Whereas, if the Ayah intended permanent marriage (Nikah Dā’im), the dowry (mahr) becomes obligatory upon the reading of the contract, whether enjoyment occurs or not. However, if divorce takes place before intercourse, half the dowry is paid.

The phrase «فمااستمتعتم به منهنّ فاتوهنّ اُجورهنّ» (for that which you have enjoyed of them, give them their wages), according to the statements of the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام (257) and most Sunni commentaries, relates to temporary marriage (Nikah Muwaqqat), which the Second Caliph forbade, even though no one has the right to prohibit a ruling of Allah. And had it not been forbidden, according to narrations (258), no one would commit fornication except the wretched ones.

Temporary marriage is a sound plan, and the world, due to its absence, has legalized fornication. Temporary marriage is a social necessity, and we read in Sunni narrations that the permissibility of Mut‘ah was due to being far from one's wife and the occurrence of wars, conditions which still exist today. Mut‘ah is a plan to solve this social problem and prevent corruption, and it retains its legal status until the Day of Judgment.

The argument of some is that the Quran discusses the issues of ‘iddah, inheritance, and guarding chastity in relation to marriage, and Mut‘ah is not marriage. But our response is: Firstly, the name marriage is also applied to Mut‘ah; and secondly, ‘iddah is also necessary in Mut‘ah, and only the law of inheritance (of the wife from the husband or vice versa) is exempted in Mut‘ah.

A few questions:

1- Is temporary marriage not a mask covering the face of immorality? Answer: No, because: A: In temporary marriage, the woman is only available to one man. B: She must observe an ‘iddah of at least forty-five days. C: The offspring of Mut‘ah is like the offspring of a permanent wife.

2- Do lustful men not misuse this law? Answer: It is possible for individuals to misuse any beneficial and necessary law. Do they not make wine from grapes? If some people misused the Hajj journey, should the Hajj journey be denied?

3- What is the status of the children of Mut‘ah? Answer: Regarding rights, inheritance, and similar matters, they have no difference from the children of a permanent wife.

(256) Tahrim, 12. (257) Kafi, Vol. 5, p. 448; Wasā’il, Vol. 21, p. 5. (258) Tafsir Kabir of Fakhr Razi, under the Ayah.

Messages

1- One must not seek out married women for marriage. «والمحصنات»

2- Women captured in war with the disbelievers should not be left without guardianship or returned to the disbelievers; rather, they should have a lawful relationship with Muslims, either as wives or as slave-girls. «الا ما ملكت ایمانكم»

3- The prohibition of marrying close relatives (maḥārim), married women, and combining two sisters in marriage are fixed, definitive, and unchangeable divine rulings. «حرمت... كتاب اللَّه علیكم»

4- Spending funds in the path of immodesty and immorality is forbidden. «تبتغوا باموالكم... غیر مسافحین»

5- Temporary marriage is lawful (Shar‘ī), and the woman's dowry must be paid. «فما استمتعتم... آتوهن أجورهن»

6- Mutual consent is a condition regarding the dowry. «فیما تراضیتم به»

7- After the term ends in temporary marriage, the term or the dowry can be extended or increased with the mutual consent of the parties. «لاجناح علیكم فیما تراضیتم به من بعد الفریضة»

8- Temporary marriage and the rulings and laws of marriage originate from the knowledge and wisdom of Allah. «علیماً حكیماً»

9- Knowledge and wisdom are two necessary conditions for a legislator. «كتاب اللّه... علیماً حكیماً»

Ayah

وَمَن لَّمْ يَسْتَطِعْ مِنكُمْ طَوْلًا أَن يَنكِحَ ٱلْمُحْصَنَـٰتِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتِ فَمِن مَّا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَـٰنُكُم مِّن فَتَيَـٰتِكُمُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتِ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِإِيمَـٰنِكُم ۚ بَعْضُكُم مِّنۢ بَعْضٍ ۚ فَٱنكِحُوهُنَّ بِإِذْنِ أَهْلِهِنَّ وَءَاتُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَّ بِٱلْمَعْرُوفِ مُحْصَنَـٰتٍ غَيْرَ مُسَـٰفِحَـٰتٍ وَلَا مُتَّخِذَٰتِ أَخْدَانٍ ۚ فَإِذَآ أُحْصِنَّ فَإِنْ أَتَيْنَ بِفَـٰحِشَةٍ فَعَلَيْهِنَّ نِصْفُ مَا عَلَى ٱلْمُحْصَنَـٰتِ مِنَ ٱلْعَذَابِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ لِمَنْ خَشِىَ ٱلْعَنَتَ مِنكُمْ ۚ وَأَن تَصْبِرُوا۟ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۲٥)
As for those of you who cannot afford to marry faithful free women, then [let them marry] from what you own, from among your faithful slave-women. Your faith is best known [only] to Allah; you are all [on a] similar [footing]. So marry them with their masters’ permission, and give them their dowries in an honourable manner—[such of them] as are chaste women, not licentious ones or those who take paramours. But should they commit an indecent act on marrying, there shall be for them [only] half the punishment for free women. This is for those of you who fear falling into fornication; but it is better that you be continent, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (25)

Key Points

As mentioned in the key points of the previous Ayah, the intent of al-muḥṣanāt al-mu’mināt (المحصنات المؤمنات) in this Ayah is free women. This is because, in contrast to it, fatiyātukum al-mu’mināt (فتیاتكم المؤمنات) is used, which means slave-girls. The Quran also uses the word fatiyāt (فتیات) elsewhere regarding slave-girls, stating: «لاتكرهوافتیاتكم على البغاء» (259) Do not compel your slave-girls to commit fornication.

The word akhdān (اَخدان), the plural of khidn (خِدن), means friend or companion, but it is usually applied to those whose relationships are secret and for illicit purposes. This word is used in contrast to musāfiḥāt (مسافحات), which means those who commit open and public fornication.

The word ṭawl (طَول) means ability and having resources, and the word ‘anat (عَنت) means hardship, suffering, and difficulties.

Those who own a slave-girl have the right to intercourse with her without needing a marriage contract. Therefore, the marriage with slave-girls mentioned in this Ayah refers to marriage with the slave-girls of others, which must be done with the permission of their owners.

Although marriage with slave-girls is discouraged, there have been many outstanding women among them who possessed virtues, and for this reason, some of Allah’s saints married them and had great children from them.

259) Noor, 33.

Messages

  1. In Islam, there is no deadlock regarding the issue of marriage. «ومن لم یستطع»
  2. The Legislator must pay attention to the natural needs of individuals in society on the one hand, and their economic pressures on the other. «و من لم یستطع....»
  3. Endure marriage with a slave-girl, but never the shame of sin. «فمن ما ملكت ایمانكم»
  4. One may forgo marriage with a free woman, but the condition of faith cannot be overlooked. «فتیاتكم المؤمنات»
  5. Faith is the fundamental condition in every marriage. «المحصنات المؤمنات, فتیاتكم المؤمنات»
  6. Apparent faith is sufficient for marriage; we are not commanded regarding the inner reality. «واللَّه اعلم بایمانكم»
  7. Marriage with a slave-girl is not a humiliation. «بعضكم من بعض»
  8. The consent of the slave-girl’s owner is a condition for marriage, just like the permission of the girl’s father. «باذن اهلهنّ»
  9. Owners should know that slave-girls are part of their own people. «اهلهنّ»
  10. Slave-girls, like free women, must be given a good dowry. «آتوهنّ اجورهنّ بالمعروف»
  11. Give the dowry to the slave-girl herself. The economic and social rights of slaves must be observed. «آتوهنّ اُجورهنّ»
  12. The amount of the dowry must be based on custom (‘urf) and the woman’s status. «بالمعروف»
  13. The condition for the continuation of marriage is chastity, avoidance of immorality, and illicit relationships. «محصنات غیر مسافحات ...»
  14. The punishment for an immoral woman, if she is a slave-girl, is reduced by half due to her status as a slave and its deprivations. «فعلیهنّ نصف ما على المحصنات»
  15. Providing easy and simple marriages in society is the way to prevent sexual corruption. «ذلك لمن خشى العنت»
  16. Psychological well-being is among the goals of Islam. «ذلك لمن خشى العنت»
  17. Patience and self-restraint are better than marrying a slave-girl. «وان تصبروا خیر لكم»

Ayah

يُرِيدُ ٱللَّهُ لِيُبَيِّنَ لَكُمْ وَيَهْدِيَكُمْ سُنَنَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ وَيَتُوبَ عَلَيْكُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ (۲٦)
Allah desires to explain [the laws] to you, and to guide you to the customs of those who were before you, and to turn toward you clemently, and Allah is all-knowing, all-wise. (26)

Messages

1- The tradition of Allah is guidance, clarification, and communication. «لیبین لكم و یهدیكم»

2- The good traditions of those who came before us must be followed and imitated. «سنن الّذین من قبلكم»

3- The rulings and regulations of Islam concerning marriage are similar to the rulings of other past heavenly religions. «سنن الّذین من قبلكم»

4- Allah returns His favor to mankind. «یتوب علیكم»

5- Cases of permissible or forbidden marriage are based on knowledge, wisdom, and expediency. «واللّه علیم حكیم»

Ayah

وَٱللَّهُ يُرِيدُ أَن يَتُوبَ عَلَيْكُمْ وَيُرِيدُ ٱلَّذِينَ يَتَّبِعُونَ ٱلشَّهَوَٰتِ أَن تَمِيلُوا۟ مَيْلًا عَظِيمًا (۲۷)
Allah desires to turn toward you clemently, but those who pursue their [base] appetites desire that you fall into gross waywardness. (27)

Key Points

Sexual freedoms, at first glance, are a type of pleasure and gratification, but considering their individual and social consequences, they are the greatest downfall and deviation. Licentiousness melts the body, preoccupies the mind, wastes wealth, and removes trust. It destroys the desire to form a family, delivers illegitimate children to society, brings forth sexually transmitted and psychological diseases, and in a word, sexual freedom leads to the greatest forms of captivity.

Messages

1- The limitations and restrictions involved in marriage are the secret of Allah's grace toward humanity and human society. «واللّه یریدان یتوب علیكم»

2- Do not step onto the path of the lustful and do not imitate them, for they are your enemies. «یرید الّذین یتّبعون الشهوات...»

3- The unrestrained pleasure-seekers are not satisfied with anything less than your drowning in desires, and they want you to be their companions on the path. «ان تمیلوا میلاً عظیماً»

4- Sexual ethics in Islam are moderate and far from extremism and negligence, but the desire-worshippers want the extremist path for you. «میلاً عظیماً»

5- Let us be careful in choosing the path. The word «یُرید» (wishes/intends) is used twice in the Ayah: one intention from the All-Knowing, Wise God and one intention from the malicious pleasure-seekers. Which intentions do we pursue? «واللّه یرید... یرید الّذین یتّبعون الشهوات»

Ayah

يُرِيدُ ٱللَّهُ أَن يُخَفِّفَ عَنكُمْ ۚ وَخُلِقَ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ ضَعِيفًا (۲۸)
Allah desires to lighten your burden, for man was created weak. (28)

Key Points

In these last three verses, a glimpse of Allah's favors regarding the matter of marriage has been successively narrated. Allah explains the rulings for you, "لِيُبَيِّنَ لَكُمْ" (that He may make clear to you); He shows the way, "يَهْدِيكُمْ" (He guides you); He returns His favor to you, "وَيَتُوبَ عَلَيْكُمْ" (and turns to you in forgiveness); and He makes things easy for you, "وَيُخَفِّفَ عَنْكُمْ" (and lightens [the burden] for you). And all this is because man is weak before the storm of instincts, and his power of patience and self-restraint is little.

Messages

1- Islam is a religion of ease and has no dead ends (or impasses). "يُرِيدُ اللَّهُ أَن يُخَفِّفَ عَنكُمْ"

2- Religious duties are based on human capacity and ability. "يُرِيدُ اللَّهُ أَن يُخَفِّفَ عَنكُمْ"

3- Creating difficulties in marriage is contrary to the Will of Allah. "يُرِيدُ اللَّهُ أَن يُخَفِّفَ"

4- Your adherence and commitment to the rulings of marriage are the basis for lightening the burden and keeping society away from afflictions and hardships. "يُرِيدُ اللَّهُ أَن يُخَفِّفَ عَنكُمْ"

5- Man is weak before the sexual instinct. "خُلِقَ الْإِنسَانُ ضَعِيفًا"

6- There is harmony between the creation (takwīn) and nature of man, and the divine legislation (tashrī') and rulings. "يُخَفِّفُ... ضَعِيفًا"

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَأْكُلُوٓا۟ أَمْوَٰلَكُم بَيْنَكُم بِٱلْبَـٰطِلِ إِلَّآ أَن تَكُونَ تِجَـٰرَةً عَن تَرَاضٍ مِّنكُمْ ۚ وَلَا تَقْتُلُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ بِكُمْ رَحِيمًا (۲۹)
O you who have faith! Do not eat up your wealth among yourselves unrightfully, but it should be trade by mutual consent. And do not kill yourselves. Indeed Allah is most merciful to you. (29)

Key Points

The secret behind the prohibition of killing, "لاتقتلوا" (do not kill), following the prohibition of consuming unlawful wealth, "لاتأكلوا" (do not consume), might be that an unhealthy economic system sets the stage for the uprising of the deprived against the hoarders of wealth, leading to conflicts, the emergence of killing, and the destruction of society.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, while reciting this Ayah, said: Whoever borrows money knowing that he cannot repay it has consumed unlawful wealth (māl bātil). [260]

Imam Baqir عليه السلام said regarding this Ayah: Usury (ribā), gambling (qimār), shortchanging (defrauding in measure), and oppression (zulm) are examples of falsehood (bātil). [261]

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, concerning performing ablution (wudū') and ritual bath (ghusl) with cold water when there is a danger to life, said: "و لاتقتلوا انفسكم انّ اللَّه كان بكم رحیماً" (And do not kill yourselves. Indeed, Allah is ever Merciful to you). [262]

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: If a Muslim enters the territory of the disbelievers alone and is killed, he has committed suicide. [263]

Killing the infallible leaders is one of the instances of suicide and bringing destruction upon society. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding "لا تقتلوا انفسكم" (Do not kill yourselves): It means, do not kill the People of the House (Ahl al-Bayt) of your Prophet. [264]

260) كافى، ج 5، ص 95. 261) تفسیر نورالثقلین. 262) تفسیر نورالثقلین. 263) تفسیر برهان، ج 1، ص 363. 264) تفسیر فرات كوفى، ص 101.

Messages

1- Individual ownership is respected, and disposing of the wealth of others is forbidden except through valid transactions based on consent. «لاتأكلوا... الا... تجارة عن تراض»

2- Society possesses a single spirit and a shared destiny. Respect the wealth of others just as you respect your own wealth. «اموالكم بینكم»

3- Any kind of disposal (of wealth) that is not based on 'right' (Haqq) is forbidden. «لاتأكلوا... بالباطل»

4- Transactions must be based on the mutual consent of both parties, not through coercion or compulsion. «عن تراض منكم»

5- Human life is sacred; therefore, suicide or killing others is forbidden. «لاتقتلوا انفسكم»

6- The rulings and regulations of Islam are a reflection of Allah's Mercy. «كان بكم رحیماً»

Ayah

وَمَن يَفْعَلْ ذَٰلِكَ عُدْوَٰنًا وَظُلْمًا فَسَوْفَ نُصْلِيهِ نَارًا ۚ وَكَانَ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ يَسِيرًا (۳٠)
And whoever does that in aggression and injustice, We will soon make him enter the Fire, and that is easy for Allah. (30)

Key Points

Perhaps the difference between 'udwān (aggression/transgression) and ẓulm (injustice/wrongdoing) is that 'udwān is transgression against others, while ẓulm also includes oppression against oneself. The word ṣalaw (صَلَو) means entering into mercy, and the word ṣalā (صَلَى) means entering into Hellfire. Therefore, the word nuṣlīhi (نُصلیه) means that We will make him enter the Fire. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Whoever commits suicide intentionally will remain in Hellfire forever. He then recited this Ayah. [265]

265) Man Lā Yaḥḍuruhu al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, p. 364.

Messages

1- Actions that do not stem from injustice (ẓulm) and aggression ('udwān) do not incur Divine wrath. «و من یفعل ذلك عدواناً و ظلماً...»

2- Intention and motive play the primary role in punishment or reward. «مَن یفعل ذلك عدواناً وظلماً»

Ayah

إِن تَجْتَنِبُوا۟ كَبَآئِرَ مَا تُنْهَوْنَ عَنْهُ نُكَفِّرْ عَنكُمْ سَيِّـَٔاتِكُمْ وَنُدْخِلْكُم مُّدْخَلًا كَرِيمًا (۳۱)
If you avoid the major sins that you are forbidden, We will absolve you of your misdeeds and admit you to a noble abode. (31)

Key Points

It is inferred from this Ayah that sins are of two types: minor (ṣaghīrah) and major (kabīrah). We also read in Ayah 49 of Sūrat Al-Kahf that the criminals, upon seeing the record of their deeds on the Day of Judgment, will say: "What is this record that leaves out no small or great sin?" «لا یغادر صغیرةً و لا كبیرةً» Imam Al-Kāẓim عليه السلام was asked: In this Ayah, Allah has promised forgiveness for minor sins (ṣaghā’ir) provided one avoids major sins (kabā’ir); so, where does intercession (shafā‘ah) fit in? The Imam replied: Intercession is for those among the Ummah who committed major sins. [266] According to narrations, a major sin (kabīrah) is one for which Allah has promised the Fire to its perpetrator. [267] Perhaps the promise of the Fire in the previous Ayah regarding murder, suicide, and transgression against people's property refers to this—that examples of major sins for which punishment has been promised are transgression against people's lives and property. The number of major sins varies in different narrations, and this is because major sins also have degrees, and some sins are referred to as "the greatest of major sins" (akbar al-kabā’ir).

266) Tawḥīd al-Ṣadūq, p. 407. 267) Al-Kāfī, Vol. 2, p. 276.

Messages

  1. Those whose fundamental intellectual and practical principles are sound should be forgiven for their minor transgressions. «ان تجتنبوا كبائر... نكفّر عنكم سیئاتكم»

  2. The reward for those who abandon major sins is that Allah will overlook their minor sins. «ان تجتنبوا كبائر... نكفّر عنكم سیئاتكم»

  3. We will not enter Paradise until we are purified of both major and minor sins. «نكفّر عنكم سیئاتكم و ندخلكم مدخلاً كریما»

Ayah

وَلَا تَتَمَنَّوْا۟ مَا فَضَّلَ ٱللَّهُ بِهِۦ بَعْضَكُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ ۚ لِّلرِّجَالِ نَصِيبٌ مِّمَّا ٱكْتَسَبُوا۟ ۖ وَلِلنِّسَآءِ نَصِيبٌ مِّمَّا ٱكْتَسَبْنَ ۚ وَسْـَٔلُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مِن فَضْلِهِۦٓ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمًا (۳۲)
Do not covet the advantage, which Allah has given some of you over others. To men belongs a share of what they have earned, and to women a share of what they have earned. And ask Allah for His bounty. Indeed Allah has knowledge of all things. (32)

Key Points

The previous verses forbade consuming unlawful wealth (haram khwari). This verse points to the root of consuming unlawful wealth, which is greed, excessive desire (or longing), and rivalry. Justice is different from equality. Justice is valuable everywhere, but equality is sometimes valuable and sometimes oppression. For example, if a doctor gives all patients the same type of medicine, or a teacher gives all students the same grade, that is equality, but it is not justice. Justice is giving everyone according to their merit, even if it results in differences, because differentiating based on competence and perfection is right, whereas discrimination (tab'idh) is wrong. Discrimination is preferring one over another without reason and based on personal whim, but differentiation is preferring one over another based on established criteria. Therefore, one should desire justice, but longing for everything and everyone to be uniform is inappropriate. The organs of a body and the parts of a machine differ in terms of material, shape, and efficiency, but their differences are based on wisdom. If Allah has not created everyone uniformly, it is according to His wisdom. Therefore, regarding matters that are not in our hands—such as gender, beauty, talent, eloquence, intelligence, lifespan, etc.—we must be content with the divine distribution and decree. Since we know Allah is Just and Wise, even if we do not understand the reason or wisdom behind something, we should not make hasty and ignorant judgments accompanied by suspicion or inappropriate expectations of the Wise God, nor should we envy those who possess these blessings. This is because with the increase of every blessing, responsibility increases, and the arrival of every blessing is accompanied by the sorrow of losing it. We read this truth in Nahj al-Balagha. In voluntary matters, through effort and striving, and by avoiding laziness and idleness, one must achieve strength, abundance, and independence through healthy competition, and not allow others to exploit or colonize them. In matters that are not in our hands, we must view the differences as a reason for the growth, testing, dynamism, cooperation, and cohesion of society, because if all people had equal resources, human qualities such as generosity, courage, and self-sacrifice would not develop in man. Man reveals himself amidst ups and downs, moves forward, and connects with others due to his needs. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, in response to the verse, said: No one should covet the wife or children of others, but rather ask Allah for the like of it [for themselves].[268] When this verse was revealed, the companions of the Holy Prophet asked: What is meant by "His bounty" (fadl) in "واسئلوا اللّه من فضله" (and ask Allah of His bounty)? It was decided that Ali عليه السلام should ask the Prophet about this matter. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله replied: Allah has distributed provisions (rizq) among people through lawful means, and He has also presented the unlawful. Whoever acquires the unlawful has reduced his lawful provision by that amount and will be held accountable for the unlawful.[269]

268) وسائل، ج ۱۲، ص ۲۴۱. 269) تفسیر عیّاشى، ج ۱، ص ۲۱۵.

Messages

1- We should be content with the divine gifts and not enviously long for the superiority of others. "ولا تتمنوا" (And do not wish for).

2- In Islam, economic and moral issues are intertwined. "لاتأكلوا...بالباطل... ولاتتمنّوا" (Do not consume... by falsehood... and do not wish for).

3- Exploitative and oppressive discriminations and superiorities are separate from the divinely bestowed superiorities. "فضل اللّه به بعضكم على بعض" (Allah has favored some of you over others).

4- The differences between men and women in terms of creation and nature are examples of Allah granting superiority in creation. "فضل اللّه... للرجال... للنساء" (Allah has favored... for men... for women).

5- Both men and women equally own their respective earnings. "للرّجال نصیب، للنساء نصیب" (For men is a share, and for women is a share).

6- Instead of longing for what others possess, we should pursue acquisition and effort. "نصیب ممّااكتسبوا..." (a share of what they have earned...).

7- Man only owns a portion of his earnings. "ممااكتسبوا" (of what they have earned). The other portion is the share of the poor and the Islamic government.

8- Both work and prayer. "اكتسبوا... واسئلوا اللّه" (earn... and ask Allah).

9- Instead of coveting the possessions of others, look toward the bounty and grace of Allah. "ولا تتمنّوا... واسئلوا اللّه" (And do not wish for... and ask Allah).

10- We should give the correct direction to our characteristics. Desire exists in man, but the direction of this desire should be asking God for blessings, not wishing for the removal of blessings from others. "لاتتمنّوا... و اسئلوا اللَّه من فضله" (Do not wish for... and ask Allah of His bounty).

11- If Allah gives something to someone or takes it away, it is done wisely and knowingly. "بكل شى ء علیماً" (He is Knower of all things). (Faith in Allah's knowledge prevents man from greed and covetousness.)

Ayah

وَلِكُلٍّ جَعَلْنَا مَوَٰلِىَ مِمَّا تَرَكَ ٱلْوَٰلِدَانِ وَٱلْأَقْرَبُونَ ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ عَقَدَتْ أَيْمَـٰنُكُمْ فَـَٔاتُوهُمْ نَصِيبَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ شَهِيدًا (۳۳)
For everyone We have appointed heirs to what the parents and near relatives leave, as well as those with whom you have made a compact; so give them their share [of the heritage]. Indeed Allah is witness to all things. (33)

Key Points

"Mawālī" (مَوالى), the plural of "Mawlā" (مولى), has various meanings, but here the intent is heirs. The phrase "عقدت ایمانكم" (ʿaqadat aymānukum) refers to contracts that were established between two people before Islam, which Islam accepted with slight modifications. Its name in jurisprudential books is "Ḍamān Jarīrah" (ضمان جریره - guarantee of offense/liability). The text of the contract was that two people would pledge to help each other in life, assist each other in paying indemnities (fines/compensation), and inherit from each other. (Something similar to an insurance contract today), where if damage occurred to one, his covenant partner would pay the blood money (Diyah) for it. Islam accepted this contract, but conditioned inheriting from the other party on the person having no (other) heir. Imam Riḍā عليه السلام said regarding the meaning of "و الذین عقدت ایمانكم": The intention is the covenant that people have made with the divine and infallible leaders. [270]

270) Wasā’il, Vol. 12, p. 241.

Messages

1- Determining the amount of inheritance is by the command of Allah. «لكل جعلنا موالى»

2- Closer relatives have priority in inheriting. «الاقربون»

3- Man has the right, under certain conditions, to transfer his ownership to another through a covenant and contract. «عقدت ایمانكم»

4- A person's commitments during his lifetime are respected after death. «والّذین عقدت ایمانكم فآتوهم نصیبهم»

5- Fulfilling covenants and promises is obligatory. «آتوهم نصیبهم»

6- Debtors must seek out creditors to pay the debt. «آتوهم نصیبهم»

7- Belief in the presence of Allah is the secret to piety and a warning to those who are not faithful to their covenants. «ان اللّه كان على كل شى ء شهیدا»

Ayah

ٱلرِّجَالُ قَوَّٰمُونَ عَلَى ٱلنِّسَآءِ بِمَا فَضَّلَ ٱللَّهُ بَعْضَهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ وَبِمَآ أَنفَقُوا۟ مِنْ أَمْوَٰلِهِمْ ۚ فَٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتُ قَـٰنِتَـٰتٌ حَـٰفِظَـٰتٌ لِّلْغَيْبِ بِمَا حَفِظَ ٱللَّهُ ۚ وَٱلَّـٰتِى تَخَافُونَ نُشُوزَهُنَّ فَعِظُوهُنَّ وَٱهْجُرُوهُنَّ فِى ٱلْمَضَاجِعِ وَٱضْرِبُوهُنَّ ۖ فَإِنْ أَطَعْنَكُمْ فَلَا تَبْغُوا۟ عَلَيْهِنَّ سَبِيلًا ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ عَلِيًّا كَبِيرًا (۳٤)
Men are the managers of women, because of the advantage Allah has granted some of them over others, and by virtue of their spending out of their wealth. Righteous women are obedient and watchful in the absence [of their husbands] in guarding what Allah has enjoined [them] to guard. As for those [wives] whose misconduct you fear, [first] advise them, and [if ineffective] keep away from them in the bed, and [as the last resort] beat them. Then if they obey you, do not seek any course [of action] against them. Indeed Allah is all-exalted, all-great. (34)

Key Points

Qawwām refers to someone who is responsible for the management and reform (or improvement) of another. Nushūz (rebellion/disobedience) comes from nashz, meaning high ground, and is also used to mean rebellion and arrogance (or high-flying ambition). The condition for guardianship and management is the competence to provide for and administer life. For this reason, men are given precedence over women not only in family matters but also in social affairs, judiciary, and warfare, (bimā faḍḍala Allāhu... wa bimā anfaqū). It did not say, (qawwāmūna ‘alā azwājihim), because the issue of zawjiyyah (spousal relationship) is specific to marriage, and God has not restricted this superiority to the home. Although some women may be superior in physical ability or financial income, in law and planning, the general rule must be observed, not rare exceptions.

There are various meanings for the phrase (ḥāfiẓātun lil-ghaybi bimā ḥafiẓa Allāhu): A: Women preserve what God desires to be preserved. B: Women preserve the rights of their husbands, just as God has preserved the rights of women under the duties He has placed upon men. C: Women are preservers, but they gain this success through Divine preservation.

When peaceful methods are not beneficial, one must either abandon the performance of duty, or, if admonition and temporary separation (estrangement) are ineffective, resort to firmness (or mild physical discipline). According to psychologists, some individuals develop a state of masochism (seeking pain), for whom a slight physical discipline acts like a balm. Even in physical discipline, Islam has advised that it should not reach the stage of bruising the body or causing injury. Furthermore, a transgressing husband is sometimes physically disciplined by the judge. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: Your striking should not be in such a way that its mark is visible on the woman's body, nor should the striking be a sign of the man showing off and displaying power to intimidate her. (ḍarban ghayra mubarriḥ - striking that is not severe/injurious). (271) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The nushūz (disobedience) of a woman is when she does not comply with her husband regarding the bed (cohabitation), and the intent of (waḍribūhunna) is striking with a toothbrush stick (miswāk) or something similar, a striking that is done with gentleness and kindness. (272)

271) Tafsir Tabari, Vol. 5, p. 68 and Durr al-Manthur, Vol. 2, p. 522. 272) Man Lā Yaḥḍuruhu al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, p. 521.

Messages

1- Even in a society of two people, one must be responsible and the guardian. The manager of the home is the man. (Ar-rijālu qawwāmūna ‘alā an-nisā’)

2- In managing life affairs, intellect and planning must govern over emotions and sentiments. (Ar-rijālu qawwāmūna ‘alā an-nisā’)
