3- The legal system of Islam is consistent with the system of creation and nature. The man's right to management is due to his physical and psychological structure. (Ar-rijālu qawwāmūna ‘alā an-nisā’)

4- Natural superiorities are Divine grace (or favor). (Bimā faḍḍala Allāhu)

5- The cost of living is the responsibility of the man. (Bimā anfaqū)

6- The legislator must pay attention to the natural differences between men and women in creation when enacting laws. (Ar-rijālu qawwāmūna... bimā faḍḍala Allāhu)

7- A sign of a woman's worthiness is her obedience and humility towards her husband. (Qānitāt)

8- A sign of a worthy wife is her self-preservation in the absence of her husband. (Ḥāfiẓātun lil-ghayb)

9- The wife's obedience to her husband and her preservation of his rights is conditional upon the man providing for her livelihood. (Bimā anfaqū... faṣ-ṣāliḥātu qānitātun ḥāfiẓāt)

10- The remedy for an event must be sought before it occurs. Find a solution before the wife becomes rebellious (nāshizah). (Takhāfūna nushūzahunna)

11- A man has no right whatsoever to harm his obedient wife. (Fa in aṭa‘nakum falā tabghū ‘alayhinna sabīlā)

12- The three stages—admonition, temporary separation (in bed), and discipline—are for making the wife obedient. Therefore, if the wife becomes obedient through another means, the husband has no right to confront her. The phrase (fa in aṭa‘nakum) indicates that the goal is obedience, even if achieved through another path.

13- A woman's humility is not weakness; it is value. (Faṣ-ṣāliḥātu qānitāt)

14- A sign of a worthy wife is keeping secrets, preserving the husband's wealth, honor, and chastity in his absence. (Faṣ-ṣāliḥātu ... ḥāfiẓātun lil-ghayb)

15- Dealing with forbidden acts must be step-by-step and stage-by-stage: admonition, estrangement, discipline. (Fa‘iẓūhunna wa-hjūrūhunna...)

16- As long as admonition is effective, estrangement and harshness are forbidden; and as long as temporary estrangement and separation are effective, physical discipline is forbidden. (Fa‘iẓūhunna wa-hjūrūhunna... waḍribūhunna)

17- Harsh confrontation and discipline are only for the performance of duty, not for revenge, malice, or pretext. (Fa in aṭa‘nakum falā tabghū ‘alayhinna)

18- The superiority of men should not lead to arrogance, because God is superior to all. (Inna Allāha kāna ‘alīyan kabīrā)

19- Attention to the superiority of God is the key to piety and avoiding oppression against wives. (Inna Allāha kāna ‘alīyan)

Ayah

وَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ شِقَاقَ بَيْنِهِمَا فَٱبْعَثُوا۟ حَكَمًا مِّنْ أَهْلِهِۦ وَحَكَمًا مِّنْ أَهْلِهَآ إِن يُرِيدَآ إِصْلَـٰحًا يُوَفِّقِ ٱللَّهُ بَيْنَهُمَآ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ عَلِيمًا خَبِيرًا (۳٥)
If you fear a split between the two of them, then appoint an arbiter from his relatives and an arbiter from her relatives. If they desire reconcilement, Allah shall reconcile them. Indeed Allah is all-knowing, all-aware. (35)

Key Points

This Ayah proposes a family court system to resolve disputes between husband and wife and prevent divorce, offering the following advantages: 1- The arbitrators should be from both families so that they possess greater empathy, commitment, and goodwill. 2- This court does not require a budget. 3- Resolving disputes in this court is swift, without case backlogs or administrative complications. 4- The secrets of the court do not reach outsiders, and the disputed matters remain among themselves. 5- Since the arbitrators are from the family itself, they are trusted by both parties.

It is permissible to select an arbitrator to solve all problems. Imam Baqir عليه السلام recited this Ayah in response to an objection raised against the acceptance of arbitration by Hadrat Ali عليه السلام at Siffin. (273)

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was asked about "فابعثوا حَكماً..." (then send an arbitrator...), and He replied: The two arbitrators cannot rule for divorce and separation unless they have permission from both parties. (274)

273) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn and Ihtijaj Tabarsi, Vol. 2, p. 58. 274) Kafi, Vol. 6, p. 146.

Messages

1- The remedy must be applied before the event occurs. Concern over discord (shiqaq) and separation is sufficient grounds to proceed with selecting arbitrators. «ان خفتم» (If you fear)

2- We should not interfere in people's lives until there is concern over discord. «وان خفتم» (And if you fear)

3- Divorce and separation are worthy of fear and concern. «خفتم شقاق» (You fear discord)

4- Husband and wife are one soul in two bodies. The word shiqaq (discord) is used where a single reality is divided into two parts.

5- Action to reconcile the husband and wife must be swift. The letter fa (ف) in the phrase "فابعثوا" (then send) indicates urgency.

6- Society has a responsibility regarding family disputes. «وان خفتم... فابعثوا» (And if you fear... then send)

7- Relatives have a greater responsibility in resolving family disputes. «من اهله» (from his family)

8- Men and women have equal rights in selecting an arbitrator. «حَكماً من اهله و حَكماً من اهلها» (an arbitrator from his family and an arbitrator from her family)

9- We should trust certain individuals in society and accept their arbitration. «فابعثوا حكماً...» (then send an arbitrator...)

10- We should solve people's problems with the help of the people themselves. «فابعثوا حكماً من اهله» (then send an arbitrator from his family)

11- We should not be hopeless, despairing, or indifferent regarding reconciliation. «فابعثوا» (then send)

12- We should not create more commotion than the concern warrants; one arbitrator for each party is sufficient. «حَكماً من اهله و حَكماً من اهلها» (an arbitrator from his family and an arbitrator from her family)

13- Islam pays attention to matters of consultation (Shura) and community-based mediation. «حكماً من اهله و... اهلها» (an arbitrator from his family and... her family)

14- Do not drag all issues to the judge and the court; resolve them internally within the group. «فابعثوا حكماً من اهله» (then send an arbitrator from his family)

15- Wherever rights and entitlements are discussed, both parties must be present. «حكماً من اهله و حَكماً من اهلها» (an arbitrator from his family and an arbitrator from her family)

16- The husband and wife must accept the judgment of the selected arbitrators. (The necessity of selecting an arbitrator is obedience to his ruling.)

17- In selecting an arbitrator, attention must be paid to their knowledge, discretion (secrecy), and desire for reconciliation. «ان یریدا اصلاحاً» (if they desire reconciliation)

18- Wherever there is good intention and a foundation for reconciliation, Divine success will flow forth. «ان یریدا اصلاحاً یوفّق اللّه بینهما» (if they desire reconciliation, Allah will reconcile them)

19- Hearts are in the hand of Allah. «یوفّق اللَّه بینهما» (Allah will reconcile them)

20- Do not be arrogant about your own intellect and planning; attribute success to Allah. «یوفّق اللّه» (Allah will reconcile)

21- The Quranic plan for family reconciliation stems from Divine knowledge and wisdom. «علیماً خبیراً» (All-Knowing, All-Aware)

22- One must have good intentions, because Allah is aware of everyone's motives. «انّ اللّه كان علیماً خبیراً» (Indeed, Allah is ever All-Knowing, All-Aware).

Ayah

۞ وَٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَلَا تُشْرِكُوا۟ بِهِۦ شَيْـًٔا ۖ وَبِٱلْوَٰلِدَيْنِ إِحْسَـٰنًا وَبِذِى ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ وَٱلْيَتَـٰمَىٰ وَٱلْمَسَـٰكِينِ وَٱلْجَارِ ذِى ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ وَٱلْجَارِ ٱلْجُنُبِ وَٱلصَّاحِبِ بِٱلْجَنۢبِ وَٱبْنِ ٱلسَّبِيلِ وَمَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَـٰنُكُمْ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ مَن كَانَ مُخْتَالًا فَخُورًا (۳٦)
Worship Allah and do not ascribe any partners to Him, and be good to parents, the relatives, the orphans, the needy, the near neighbour and the distant neighbour, the companion at your side, the traveller, and your slaves. Indeed Allah does not like those who are arrogant and boastful. (36)

Key Points

This Ayah explains rights ranging from the right of Allah ("واعبدوااللَّه") to the right of slaves ("ملكت ایمانكم"), and this is a sign of the comprehensiveness of Islam. "الجار ذى القربى والجار الجنب" includes neighbors who are relatives and non-relatives, co-religionists and non-co-religionists. According to narrations, neighbors extend up to forty houses in every direction. [275] This means that in small areas, everyone is a neighbor. "الصاحب بالجنب" includes permanent or temporary companions, travel companions, or those who approach a person hoping for some benefit. "ابن السبیل" (the wayfarer) might be wealthy in his own homeland, and we may have no knowledge of him or his relatives; we only know that he is stranded during travel and is a child of the road, who must be supported. "مختال" is someone who considers himself great in the world of imagination and acts arrogantly. If a horse is also called "خیل" (khayl), it is because it walks haughtily. Kindness (Ihsan) to parents includes affection, service, financial, intellectual, and emotional help, and consulting with them. The condition for being kind to parents is not that they must be good, but obedience to parents is only required if they do not command something contrary to the pleasure of Allah. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and Ali عليه السلام are the fathers of this Ummah, because it is stated in the Quran: "اعبدوا اللّه... و بالوالدین احساناً" [276]

275) وسائل، ج 12، ص 131. 276) تفسیر عیّاشى، ج 1، ص 241.

Messages

1- Worshipping Allah alone is not enough; avoiding all forms of polytheism (shirk) and hypocrisy (riya) is necessary. "واعبدوا اللَّه ولاتشركوا..."

2- Kindness to parents is mentioned alongside the servitude of Allah and Monotheism (Tawhid). "واعبدوااللَّه... و بالوالدین احسانا"

3- A true worshipper of Allah must be committed and responsible towards his relatives, the deprived, and his neighbors. "اعبدوااللَّه... ذى القربى و الیتامى"

4- Distant neighbors also have a right over a person. "الجار الجنب"

5- Pay attention to priorities when showing kindness. The name of the parents comes before relatives, and their name comes before the orphans.

6- Disregard for parents, relatives, orphans, and the deprived members of society is a sign of arrogance and boastfulness. "ان اللّه لایحبّ من كان مختالاً فخوراً"

7- Kindness (Ihsan) must be accompanied by humility. At the beginning of the Ayah, kindness is commanded, and at the end, arrogance is forbidden. "لایحبّ كلّ مختال"

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يَبْخَلُونَ وَيَأْمُرُونَ ٱلنَّاسَ بِٱلْبُخْلِ وَيَكْتُمُونَ مَآ ءَاتَىٰهُمُ ٱللَّهُ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ ۗ وَأَعْتَدْنَا لِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ عَذَابًا مُّهِينًا (۳۷)
—Those who are [themselves] stingy and bid [other] people to be stingy, and conceal whatever Allah has given them out of His bounty; and We have prepared for the faithless a humiliating punishment. (37)

Key Points

Stinginess, in addition to being stingy with wealth and not giving to others, also includes stinginess regarding knowledge, honor, power, and resources.

Messages

1- Human spiritual diseases are prone to spreading. Stingy and sick individuals like others to be stingy too. «یبخلون و یأمرون الناس بالبخل»

2- The stingy person is deprived of Allah's love. «لا یحبّ كلّ مختال...الّذین یبخلون»

3- Stinginess is among the signs of arrogance and boasting. «مختال فخور الّذین یبخلون»

4- Manifesting Allah's blessings is a type of gratitude, while concealing them is a type of ingratitude and denial of favor. «یكتمون ما اتاهم اللَّه»

5- The stingy person feigns poverty in order to abandon benevolence. «یبخلون و یكتمون»

6- Blessings are Allah's favor, not merely the result of our effort and planning. «آتاهم اللّه من فضله»

7- If we know that blessings are from Him, we will not be stingy. «اتاهم اللَّه من فضله»

8- Stinginess can sometimes be a precursor to disbelief (Kufr).[277] «یبخلون ... كافرین»

9- The punishment for arrogance is humiliation in the Hereafter. «مختال - مهین»

277) It is stated in another Ayah: «و ویل للمشركین الّذین لایؤتون الزكاة». Fussilat, 7.

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ يُنفِقُونَ أَمْوَٰلَهُمْ رِئَآءَ ٱلنَّاسِ وَلَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱللَّهِ وَلَا بِٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ ۗ وَمَن يَكُنِ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ لَهُۥ قَرِينًا فَسَآءَ قَرِينًا (۳۸)
And those who spend their wealth to be seen by people, and believe neither in Allah nor in the Last Day. As for him who has Satan for his companion—an evil companion is he! (38)

Key Points

Satan sometimes inspires or whispers things to people from afar, and sometimes he becomes their close companion. Believers flee from the whispers and inspirations of Satan, but as we read in Ayah 36 of Surah Az-Zukhruf: «من یعش عن ذكر الرّحمان نقیّض له شیطاناً فهو له قرین» (Whoever turns away from the remembrance of the Most Gracious), We appoint for him a devil, who will be his constant companion.

Messages

1- Both stinginess and abandoning charity (infaq) are bad, as is charitable giving done out of ostentation (riya'). In the previous Ayah, stinginess was condemned, and here, ostentatious giving is condemned. «یبخلون، ینفقون... رئاء الناس»

2- The cause of ostentation (riya') is the lack of true faith in Allah and the Day of Judgment. «رئاء الناس و لایؤمنون باللّه و لا بالیوم الآخر»

3- The goal of charity (infaq) is not merely to feed the hungry, because this goal can be achieved even through ostentation. Rather, the goal is also the spiritual growth of the giver. «ینفقون اموالهم رئاء الناس»

4- Allah does not love the ostentatious. «لایحبّ كلّ مختال... والّذین ینفقون رئاء الناس»

5- When the heart is emptied of Allah, it becomes the permanent abode of Satan. «لایؤمنون باللَّه... یكن الشیطان له قریناً»

Ayah

وَمَاذَا عَلَيْهِمْ لَوْ ءَامَنُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ وَأَنفَقُوا۟ مِمَّا رَزَقَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ بِهِمْ عَلِيمًا (۳۹)
What harm would it have done them had they believed in Allah and the Last Day, and spent out of what Allah has provided them? Allah knows them well. (39)

Messages

1- Expressing sorrow over the condition of those who have deviated is one of the methods of warning and propagation. «و ماذا علیهم...»

2- The prerequisite for faith in Allah is spending (charity) upon the deprived. «آمنوا... انفقوا»

3- Spending (charity) is not limited only to wealth, but is commendable from everything that Allah has provided (knowledge, reputation, status...). «ممّا رزقهم اللّه»

4- If we know that blessings are from Allah, the spirit of spending (charity) becomes strong within us. «رزقهم اللَّه»

5- Let the stingy or hypocritical individuals know that they are under the observation of Allah. «كان اللّه بهم علیما»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَظْلِمُ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ ۖ وَإِن تَكُ حَسَنَةً يُضَـٰعِفْهَا وَيُؤْتِ مِن لَّدُنْهُ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا (٤٠)
Indeed Allah does not wrong [anyone] [even to the extent of] an atom’s weight, and if it be a good deed He doubles it[s reward], and gives from Himself a great reward. (40)

Key Points

Usually, the root of oppression is either ignorance, or fear, or need, or greed, and so on. But Allah is Self-Sufficient, All-Knowing, and Exalted; thus, He does not commit oppression. Furthermore, Allah Himself has commanded justice and goodness; how could He commit oppression? Of course, the reward from Allah is multiplied (tenfold up to seven hundredfold), and this is His goodness and grace.

Messages

1- Allah is Just, and His punishments are the reaction to our own deeds. «ان اللّه لایظلم...»

2- Divine penalties correspond to the crime, but Divine rewards are multiplied. «یضاعف»

3- In addition to increasing good deeds, Allah also grants a great reward from His own presence. «من لدنه اجراً عظیماً»

Ayah

فَكَيْفَ إِذَا جِئْنَا مِن كُلِّ أُمَّةٍۭ بِشَهِيدٍ وَجِئْنَا بِكَ عَلَىٰ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ شَهِيدًا (٤۱)
So how shall it be, when We bring a witness from every nation and We bring you as a witness to them? (41)

Key Points

The content of this Ayah, which describes the witnessing of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله over the deeds of the nation (Ummah), has also appeared in other instances. [278] When Ibn Mas'ud recited this Ayah to the Prophet during his recitation, the tears of that حضرت (His Holiness) would flow. [279] Although Allah has no need to bring witnesses, man is such that the more he senses the presence of witnesses, the more effective it is for his training and piety (Taqwa). A person who speaks on television to millions of people, because everyone sees him, feels more embarrassed when he sneezes or makes a mistake. According to the verses of the Quran, the witnesses on the Day of Judgment are numerous, including Allah, [280] the Prophets, the infallible Imams, [281] [282] the angels, [283] the earth, [284] time, [285] the body parts, [286] and the Day of Judgment is the day the witnesses are established. «یوم یقوم الاشهاد» [287] The Divine tradition (Sunnah) is that the Prophet of every nation (Ummah) shall be a witness over them. After the passing (Rihlat) of the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, someone who is like the Prophet in characteristics must also be a witness over the people. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: In every generation (time), an Imam from us, the Ahl al-Bayt, is a witness over the people, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله is a witness over us. [288] Hadrat Ali عليه السلام, after reciting this Ayah, said: «هو - محمّد - شهید على الشهداء و الشهداء هم الرّسل». [289]

278) Baqarah, 143; Nahl, 89; Hajj, the last Ayah. 279) Tafsir Kabir of Fakhr Razi and Tafsir Maraghi, quoting Sahih Bukhari, Tirmidhi, and Nasa'i. 280) «انّ اللّه على كلّ شى ء شهید». Hajj, 17. 281) «جئنا من كلّ امّة بشهید». Nisa, 41. 282) «وكذلك جعلناكم امّة وسطا لتكونوا شهداء على النّاس». Baqarah, 143. 283) «وجاءت كلّ نفس معها سائق و شهید». Qaf, 21. 284) «یومئذ تحدّث اخبارها». Zilzal, 4. 285) In a Hadith, it is stated: Every new "day" addresses man: «اَنا یوم جدید و اَنا علیك لشهید، اشهد به یوم القیامة». Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 5, p. 112. 286) «تشهد علیهم السنتهم و ایدیهم و ارجلهم». Nur, 24. 287) Ghafir, 51. 288) Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 399. 289) Tafsir Burhan, Vol. 1, p. 370 and Ayyashi, Vol. 1, p. 242.

Messages

1- The Prophets are the role models (Uswah) for people in this world and witnesses over people in the Hereafter. «من كلّ اُمّة بشهید»

2- The prerequisite for being a witness is having knowledge; thus, the Prophets are aware of our deeds. «بشهیدٍ»

3- The Prophet of Islam is the noblest of the Prophets, because while the Prophets are witnesses over the deeds of their nations, the Prophet of Islam is a witness over the deeds of the Prophets. «جئناك بك على هؤلاء شهیدا»

Ayah

يَوْمَئِذٍ يَوَدُّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَعَصَوُا۟ ٱلرَّسُولَ لَوْ تُسَوَّىٰ بِهِمُ ٱلْأَرْضُ وَلَا يَكْتُمُونَ ٱللَّهَ حَدِيثًا (٤۲)
On that day those who were faithless and [who] disobeyed the Apostle will wish the earth were levelled with them, and they will not conceal any matter from Allah. (42)

Key Points

The sinful disbelievers, before the witnesses are established, deny their sin and corruption and seek a way out, saying: «واللّه ربّنا ما كنّا مشركین»[290] (By Allah, our Lord, we were not polytheists). But when the witnesses speak the truths, there is no longer any room for concealment. In the verses of the Quran, the regretful wishes of the sinners are presented in this manner: I wish I were dust. I wish I were annihilated in the dust. I wish I had not befriended so-and-so. I wish I had been a person of intellect and thought in the world. I wish I had perished with that very death...

290) Al-An'am, 23.

Messages

1- Disobedience to the governmental commands of the Prophet is equivalent to disbelief in Allah. «كفروا و عصوا الرسول»

2- The Day of Judgment is a day of regret and longing. «یودّ... لوتسوّى»

3- On the Day of Judgment, no word or matter remains hidden. «لایكتمون اللّه حدیثا»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَقْرَبُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَأَنتُمْ سُكَـٰرَىٰ حَتَّىٰ تَعْلَمُوا۟ مَا تَقُولُونَ وَلَا جُنُبًا إِلَّا عَابِرِى سَبِيلٍ حَتَّىٰ تَغْتَسِلُوا۟ ۚ وَإِن كُنتُم مَّرْضَىٰٓ أَوْ عَلَىٰ سَفَرٍ أَوْ جَآءَ أَحَدٌ مِّنكُم مِّنَ ٱلْغَآئِطِ أَوْ لَـٰمَسْتُمُ ٱلنِّسَآءَ فَلَمْ تَجِدُوا۟ مَآءً فَتَيَمَّمُوا۟ صَعِيدًا طَيِّبًا فَٱمْسَحُوا۟ بِوُجُوهِكُمْ وَأَيْدِيكُمْ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ عَفُوًّا غَفُورًا (٤۳)
O you who have faith! Do not approach prayer when you are intoxicated, [not] until you know what you are saying, nor [enter mosques] in the state of ritual impurity until you have washed yourselves, except while passing through. But if you are sick or on a journey, or any of you has come from the toilet, or you have touched women, and you cannot find water, then make your ablution on clean ground and wipe a part of your faces and your hands. Indeed Allah is all-excusing, all-forgiving. (43)

Key Points

Since the prohibition of wine (alcohol) took place gradually in early Islam. First, wine was introduced as an undesirable drink; «ومن الثمرات النخیل و الاعناب تتّخذون منه سَكراً و رزقاً حسنا»[291] Then this Ayah was revealed[292] and prohibited prayer while intoxicated. After that, He declared its harm to be greater than its benefits, «یسئلونك عن الخمر و المیسر قل فیهما اثم كبیر و منافع للنّاس و اثمهما اكبر من نفعهما»[293] Then its consumption was prohibited as a foul and satanic element. «انّما الخمر و المیسر... رجس من عمل الشیطان»[294] It is mentioned in narrations[295] that you should avoid prayer while sleepy, lethargic, or heavy (sluggish). Other verses also consider praying out of laziness to be a sign of the hypocrites.[296] Ghā'iṭ (غائط) means a low place (a hollow). Since in the past, pits were prepared for relieving oneself, ghā'iṭ is a euphemism for going to relieve oneself (defecate). Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Ṣaʿīd (صَعید) is a high place from which water flows. Imam[297] Sadiq عليه السلام said: The intent of lāmas-tum (لامستم) is sexual intercourse, but Allah is the Concealer (Sattār) and loves concealment.[298]

291) An-Nahl, 67. 292) Tafsir Ayyashi, Vol. 1, p. 242. 293) Al-Baqarah, 219. 294) Al-Ma'idah, 90. 295) Kafi, Vol. 3, p. 371. 296) An-Nisa, 142 and At-Tawbah, 54. 297) Bihar, Vol. 76, p. 347. 298) Wasā'il, Vol. 20, p. 133.

Messages

1- The status of prayer is so elevated that an alcohol consumer should not approach it. «لاتقربوا الصلوة و انتم سُكارى»

2- In prayer, mere recitations and movements are not enough; attention and awareness are necessary. «حتى تعلموا...»

3- Unaware acts of worship are worthless, even if they fulfill the obligation. «حتى تعلموا ما تقولون»

4- One should not enter the mosque while in a state of major ritual impurity (janābah). «ولا جنبا...»

5- In ghusl (ritual bath), washing the entire body is necessary, because no specific limb is mentioned in the Ayah. «تغتسلوا»

6- The possibility of danger or harm either removes the obligation from a person or causes the obligation to be lightened. «و ان كنتم مرضى... فتیمّموا»

7- Decorum must be observed in expressions and speech. (The expression "returning from the hollow" for relieving oneself, and "touching women," are euphemisms for sexual intercourse; otherwise, mere touching does not necessitate wuḍū'.)[299] «من الغائط او لامستم النار»

8- Allah's rulings allow for dispensation but not for abandonment. If water is not found, one must perform tayammum. «فلم تجدوا ماءً فتیمّموا»

9- The condition for connecting with Allah through prayer is ritual purity (ṭahārah), either wuḍū' or tayammum. «فلم تجدوا ماء فتیمّموا»

10- Tayammum must be performed on pure earth. «صعیداً طیّباً»

11- The lightening of rulings is a manifestation of Divine mercy and forgiveness. «فلم تجدوا ماءً فتیمّموا... غفوراً رحیماً»

299) Tahdhib al-Ahkam, Vol. 1, p. 22.

Ayah

أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ نَصِيبًا مِّنَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ يَشْتَرُونَ ٱلضَّلَـٰلَةَ وَيُرِيدُونَ أَن تَضِلُّوا۟ ٱلسَّبِيلَ (٤٤)
Have you not regarded those who were given a share of the Book, who purchase error and desire that you [too] should lose the way? (44)

Messages

1- Knowledge of the Divine Book alone is not sufficient for guidance. «اُتوا نصیباً من الكتاب... یشترون الضّلالة»

2- The awareness of the scholars among the People of the Book regarding Divine teachings is incomplete, and this very deficiency becomes the ground for deviation. «نصیباً من الكتاب یشترون الضّلالة»

3- Deviant scholars misuse their knowledge and position and lie in wait to cause the deviation and misguidance of others. «یریدون ان تضلّوا السبیل»

Ayah

وَٱللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِأَعْدَآئِكُمْ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ وَلِيًّا وَكَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ نَصِيرًا (٤٥)
But Allah knows your enemies better, and Allah suffices as guardian, and Allah suffices as helper. (45)

Messages

1- We might make mistakes in recognizing the enemy, but Allah knows our real enemies better. «واللّه أعلم باعدائكم»

2- Given Allah's promises regarding the guardianship and help of the believers, one should not fear conspiracies and enmities. «و كفى باللَّه ولیّاً»

3- Guardianship must be accompanied by power and help. «ولیّاً و كفى باللَّه نصیراً»

Ayah

مِّنَ ٱلَّذِينَ هَادُوا۟ يُحَرِّفُونَ ٱلْكَلِمَ عَن مَّوَاضِعِهِۦ وَيَقُولُونَ سَمِعْنَا وَعَصَيْنَا وَٱسْمَعْ غَيْرَ مُسْمَعٍ وَرَٰعِنَا لَيًّۢا بِأَلْسِنَتِهِمْ وَطَعْنًا فِى ٱلدِّينِ ۚ وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ قَالُوا۟ سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا وَٱسْمَعْ وَٱنظُرْنَا لَكَانَ خَيْرًا لَّهُمْ وَأَقْوَمَ وَلَـٰكِن لَّعَنَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ بِكُفْرِهِمْ فَلَا يُؤْمِنُونَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا (٤٦)
Among the Jews are those who pervert words from their meanings and say, ‘We hear and disobey’ and ‘Hear without listening!’ and ‘Ra’ina,’ twisting their tongues and reviling the faith. But had they said, ‘We hear and obey’ and ‘Listen’ and ‘Unzurna,’ it would have been better for them and more upright. But Allah has cursed them for their faithlessness, so they will not believe except a few. (46)

Key Points

The expression «سَمِعنا و عَصَینا» (We heard and we disobeyed) is like the sarcastic Persian saying: "It's yours to say, and ours not to listen." The Muslims used to say the word «راعِنا» (Rā'inā) to the Messenger of Allah, meaning: "Be mindful and attentive to us." However, the Jews, by distorting this word, intended an ugly meaning. If the word «راعِنا» is derived from the root ra'y (رعى), its meaning is "to observe/to take care of." But if it is derived from ru'ūnah (رعونت) and read with a doubled nūn (راعنّا - Rā'innā), it means: "Make us foolish/stupid." The Jews, by twisting their tongues and shifting words and letters, sought to humiliate the Muslims and mock the Prophet.

Messages

  1. The sin of some individuals within a group should not be attributed to all of them. «مِن الّذین...»
  2. Distortion in the meanings of words and culture is one of the key sins that paves the way for other sins and weakens the school of thought (the religion). «لیّاً بالسنتهم و طعناً فى الدین»
  3. Insulting sanctities and violating boundaries is the perpetual weapon of the disbelievers. «واسمع غیر مسمع»
  4. Responding to the call of the prophets leads to goodness and firmness. «خیراً لهم و اَقوَم»
  5. Man's destiny is the result of his own choice and performance. «لعنهم اللّه بكفرهم»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ ءَامِنُوا۟ بِمَا نَزَّلْنَا مُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا مَعَكُم مِّن قَبْلِ أَن نَّطْمِسَ وُجُوهًا فَنَرُدَّهَا عَلَىٰٓ أَدْبَارِهَآ أَوْ نَلْعَنَهُمْ كَمَا لَعَنَّآ أَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلسَّبْتِ ۚ وَكَانَ أَمْرُ ٱللَّهِ مَفْعُولًا (٤۷)
O you who were given the Book! Believe in what We have sent down confirming what is with you, before We blot out the faces and turn them backwards, or curse them as We cursed the People of the Sabbath, and Allah’s command is bound to be fulfilled. (47)

Key Points

The meaning of the effacement and turning back of faces can be the transformation of the heart, the sound nature (Fitrah), and the intellect away from the path of felicity. As a result of obstinacy and stubbornness against the Divine signs, instead of taking a step forward, they take a step backward, and their human countenance is transformed and effaced. Perhaps the intention is the physical turning of the face backward and the transformation (Maskh) of the countenance, or a change of form on the Day of Judgment. The People of the Sabbath (As-hab as-Sabt) were disobedient Jews who defied God's command regarding the prohibition of fishing on Saturdays. Using a trick, they trapped the fish on Saturday and caught them on Sunday. They were afflicted by Divine wrath and their faces were transformed into the shape of apes.[300]

300) Al-A'raf, 163.

Messages

1- Islam invites the followers of other religions to the religion of Allah. «یا ایهاالّذین اوتوا الكتاب آمنوا»

2- The scholarly figures and the knowledgeable members of society are more responsible and are addressed more frequently by Allah. «یا ایها الّذین اوتوا الكتاب آمنوا»

3- In the method of invitation (Da'wah) and propagation, the rightful beliefs and commonalities of others must be respected. «مصدقاً لما معكم»

4- The general principles of the programs of all the Prophets are in harmony with one another. «مصدّقاً لما معكم»

5- Against obstinate individuals, the leverage of threat must be used. «من قبل ان نطمس»

6- To make a threat effective, its concrete and practical examples must be pointed out. «كما لعنا اصحاب السبت»

7- Divine wrath against the obstinate is a tradition (Sunnah). «كما لعنّا اصحاب السبت»

8- The command of Allah is definite. «كان امراللّه مفعولاً»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَغْفِرُ أَن يُشْرَكَ بِهِۦ وَيَغْفِرُ مَا دُونَ ذَٰلِكَ لِمَن يَشَآءُ ۚ وَمَن يُشْرِكْ بِٱللَّهِ فَقَدِ ٱفْتَرَىٰٓ إِثْمًا عَظِيمًا (٤۸)
Indeed Allah does not forgive that partners should be ascribed to Him, but He forgives anything besides that to whomever He wishes. Whoever ascribes partners to Allah has indeed fabricated [a lie] in great sinfulness. (48)

Key Points

The topic of shirk (polytheism/association) has been raised more than two hundred times in the Quran, and every kind of attention directed toward other than Allah, under any title (idol worship, hypocrisy, non-divine inclinations, materialism, etc.), has been criticized. The content of this Ayah is repeated exactly in Ayah 116 of this same Surah, and repetition is a factor for guidance. Since shirk is an exit from the path of truth, a severance from Allah, and an attachment to another, it is not forgiven without repentance (tawbah). However, if the polytheist abandons shirk and performs genuine repentance, Allah forgives (him). In Ayah 54 of Surah Az-Zumar, He says: Allah forgives all sins, so do not despair of His mercy, and repent to Him. Since no one knows upon whom Allah's wise gaze of mercy will fall, there remains no room for arrogance or boldness in sinning. The factors for forgiveness are several: 1. Repentance (tawbah). 2. Performing good deeds. 3. Avoiding major sins (kabirah). 4. Intercession (shafa'ah). 5. Divine pardon. (All these cases are addressed in this Ayah). Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding "یغفر ما دون ذلك" (He forgives what is less than that): The intention is major sins and other sins (which Allah forgives even without repentance if He wills).[301]

301) كافى، ج ۲، ص ۲۸۴.

Messages

  1. Shirk is the most detested sin and an impediment to Divine forgiveness. «لایغفر ان یشرك به»
  2. The forgiveness of sins is related to Allah's wise Will. «لمن یشاء»
  3. Shirk is an unfounded claim and a great lie. «فقد افترى اثماً عظیما»

Ayah

أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى ٱلَّذِينَ يُزَكُّونَ أَنفُسَهُم ۚ بَلِ ٱللَّهُ يُزَكِّى مَن يَشَآءُ وَلَا يُظْلَمُونَ فَتِيلًا (٤۹)
Have you not regarded those who style themselves as pure? Indeed, it is Allah who purifies whomever He wishes, and they will not be wronged [so much as] a single date-thread. (49)

Key Points

Self-praise, considering oneself faultless, and seeing oneself as chosen and distinguished, are condemned by the Quran, as He says: «فلا تزكّوا انفسكم هو اعلم بمن اتّقى »[302] Do not praise yourselves; Allah knows better who is the God-fearing (pious) person. Hadrat Ali عليه السلام, in the sermon of "Hammam," considers one of the signs of the God-fearing to be that whenever they are praised, they become fearful.

302) Najm, 32.

Messages

1- Self-praise, which arises from arrogance and self-aggrandizement, is not permissible. «یزكّون انفسهم» 2- Purification (Tazkiyah) is valuable only if it is based on Divine commands, not human guidance. «بل اللّه یزكّى من یشاء» 3- Divine punishments are the result of man's undesirable deeds, not oppression by Allah. «لایظلمون فتیلا»

Ayah

ٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ يَفْتَرُونَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ٱلْكَذِبَ ۖ وَكَفَىٰ بِهِۦٓ إِثْمًا مُّبِينًا (٥٠)
Look, how they fabricate lies against Allah! That suffices for a flagrant sin. (50)

Key Points

The self-superiority of the Jews—who considered themselves the superior race and the children and beloved ones of Allah—is the greatest fabrication against Allah, because Allah has not taken anyone as His child, and everyone is equal in His sight, and distinction is based on faith and piety (taqwa). Fabrication (slander) includes, besides lying, accusation, harassment, oppression, and defamation. Slander against Allah invalidates the fast. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: Lying is worse than drinking wine, but slander is even more severe than lying.

Messages

1- The Prophet is obligated to recognize and evaluate the actions and beliefs of the People of the Book. «انظر كیف یفترون...»

2- Undue self-praise and considering oneself close to Allah is a fabrication against Allah and an obstacle to growth. The word «اثم» (ithm) linguistically refers to something that prevents a person from righteousness and reward. «كفى به اثماً مبیناً»

Ayah

أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ نَصِيبًا مِّنَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْجِبْتِ وَٱلطَّـٰغُوتِ وَيَقُولُونَ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ أَهْدَىٰ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ سَبِيلًا (٥۱)
Have you not regarded those who were given a share of the Book, believing in idols and fake deities and saying of the pagans: ‘These are better guided on the way than the faithful’? (51)

Key Points

After the Battle of Uhud, a group of Jews went to Mecca to ally themselves with the polytheists against the Muslims. To please and reassure the disbelievers (Kuffar), they prostrated before their idols and said: Your idol worship is better than the Islam of the Muslims. "Jibt" (جِبت), which refers to an idol, a sorcerer, or a soothsayer, has appeared once in the Quran, but the word "Taghut" (طاغوت), derived from the root tughyan (rebellion/transgression), has appeared eight times. Perhaps the intent behind Jibt and Taghut is the two idols before which the Jews prostrated, or perhaps the intent behind Jibt is the idol, and the intent behind Taghut is the idolaters and the supporters of idol worship.

Messages

1- Incomplete knowledge of religion is the ground for deviation. «نصیباً من الكتاب»

2- Enemies abandon even their own beliefs to fight Islam (the Jews prostrated before the idol to please the polytheists and coordinate against the Muslims). «یؤمنون بالجبت و الطاغوت»

3- The spirit of obstinacy and malice changes the course of judgment. «یؤمنون بالجبت و الطاغوت و یقولون... هؤلاء اهدى...»

Ayah

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَعَنَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ ۖ وَمَن يَلْعَنِ ٱللَّهُ فَلَن تَجِدَ لَهُۥ نَصِيرًا (٥۲)
They are the ones whom Allah has cursed, and whomever Allah curses, you will never find any helper for him. (52)

Messages

1- Every alliance against the truth is cursed. The alignment of the People of the Book with the polytheists against Islam caused them to be cursed. «لعنهم اللّه»

2- The unholy covenants of the enemies against the truth will never reach the desired outcome and are of no benefit against the Will of Allah. «فلن تجد له نصیراً»

3- He who is detested by Allah will always be vanquished. «و من یلعن اللّه فلن تجدله نصیراً»

Ayah

أَمْ لَهُمْ نَصِيبٌ مِّنَ ٱلْمُلْكِ فَإِذًا لَّا يُؤْتُونَ ٱلنَّاسَ نَقِيرًا (٥۳)
Do they have a share in sovereignty? If so, they will not give those people [so much as] a speck on a date-stone! (53)

Key Points

  • Naqīr (نَقیر) is either the hollow on the back of the date stone, or it is that which birds pick up with their beaks, and it is a metaphor for something insignificant and meager. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said regarding "نصيب من الملك" (a share of the dominion): The intention is the Imamate and the Caliphate, and the intention behind "الناس" (the people) is us, the Ahl al-Bayt (the Household of the Prophet). [303] Meaning, if certain people attain power, they will not grant the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام power even equal to the thread of a date stone. It is mentioned in the commentary Majma' al-Bayān that the Jews are so monopolistic that if they gain power, they will not give it to others. This meaning is more consistent with the spirit of stinginess and narrow-mindedness of the Jews.

303) كافى، ج 1، ص 205.

Messages

1- Do not worry about the unfounded judgments of others regarding your religion. "أم لهم نصيب"

2- Unlimited dominion belongs exclusively to Allah. Every individual and nation takes hold of only a corner of the dominion, and that too, only for a few days. "نصيب من الملك"

3- Know your enemies, for if they attain power, they will completely ignore you. "لا يؤتون الناس..."

4- Whoever takes hold of governance and power must be concerned with the welfare of the people. "نصيب من الملك... لا يؤتون"

Ayah

أَمْ يَحْسُدُونَ ٱلنَّاسَ عَلَىٰ مَآ ءَاتَىٰهُمُ ٱللَّهُ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ ۖ فَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَآ ءَالَ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَٱلْحِكْمَةَ وَءَاتَيْنَـٰهُم مُّلْكًا عَظِيمًا (٥٤)
Do they envy those people for what Allah has given them out of His bounty? We have certainly given the progeny of Abraham the Book and wisdom, and We have given them a great sovereignty. (54)

Key Points

The previous Ayah referred to the stinginess of the Jews, and this Ayah refers to their envy. Envy is worse than stinginess. This is because in stinginess, one does not give away his own wealth, but in envy, he is also displeased by the giving received by others. The Jews, who were previously favored by Allah's grace and blessings, why are they displeased, out of envy, that others attain blessings and power? Why should the Family of Abraham (Āl Ibrāhīm) be favored while the Family of Muhammad (Āl Muḥammad) and Banu Hashim are deprived? In narrations, the Ahl al-Bayt (the Household of the Prophet) have stated: We are those who have been envied. (304) In a hadith from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, we read that He said: The meaning of "the Book" (al-Kitāb) is prophethood, the meaning of "wisdom" (al-Ḥikmah) is understanding and judgment, and the meaning of "great dominion" (mulkan ʿaẓīm) is the obedience of the people. (305) Imam Baqir عليه السلام, in interpreting "great dominion" (mulkan ʿaẓīm), said: The meaning is that Allah placed rightful Imams among them. (306) In another hadith from Hadrat Ali عليه السلام, we read that He said: The meaning of the Family of Abraham (Āl Ibrāhīm) is us, the Household of the Prophet (Ahl al-Bayt). (307)

304) كافى، ج 1، ص 186. 305) كافى، ج 1، ص 306. 306) كافى، ج 1، ص 306. 307) بحار، ج 28، ص 275.

Messages

1- The envious person stands against the Will of Allah. «یحسدون الناس على ما آتاهم اللّه»

2- The source of all blessings and benefits is the Grace of Allah. «من فضله» Instead of wishing for the disappearance of others' blessings, wish for His Grace from Allah.

3- The alignment of the People of the Book with the polytheists and their judgment that your polytheism is better than the monotheism of the Muslims is due to envy. In previous Ayahs, we read that the Jews told the polytheists: «هولاء اهدى...» In this Ayah, we read: «ام یحسدون»

4- All favors are from Him. (The word «آتینا» [We gave] is repeated three times.)

5- Divine governance must be in the hands of those who, prior to ruling, possess high spiritual and scholarly status and insight. The names of the Book and Wisdom precede great dominion (mulkan ʿaẓīm). (Yes, spiritual blessings precede material ones.) «آتینا... الكتاب و الحكمة و آتیناهم مُلكاً عظیماً»

Ayahs

فَمِنْهُم مَّنْ ءَامَنَ بِهِۦ وَمِنْهُم مَّن صَدَّ عَنْهُ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِجَهَنَّمَ سَعِيرًا (٥٥)
Of them are some who believe in him, and of them are some who deter [others] from him; and hell suffices for a blaze! (55)
إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا سَوْفَ نُصْلِيهِمْ نَارًا كُلَّمَا نَضِجَتْ جُلُودُهُم بَدَّلْنَـٰهُمْ جُلُودًا غَيْرَهَا لِيَذُوقُوا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ عَزِيزًا حَكِيمًا (٥٦)
Indeed We shall soon make those who deny Our signs enter a Fire: as often as their skins become scorched, We shall replace them with other skins, so that they may taste the punishment. Indeed Allah is all-mighty, all-wise. (56)

Key Points

Since they persist in obstinacy every moment, they must be punished every moment with new skin. As stated in Tafsir Safi: "Ibn Abi al-Awja'," one of the opponents of religion during the era of the Imams, asked Imam Sadiq عليه السلام: What is the sin of the new skin? The Imam replied: The new skin is made from the burnt remnants of the first skin, like a brick that is broken and crushed, and then new clay and a new brick are made again.

Messages

1- The punishment of the disbelievers is perpetual. «بدّلناهم جلوداً»

2- The new skin is for the sake of tasting severe torment. (Because in burning, most of the pain and stinging relates to the skin, and if the fire reaches the bone, the pain lessens). «بدّلناهم جلوداً»

3- The Resurrection (Ma'ad) is physical. «بدّلناهم جلوداً»

4- The torment will never become ordinary for the disbelievers due to its continuity. «لیذوقوا العذاب»

5- The perpetual tormenting of the disbelievers is wise, and no power is capable of preventing the wrath of Allah in Hellfire. «عزیزاً حكیماً»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ سَنُدْخِلُهُمْ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَآ أَبَدًا ۖ لَّهُمْ فِيهَآ أَزْوَٰجٌ مُّطَهَّرَةٌ ۖ وَنُدْخِلُهُمْ ظِلًّا ظَلِيلًا (٥۷)
As for those who have faith and do righteous deeds, We shall admit them into gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them forever. In it, there will be chaste mates for them, and We shall admit them into a deep shade. (57)

Key Points

In education (or upbringing), fear and hope must be side by side. In the previous Ayah, there was fear, and this Ayah contains hope. Ẓalīl (ظَلیل) means an extended and continuous shade. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The meaning of azwāj muṭahharah (pure spouses) is wives who are clean from the impurity of menstruation (ḥayḍ) and ritual impurity (ḥadath). [308]

308) Man Lā Yaḥḍuruhu al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, p. 50.

Messages

1- Faith (īmān) and righteous deeds (‘amal ṣāliḥ) are the two fundamental conditions for entering Paradise. «آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ سَنُدْخِلُهُمْ»

2- Allah’s grace is swifter than His wrath. (In the previous Ayah, the particle sawfa (سوف) was used for the inhabitants of the Fire, which indicates the distant future, but in this Ayah, the particle sīn (س) «سَيُدْخِلُهُمْ» is used, which indicates the near future.)

3- The purity of the spouses in Paradise is both physical and spiritual. Muṭahharah (مُطَهَّرَة) is said of someone who is pure and immaculate both physically and spiritually.

Ayah

۞ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَأْمُرُكُمْ أَن تُؤَدُّوا۟ ٱلْأَمَـٰنَـٰتِ إِلَىٰٓ أَهْلِهَا وَإِذَا حَكَمْتُم بَيْنَ ٱلنَّاسِ أَن تَحْكُمُوا۟ بِٱلْعَدْلِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ نِعِمَّا يَعِظُكُم بِهِۦٓ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ سَمِيعًۢا بَصِيرًا (٥۸)
Indeed Allah commands you to deliver the trusts to their [rightful] owners, and to judge with fairness when you judge between people. Excellent indeed is what Allah advises you. Indeed Allah is all-hearing, all-seeing. (58)

Key Points

Trustworthiness and just behavior free from discrimination are among the important signs of faith, just as betrayal of trust is a sign of hypocrisy. It is stated in a hadith: Do not look at the lengthy bowing (ruku') and prostration (sujud) of individuals, but rather look at their truthfulness and trustworthiness. (309) In numerous narrations, the meaning of trust (al-amānah) has been identified as the leadership of the community, whose rightful possessors (ahl) are the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام. This is a clear manifestation of acting upon this Ayah. Yes, the key to the happiness of society is having qualified individuals in charge and just behavior, while the source of social disorder is the leadership of the unqualified and oppressive judgments. (310) Betrayal of trust includes concealing knowledge, profession, and truth (haqq), seizing people's property, obeying non-Divine leaders, choosing an unqualified spouse or teacher for one's children, and so forth. Imam Baqir and Imam Sadiq علیهما السلام said: The commands and prohibitions of Allah are Divine trusts. (311) Imam Sadiq علیه السلام, in interpreting this Ayah, said: Allah has commanded that every Imam should entrust what is with him to the Imam succeeding him. (312) Trust (amānah) is of three types: A: Between man and Allah. (The duties and obligations prescribed for man.) (313) B: Between man and others. (The property or secrets of others held by man.) C: Between man and himself. (Such as knowledge, lifespan, and power, which are trusts in our hands.)

309) Kāfī, Vol. 2, p. 105. 310) Hadrat Amīr علیه السلام said: «من تقدّم على قوم و هو یرى فیهم من هو افضل، فقد خان اللّه و رسوله والمؤمنین» (Whoever puts himself forward as a leader over a people while knowing there is someone among them better than him, has certainly betrayed Allah, His Messenger, and the believers.) Al-Ghadīr, Vol. 8. 311) Tafsīr Nūr al-Thaqalayn. 312) Kāfī, Vol. 1, p. 277. 313) Tafsīr Majma' al-Bayān.

Messages

1- Entrusting affairs to the competent and judging justly are instances of righteous deeds and signs of faith. (Considering the previous Ayah)

2- Every trust has an owner, and everyone has the competence and aptitude for a specific task or position. "اهلها" (its rightful possessors)

3- Faith is not a prerequisite for fulfilling trusts and administering justice. One must be trustworthy and just toward all people. "اهلها، الناس" (its rightful possessors, the people)

4- The judge and ruler must be just. "تحكموا بالعدل" (that you judge with justice)

5- Establishing a government is necessary. The requirement for entrusting responsibilities to their rightful possessors and judging justly is the establishment of a Divine system and government. "تؤدّوا الامانات... تحكموا بالعدل" (that you render the trusts... that you judge with justice)

6- Good admonition is that which, besides moral advice, also pays attention to social and judicial issues. "نعمّا یعظكم به" (Excellent is that with which He admonishes you)

7- Even if people do not realize the betrayal of trust or oppression in judgment, Allah is All-Hearing and All-Seeing. "انّ اللّه كان سمیعاً بصیراً" (Indeed, Allah is ever All-Hearing, All-Seeing)

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَطِيعُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُوا۟ ٱلرَّسُولَ وَأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَمْرِ مِنكُمْ ۖ فَإِن تَنَـٰزَعْتُمْ فِى شَىْءٍ فَرُدُّوهُ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ وَٱلرَّسُولِ إِن كُنتُمْ تُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ خَيْرٌ وَأَحْسَنُ تَأْوِيلًا (٥۹)
O you who have faith! Obey Allah and obey the Apostle and those vested with authority among you. And if you dispute concerning anything, refer it to Allah and the Apostle, if you have faith in Allah and the Last Day. That is better and more favourable in outcome. (59)

Key Points

The previous Ayah stated the duties of rulers—that they should be just and trustworthy. This Ayah refers to the duty of the people towards Allah and the Prophet. With the existence of three authorities: "Allah," "the Prophet," and "Ulul-Amr" (those vested with authority), people will never reach a dead end. The inclusion of these three authorities for obedience does not contradict Quranic Monotheism (Tawhid), because obedience to the Prophet and Ulul-Amr is a ray of obedience to Allah and is in line with it (in tul), not parallel to it (in ard), and obedience to these two is necessary by the command of Allah.

In Tafsir Namuneh, quoting Ibn Abbas, it is stated that this Ayah was revealed when the Prophet of Islam, upon departing for Tabuk, left Ali عليه السلام in Medina in his place and said: "You are to me in the position of Aaron to Moses."

In the previous Ayah, it was recommended that trusts be handed over to those who are worthy of them. This Ayah seems to state: This handing over to the worthy is achieved under the shadow of obedience to Allah, the Messenger, and Ulul-Amr.

The repetition of the command "Obey" (اطیعوا) is the secret behind the diversity of the instructions. The Prophet sometimes explained divine rulings, and sometimes issued governmental orders, holding two positions: "Prophethood (Risalah)" and "Governance (Hukūmah)." The Quran sometimes addresses the Prophet, saying: «انزلنا الیك الذّكر لتبیّن للناس ما نزل الیهم» [314] (We have sent down to you the Reminder that you may explain to the people what was sent down to them.) And sometimes it says: «لتحكم بین الناس بما اراك اللّه» [315] (That you may judge between the people by what Allah has shown you.)

Regarding the corrupt, the extravagant, the misguided, the ignorant, the tyrants, and others, the Quran gives the command "Do not obey" (لاتطع) and "Do not follow" (لاتتّبع). Therefore, those included in the command "Obey" (اطیعوا) must be individuals whose obedience has not been forbidden, and whose obedience does not contradict the commands of Allah and the Messenger.

Obedience to Ulul-Amr is mentioned in the Ayah, but in times of dispute, referring back to them is not mentioned; rather, only Allah and the Messenger are introduced as the sole authorities for resolving disputes. This indicates that if a dispute arises even regarding the identification and instance of Ulul-Amr, you must refer back to Allah and the Messenger, for in the Prophetic narrations, Ulul-Amr have been introduced as the Ahl al-Bayt (Household) of the Prophet. [316]

Husayn ibn Abi al-Ala says: I presented my belief regarding the successors (Awsiya) of the Prophet and that their obedience is obligatory, to Imam Sadiq عليه السلام. He said: Yes, it is so. These are the ones about whom Allah said: «اطیعوا اللّه...» and they are the ones about whom He said: «انمّا ولیّكم اللّه... و هم راكعون» [317]

Imam Ali عليه السلام stated in Sermon 125 of Nahj al-Balaghah and in the Covenant to Malik al-Ashtar: The meaning of referring back to Allah and the Messenger is referring back to the clear verses (Ayat Muhkamat) and the Sunnah upon which all agree.

Imam Ali عليه السلام said: Any ruler who judges based on anything other than the view of the infallible Ahl al-Bayt of the Prophet علیهم السلام is a Taghut (tyrant/false deity). [318]

314) An-Nahl, 44. 315) An-Nisa, 105. 316) Kamal al-Din al-Saduq, p. 222. 317) Al-Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 187. 318) Da'a'im al-Islam, Vol. 2, p. 530.

Messages

  1. People must accept the Islamic system and support its divine leaders in word and deed. «اطیعوااللّه و اطیعواالرسول و اولى الامر منكم»
  2. Islam is a school of thought whose beliefs and politics are intertwined. Obedience to the Messenger and Ulul-Amr, which is a political matter, is combined with faith in Allah and the Day of Judgment, which is a doctrinal matter. «اطیعوااللّه و اطیعوا الرسول و اولى الامر»
  3. Hierarchy in obedience must be maintained. «اللّه، الرسول، اولى الامر»
  4. Ulul-Amr must be infallible (Ma'sum) like the Prophet, so that obedience to them, being on par with obedience to the Prophet, is unquestioning. «اطیعواالرسول و اولى الامر»
  5. Obedience is obligatory only to a ruler who is a believer and is from among the believing people themselves. «اطیعوا... منكم»
  6. The mention of Ulul-Amr alongside the names of Allah and the Messenger, coupled with absolute obedience, indicates that the head of the Islamic government and system must possess high sanctity. «اطیعواالرسول و اولى الامر»
  7. The sign of true faith is referring back to Allah and the Messenger during disputes and escalating conflicts; obedience under normal circumstances is not a significant achievement. «فان تنازعتم»
  8. One of the duties of the Islamic government is to create unity and resolve disputes. «ان تنازعتم...»
  9. A complete religion must have a solution for all disagreements. « فى شى ء فردوه الى اللّه و الرسول»
  10. Accepting commands from non-divine and Taghut governments is forbidden (Haram). «فردّوه الى اللَّه والرسول»
  11. If all sects consider the Quran and the Sunnah as the reference point, disagreements will be resolved, and unity and solidarity will prevail. «فردّوه الى اللَّه والرسول»
  12. Those who oppose the rulings of Allah, the Messenger, and the divine leaders should doubt their own faith. «ان كنتم تؤمنون...»
  13. The practical program of Islam is based on its doctrinal program. «اطیعوا... ان كنتم تؤمنون» In other words, faith in Allah and the Day of Judgment is the executive guarantee for the rulings of Islam and the source of avoiding disobedience to Allah and the Messenger.
  14. Foresight and consideration of long-term interests are the measure of value. «احسن تأویلا»

Ayah

أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى ٱلَّذِينَ يَزْعُمُونَ أَنَّهُمْ ءَامَنُوا۟ بِمَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ وَمَآ أُنزِلَ مِن قَبْلِكَ يُرِيدُونَ أَن يَتَحَاكَمُوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱلطَّـٰغُوتِ وَقَدْ أُمِرُوٓا۟ أَن يَكْفُرُوا۟ بِهِۦ وَيُرِيدُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ أَن يُضِلَّهُمْ ضَلَـٰلًۢا بَعِيدًا (٦٠)
Have you not regarded those who claim that they believe in what has been sent down to you and what was sent down before you? They desire to seek the judgment of fake deities, though they were commanded to reject them, and Satan desires to lead them astray into far error. (60)

Key Points

The previous Ayah introduced Allah and the Prophet as the reference point for disputes. This Ayah criticizes those who make the Taghut (tyrant/false authority) the reference point for their disputes. A dispute arose between a hypocrite (who pretended to be Muslim) and a Jew. They agreed to arbitration. The Jew accepted the Prophet as the arbitrator due to His trustworthiness and justice, but the hypocrite said: "I am satisfied with the arbitration of Ka'b ibn al-Ashraf (who was one of the Jewish leaders)," because he knew that he could sway Ka'b's opinion in his favor through gifts. This Ayah was revealed in condemnation of that behavior. According to Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, Taghut is someone who does not rule justly, and who issues commands and is obeyed in opposition to the command of Allah. (319)

(319) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- Faith without disavowal (tabarri) of the Taghut is not faith; it is merely the illusion of faith. «يزعمون أنهم آمنوا... يتحاكموا إلى الطاغوت»

2- Religions share a common goal and essence, and belief in the commands of all Prophets is necessary. «أنزل إليك وما أنزل من قبلك»

3- Compromise with the Taghut is forbidden (haram). «أمروا أن يكفروا به»

4- First, show the path of truth, then negate the path of falsehood. «فرُدّوه إلى اللَّه والرسول... يريدون أن يتحاكموا إلى طاغوت»

5- Even the thought and intention of referring to the Taghut are subject to criticism. «يريدون أن يتحاكموا إلى الطاغوت»

6- Satan's success lies in the people's obedience to the Taghuts. «يتحاكموا إلى الطاغوت... يريد الشيطان أن يضلّهم»

7- The hypocrite, the Taghut, and Satan are on one path, standing against the Prophets. «الّذين يزعمون... يتحاكموا إلى الطاغوت... يريد الشيطان»

8- Disbelief in the Taghut and the prohibition of referring to tyrannical courts are commands of all heavenly religions. «ألم تر... أنزل إليك وما أنزل من قبلك... يتحاكموا إلى الطاغوت»

9- Seeking out foreigners to resolve internal disputes is the desire of Satan and is forbidden. «يريد الشيطان...»

10- Hypocrites are under the will of Satan, and the Taghuts are the traps and tools of Satan. «يريد الشيطان أن يضلهم»

11- The result of referring to the Taghut is falling into a terrifying abyss. «ضلالاً بعيداً»

Ayah

وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ تَعَالَوْا۟ إِلَىٰ مَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ وَإِلَى ٱلرَّسُولِ رَأَيْتَ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقِينَ يَصُدُّونَ عَنكَ صُدُودًا (٦۱)
When they are told, ‘Come to what Allah has sent down and [come] to the Apostle,’ you see the hypocrites keep away from you aversely. (61)

Messages

1- Although the hypocrites are not submissive to the law of Allah and the command of the Prophet, we must invite them. «قیل لهم تعالوا»

2- In accepting the invitation of the prophets, there is growth and elevation. («تعالوا» is an invitation to ascend.)

3- The Book and the Sunnah are harmonious; otherwise, referring people to two contradictory things would not be wise. «الى ما انزل اللَّه و الى الرّسول»

4- Referring to the Taghut and seeking judgment from outsiders is the characteristic and spirit of the hypocrites, who oppose the divine leader and turn away from the path of truth. (The word «یصدّون» is a sign of characteristic and persistence.)

5- The hypocrite, besides turning away himself, also prevents others from the path. «یصدون»

6- What unveils the disbelief of the hypocrites is their opposition to the divine leadership. «عنك»

7- Hypocrites have no sensitivity regarding people's heartfelt faith in Allah; their main distress is the gathering of people around the divine leader. «یصدّون عنك صدوداً»

Ayah

فَكَيْفَ إِذَآ أَصَـٰبَتْهُم مُّصِيبَةٌۢ بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ ثُمَّ جَآءُوكَ يَحْلِفُونَ بِٱللَّهِ إِنْ أَرَدْنَآ إِلَّآ إِحْسَـٰنًا وَتَوْفِيقًا (٦۲)
But how will it be when an affliction visits them because of what their hands have sent ahead? Then they will come to you, swearing by Allah, ‘We desired nothing but to do good and to bring about comity.’ (62)

Key Points

The hypocrites' justification for seeking judgment from the Taghut (false authority) was that their goal was reconciliation, and if they had gone to the Prophet for judgment and He had ruled in favor of one party, the other would naturally have become upset with the Messenger of Allah and caused an uproar, which was incompatible with the Prophet's dignity. Therefore, to preserve the Prophet's honor, position, and popularity, they did not take the dispute to Him.

Messages

1- The cause of danger is man's own deeds. «مصیبة بما قدّمت ایدیهم»

2- Double-faced Muslims and hypocrites approach the Divine leaders when problems arise and they sense danger. «جاؤك»

3- Referring to the Taghut results in disorder and calamities, «اصابتهم مصیبة», which necessitates returning to the path of the Prophets. «جاؤك»

4- Hypocrites justify their wrongdoings. «ان اردنا الاّ احساناً»

5- The hypocrite uses swearing by Allah as a cover for his goals. «یحلفون بالله»

6- Sometimes sacred words become tools in the hands of unholy individuals. «احساناً و توفیقاً»

Ayah

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ يَعْلَمُ ٱللَّهُ مَا فِى قُلُوبِهِمْ فَأَعْرِضْ عَنْهُمْ وَعِظْهُمْ وَقُل لَّهُمْ فِىٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ قَوْلًۢا بَلِيغًا (٦۳)
They are the ones whom Allah knows as to what is in their hearts. So let them alone, and advise them, and speak to them concerning themselves far-reaching words. (63)

Messages

1- The efforts of the hypocrites are futile, because Allah is aware of their inner thoughts and intentions, and He exposes them at the right time. «یعلم اللّه ما فى قلوبهم»

2- The apologies of the hypocrites are not sincere, otherwise the command to turn away from them would not have been issued. «یعلم اللّه ما فى قلوبهم فأعرض عنهم»

3- The leader's method of dealing with the hypocrites must be one of turning away and admonition. «فأعرض عنهم و عِظهم»

4- In the position of admonition, the hypocrites must be explicitly warned about the consequences of their actions and their destiny. «فى انفسهم قولاً بلیغاً»

Ayah

وَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَا مِن رَّسُولٍ إِلَّا لِيُطَاعَ بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ إِذ ظَّلَمُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ جَآءُوكَ فَٱسْتَغْفَرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَٱسْتَغْفَرَ لَهُمُ ٱلرَّسُولُ لَوَجَدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ تَوَّابًا رَّحِيمًا (٦٤)
We did not send any apostle but to be obeyed by Allah’s leave. Had they, when they wronged themselves, come to you and pleaded to Allah for forgiveness, and the Apostle had pleaded for them [to Allah]for forgiveness, they would have surely found Allah all-clement, all-merciful. (64)

Key Points

The portrait of a healthy society and its sound management is that its people are faithful, its leader is heavenly, the obedience and submission of the people are strong, its deviants are regretful and seek forgiveness (Istighfar), and its leader is accepting and kind. Just as the prayer (Du'a) of the Prophet for the believers is answered, «استغفر لهم الرسول لوجدوا اللّه» (if the Messenger asks forgiveness for them, they would find Allah), the prayer of the righteous and the angels also has an effect on them. In one place, the angels' seeking forgiveness (Istighfar) is mentioned for the people: «و یستغفرون لمن فى الارض» (and they ask forgiveness for those on earth) [320], and in another place, for the believers: «و یستغفرون للذین آمنوا» (and they ask forgiveness for those who have believed) [321].

320) Ash-Shura, 5. 321) Ghafir, 7.

Messages

1- The purpose of sending the prophets is the guidance of people through obedience to them. «الا لیطاع»

2- Obedience belongs exclusively to Allah. Even obedience to the prophets must be by Allah's permission (Idhn), otherwise, it is polytheism (Shirk). «لیطاع باذن اللّه»

3- The repentance for abandoning the leader is returning to the leader. «لیطاع - جاؤك»

4- Abandoning the prophets and resorting to the tyrant (Taghut) is oppression against one's own human status. «ظلموا انفسهم» (Considering the connection of this Ayah with the preceding Ayahs).

5- The connection of all people with the Messenger of Allah must be strong, whether they are believers or transgressors (Fasiq). The believer is for gaining grace (Faydh), and the transgressor is for attaining intercession (Shafa'at). «جاؤك»

6- The path of return and repentance (Tawbah) is open for wrongdoers and even hypocrites. (Considering the preceding Ayahs which relate to the hypocrites). «فاستغفروا اللّه»

7- Visiting the heavenly leaders, seeking aid (Istidmad), and seeking intercession (Tawassul) through those dear ones is confirmed by the Quran. «جاؤك فاستغفروااللّه و استغفر لهم الرسول»

8- The Prophet is not the forgiver of sins; He is the intermediary for Divine pardon. «استغفر لهم الرسول»

9- The wrongdoer must first regret and return to the Truth, and then, to strengthen the relationship with Allah, seek help from the station of Messengership. «استغفروا اللّه واستغفر لهم الرسول»

10- Sinners should not despair. Seeking forgiveness (Istighfar), repentance (Tawbah), and visiting the friends of Allah (Awliya Allah) are means for their spiritual reconstruction. «جاءوك فاستغفروا...»

11- During the visit to the Messenger of Allah, and by confessing one's slip and seeking forgiveness, one feels relieved of sin. «لوجدوا اللَّه تواباً»

12- If you forgive the wrongdoers, also show them kindness. «توّاباً رحیماً»

13- Sin is a veil between man and Divine Mercy, and as soon as that veil is removed, mercy is received. «استغفر.... لوجدوا اللَّه»

14- Acknowledging and confessing sin and seeking forgiveness for it in the presence of the friends of Allah (Awliya Allah) is a means and a basis for Divine pardon. «جاؤك فاستغفروا... لوجدوا اللَّه توّاباً رحیماً»

Ayah

فَلَا وَرَبِّكَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ حَتَّىٰ يُحَكِّمُوكَ فِيمَا شَجَرَ بَيْنَهُمْ ثُمَّ لَا يَجِدُوا۟ فِىٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ حَرَجًا مِّمَّا قَضَيْتَ وَيُسَلِّمُوا۟ تَسْلِيمًا (٦٥)
But no, by your Lord! They will not believe until they make you a judge in their disputes, then do not find within their hearts any dissent to your verdict and submit in full submission. (65)

Key Points

A dispute arose between Zubayr ibn al-Awwam—one of the Muhajirūn (Emigrants)—and one of the Ansar (Helpers) over the irrigation of a palm grove. The Prophet judged that since the upper part of the garden belonged to Zubayr, he should irrigate first. The Ansarī man became upset with the Prophet's judgment and said that because Zubayr was the son of the Prophet's aunt, He had ruled in his favor! The Prophet's color changed, and this Ayah was revealed. It is astonishing that they themselves accepted the Messenger of Allah as a judge, yet they refused to submit! Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: If people perform prayer, Hajj, and Zakat, but harbor suspicion regarding the actions of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, they are not truly believers. He then recited this Ayah. [322]

322) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Al-Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 390.

Messages

1- The signs of true faith are three: A: Taking the judgment to the Prophet instead of the Taghut (tyrant). «یحكموك» B: Not harboring suspicion regarding the Prophet's judgment. «لایجدوا فى انفسهم حرجاً» C: Accepting the Prophet's command wholeheartedly and submitting fully. «و یسلّموا تسلیما»

2- Islam pays complete attention not only to outward submission but also to the spiritual dimensions and heartfelt submission. «لا یجدوا فى انفسهم حرجاً»

3- Judgment (Qada') is among the functions of Prophethood (Risalah) and Guardianship (Wilayah). «ممّا قَضیت»

4- The obligation of submission before the Prophet's judgment is a sign of His infallibility ('Ismah). «یُسلّموا تسلیماً»

Ayah

وَلَوْ أَنَّا كَتَبْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ أَنِ ٱقْتُلُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَكُمْ أَوِ ٱخْرُجُوا۟ مِن دِيَـٰرِكُم مَّا فَعَلُوهُ إِلَّا قَلِيلٌ مِّنْهُمْ ۖ وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ فَعَلُوا۟ مَا يُوعَظُونَ بِهِۦ لَكَانَ خَيْرًا لَّهُمْ وَأَشَدَّ تَثْبِيتًا (٦٦)
Had We prescribed for them, [commanding]: ‘Slay [the guilty among] your folks or leave your habitations,’ they would not have done it except a few of them. And if they had done as they were advised, it would have been better for them and stronger in confirming [their faith]. (66)

Key Points

This Ayah refers to the difficult obligations that were placed upon previous nations. For example, as punishment for the calf-worship of the Children of Israel, Allah commanded them to kill one another. This was because a great sin like idolatry required a massive compensation, such as killing one another and leaving their cities and homelands.

Messages

1- Believers must assess themselves: if a difficult command is issued, how will they respond to it? «و لو انّا كتبنا علیهم»

2- During trials, fewer people are capable of fulfilling the obligation and proving their claim. «ما فعلوه الا قلیل»

3- The command to disbelieve in the Taghut (tyrant/false deity), to refer to the Prophet, and to submit to his judgments, is divine admonition and benevolence. «ما یوعظون به لكان خیراً لهم»

4- Goodness and felicity depend on action. «فعلوا - خیراً»

5- Divine laws (commands and prohibitions) are the admonitions of Allah. «فعلوا ما یوعظون به»

6- The more we step forward in the way of Allah, the greater our steadfastness and faith become. «فعلوا...اشدّ تثبیتاً»

7- Action affects the soul and spirit. Acting upon the divine laws causes the permanence of faith. «فعلوا... اشدّ تثبیتاً»

8- Divine obligations, even if seemingly unpleasant and difficult, are ultimately for the good and welfare of humanity. «و لو انّهم فعلوا... لكان خیراً لهم»

Ayahs

وَإِذًا لَّـَٔاتَيْنَـٰهُم مِّن لَّدُنَّآ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا (٦۷)
Then We would surely have given them from Us a great reward, (67)
وَلَهَدَيْنَـٰهُمْ صِرَٰطًا مُّسْتَقِيمًا (٦۸)
and We would have guided them to a straight path. (68)

Key Points

|

Messages

  1. Attaining goodness (khayr), steadfastness, great reward, and guidance to the straight path is achieved through human action upon admonitions and perseverance. «فعلوا... خیراً - تثبیتاً - اجراً - مستقیماً»

  2. Stepping onto the path of goodness leads to the attainment of higher and greater goodness. «فعلوا - اشدّ تثبیتاً - اجراً عظیماً - صراطاً مستقیماً»

Ayahs

وَمَن يُطِعِ ٱللَّهَ وَٱلرَّسُولَ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ مَعَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَنْعَمَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِم مِّنَ ٱلنَّبِيِّـۧنَ وَٱلصِّدِّيقِينَ وَٱلشُّهَدَآءِ وَٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ ۚ وَحَسُنَ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ رَفِيقًا (٦۹)
Whoever obeys Allah and the Apostle—they are with those whom Allah has blessed, including the prophets and the truthful, the martyrs and the righteous, and excellent companions are they! (69)
ذَٰلِكَ ٱلْفَضْلُ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ عَلِيمًا (۷٠)
That is the grace of Allah, and Allah suffices as knower [of His creatures]. (70)

Key Points

In Surah Al-Fatiha, alongside the Straight Path (صراط مستقیم), the group "انعمت علیهم" (those upon whom You have bestowed favor) was mentioned. This is the second time that the group "انعم اللّه علیهم" (those upon whom Allah has bestowed favor) is discussed alongside the verse of the Straight Path. It seems that apart from the Prophets (انبیا), the martyrs (شهدا), the truthful (صدّیقان), and the righteous (صالحان), others go astray, and the Straight Path is exclusively the path of one of these four groups. In narrations, the best examples of the truthful (صدّیقان) are introduced as the infallible Imams (علیهم السلام), and the truthful woman (صدیقه) is Fatimah al-Zahra (علیها السلام). The meaning of "شهدا" (martyrs) is either those slain in the field of Jihad, or the witnesses of deeds on the Day of Judgment. Companionship with the Prophets in this world is not possible for all true followers; therefore, the intent of the verse is companionship in the Hereafter.

Messages

1- Being on the path of the Prophets and martyrs and having good companions is only achieved through obedience to the command of Allah and the Messenger. «و من یطع اللّه و الرسول، فاولئك ...»

2- Good companions are the Prophets, the martyrs, the truthful, and the righteous. Worldly companions must also be chosen based on these characteristics. «حَسُن اولئك رفیقاً»

3- Obedience to the Messenger is a reflection of obedience to Allah and is aligned with it; thus, it does not contradict Monotheism (Tawhid). «من یطع اللّه و الرسول»

4- The reward for obeying the Prophet is proximity to all the Prophets, because they are all one light and share one goal, and obedience to one is joining with all. «من یطع اللَّه و الرسول... مع... النّبیین»

5- Allah's awareness is the best encouraging factor for fulfilling one's duty. «باللّه علیماً»

6- The rank of Prophethood is higher than the rank of the truthful (صدّیقین), the martyrs, and the righteous (because the name of the Prophets is mentioned before theirs). «من النّبیین و الصدّیقین...»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ خُذُوا۟ حِذْرَكُمْ فَٱنفِرُوا۟ ثُبَاتٍ أَوِ ٱنفِرُوا۟ جَمِيعًا (۷۱)
O you who have faith! Take your precautions, then go forth in companies, or go forth en masse. (71)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, obedience to Allah, the infallible leadership, and the sovereignty of the Prophet were discussed, while in this Ayah, the necessity of vigilance and military readiness for the Islamic Ummah and the rightful leadership is addressed. Hidhru («حِذْر»), means alertness, readiness, and means of defense. Thubāt («ثُبات»), the plural of thubah («ثُبه»), refers to scattered groups (including partisan groups that confuse the enemy and disrupt their concentration). Muslims must protect the borders of their country. Acting upon this Ayah is the secret to honor, and neglecting it is the secret to the downfall and defeat of Muslims.

Messages

1- Muslims must be ready and vigilant at all times, aware of the enemies' plans, personnel, type of weaponry, morale, and internal and external cooperation, and plan and act accordingly. (یا ایها الّذین آمنوا خذوا حذركم)

2- Military readiness is valuable when it is under the shade of faith. (یاایّها الّذین آمنوا خذوا حذركم)

3- Muslims must mobilize. (انفروا جمیعاً)

4- Muslims must use various tactics to confront the enemy. (ثبات او انفروا جمیعاً)

5- Determine the method of mobilization and struggle yourselves. (ثبات او انفروا جمیعاً)

Ayah

وَإِنَّ مِنكُمْ لَمَن لَّيُبَطِّئَنَّ فَإِنْ أَصَـٰبَتْكُم مُّصِيبَةٌ قَالَ قَدْ أَنْعَمَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىَّ إِذْ لَمْ أَكُن مَّعَهُمْ شَهِيدًا (۷۲)
Among you is indeed he who drags his feet, and should an affliction visit you, he says, ‘It was certainly Allah’s blessing that I did not accompany them!’ (72)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the danger of external enemies was discussed; here, the danger of internal enemies, infiltrators, and hypocrites is discussed. The word "يُبَطِّئَنَّ" (yubatti'anna) is derived from the root baṭw (بطو), meaning slowness or tardiness. According to linguists, buṭū' (بطوء) refers both to slow movement and to calling others to move slowly.

Messages

1- Battlefields are a good means for recognizing individuals weak in faith and hypocrites. "و ان منكم..."

2- Allah unveils the thoughts and words of the hypocrites. "و ان منكم ..."

3- Hypocrites sometimes blend in with the believers so much that they appear to be part of them. "منكم"

4- Not all companions of the Prophet were just or followed his path. "منكم... ليُبَطِّئَنَّ"

5- Hypocrites are factors that weaken the morale of the Muslims ("ليُبَطِّئَنَّ"); therefore, they must be identified and not sent to the front.

6- Hypocrites consider non-participation in war, fleeing the front, and escaping death as the key to success and felicity. "أَنْعَمَ اللَّهُ عَلَيَّ"

7- Not every comfort, immunity, or favor is a blessing (Ni'mah) from Allah. "أَنْعَمَ اللَّهُ عَلَيَّ"

8- Experiencing hardships alongside the believers is a blessing, but comfort separate from the believers is not a blessing. "أَصَابَتْكُمْ مُصِيبَةٌ... لَمْ أَكُنْ مَعَهُمْ"

Ayah

وَلَئِنْ أَصَـٰبَكُمْ فَضْلٌ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ لَيَقُولَنَّ كَأَن لَّمْ تَكُنۢ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُۥ مَوَدَّةٌ يَـٰلَيْتَنِى كُنتُ مَعَهُمْ فَأَفُوزَ فَوْزًا عَظِيمًا (۷۳)
But should a bounty from Allah come to you, he will say—as if there were no [tie of friendship and] affection between you and him—‘I wish I were with them so that I had achieved a great success!’ (73)

Key Points

In the attainment of grace and spoils, the name of Allah is mentioned, «فضل من اللّه» (Grace is from Allah), but in encountering the hardships mentioned in the previous Ayah, the name of Allah is not mentioned, «اصابتكم مصیبة» (A misfortune afflicted you). This seems to indicate that nothing reaches us from Allah except grace and mercy.

Messages

1- Victory in war and its spoils are from the grace of Allah. «اصابكم فضل من اللّه»

2- Due to worldly interests, hypocrites lose their affection. «كان لم تكن بینكم و بینه مودّة»

3- The wishes of the hypocrite are not sincere. «لئن اصابكم فضل... یالیتنى»

4- The hypocrite is an opportunist. During misfortunes, he says: Allah favored (us) by our absence. «انعم اللّه على» And during victory and spoils, he says: I wish I had been with them. «یالیتنى كنت معهم»

5- He who does not share in the sorrows of the believers but wishes to share in the benefits possesses a characteristic of the hypocrites. «لئن اصابكم فضل... یالیتنى كنت معهم»

6- In the view of the hypocrites, salvation is the attainment of the world. «فوزاً عظیماً»

Ayah

۞ فَلْيُقَـٰتِلْ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّذِينَ يَشْرُونَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَا بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۚ وَمَن يُقَـٰتِلْ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ فَيُقْتَلْ أَوْ يَغْلِبْ فَسَوْفَ نُؤْتِيهِ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا (۷٤)
Let those who sell the life of this world for the Hereafter fight in the way of Allah; and whoever fights in the way of Allah, and then is slain, or he subdues [the enemy], We shall soon give him a great reward. (74)

Messages

1- The goal of war in Islam is solely Allah, not territorial expansion, exploitation, or revenge. «فى سبیل اللّه»

2- Jihad is a divine success (Tawfiq) that is not granted to everyone. «فلیقاتل ... الّذین یشرون الحیوة الدنیا» Worldly seekers possess neither the merit for the battlefield nor the courage for it.

3- The Greater Jihad (Jihad al-Akbar), which is the struggle against worldliness, is the prerequisite for the Lesser Jihad (Jihad al-Asghar), which is the struggle against the enemy. «فلیقاتل فى سبیل اللّه الّذین ...»

4- The Mujahid (striver) in the way of Allah is never defeated. (Instead of saying «یُغلب» [he is defeated], He says: «یقتل» [he is killed].)

5- On the front of truth, flight and defeat are irrelevant; it is either martyrdom or victory. «یقتل او یغلب»

6- The hypocrite considers only the spoils of war as the «فوز عظیم» (great attainment) (in the previous Ayah), but the Quran has designated «اجر عظیم» (great reward) for both victory and martyrdom. «اجراً عظیما»

7- The promise of «اجر عظیم» is an encouragement to depart for the battlefield.

8- The life of the Hereafter is superior to the life of this world, and trading this world for the Hereafter is a great undertaking. «یشرون الحیوة الدنیا بالاخرة... اجراً عظیما»

9- The reward for the martyr and the victorious combatant is the same. «فیقتل او یغلب....اجراً عظیماً»

Ayah

وَمَا لَكُمْ لَا تُقَـٰتِلُونَ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ وَٱلْمُسْتَضْعَفِينَ مِنَ ٱلرِّجَالِ وَٱلنِّسَآءِ وَٱلْوِلْدَٰنِ ٱلَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَآ أَخْرِجْنَا مِنْ هَـٰذِهِ ٱلْقَرْيَةِ ٱلظَّالِمِ أَهْلُهَا وَٱجْعَل لَّنَا مِن لَّدُنكَ وَلِيًّا وَٱجْعَل لَّنَا مِن لَّدُنكَ نَصِيرًا (۷٥)
Why should you not fight in the way of Allah and the oppressed men, women, and children, who say, ‘Our Lord, bring us out of this town whose people are oppressors, and appoint for us a guardian from Yourself, and appoint for us a helper from Yourself’? (75)

Key Points

The Ayah concerns the Muslims who were under pressure and torture by the people of Mecca. It criticizes those who abandoned the struggle (Jihad) for their freedom. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The meaning of the Mustaḍ'afīn (the oppressed) who are under the rule of tyrants is us. And Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, in response to the question of who the Mustaḍ'afīn are, said: They are those who are neither believers nor disbelievers. [323]

323) Tafsir Ayyashi, Vol. 1, p. 257.

Messages

1- Muslims must have zeal and ideological commitment towards each other and must not be indifferent to cries and pleas for help. «مالكم....»

2- One of the goals of Islamic Jihad is the effort to free the oppressed (Mustaḍ'afīn) from the domination of the tyrants. «فى سبیل اللّه و المستضعفین».

3- Islam recognizes no borders; wherever there is an oppressed person (Mustaḍ'af), there is Jihad. «والمستضعفین»

4- The army of Islam must be powerful enough to be the savior of all the deprived people of the world. «ما لكم لاتقاتلون»

5- In the method of calling to Jihad, one must utilize people's emotions and sympathies. «المستضعفین... الّذین یقولون ربّنا...»

6- Guardianship (Wilayah) and authority over Muslims must be from Allah. «من لدنك وليّاً»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ يُقَـٰتِلُونَ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ يُقَـٰتِلُونَ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱلطَّـٰغُوتِ فَقَـٰتِلُوٓا۟ أَوْلِيَآءَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ ۖ إِنَّ كَيْدَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ كَانَ ضَعِيفًا (۷٦)
Those who have faith fight in the way of Allah, and those who are faithless fight in the way of fake gods. So fight the friends of Satan; indeed the stratagems of Satan are always flimsy. (76)

Key Points

Although the satanic plots of the disbelievers are great, «ومكروا مكراً كبّارا»[324] and it is close that the mountains be removed from their place due to the plotting of the disbelievers, «و ان كان مكرهم لتزول منه الجبال»[325] yet they are weak and feeble in the face of the Will of Allah.

324) Nuh, 22. 325) Ibrahim, 46.

Messages

1- The requirement of faith is striving (Jihad) against the disbelievers. «الّذین آمنوا یقاتلون»

2- Islamic striving (Jihad) is in the way of Allah and for the preservation of the religion of Allah, not for territorial expansion or revenge. «یقاتلون فى سبیل اللّه»

3- The divine society and the non-divine society can be recognized by the nature of their conflicts. «فى سبیل اللّه، فى سبیل الطاغوت»

4- Conflict and opposition have always existed and continue to exist, but in what way and for what purpose? «فى سبیل اللّه...»

5- The triangle of disbelief (Kufr), tyranny (Taghut), and Satan has a strong alliance. «كفروا، الطاغوت، الشیطان»

6- By comparing the two types of struggle and goals, realize the value of your work and strive in battle. «فقاتلوا اولیاء الشیطان»

7- Although the enemies have plots, do not fear, for their plots are weak. «انّ كید الشیطان كان ضعیفاً»
