8- The end of the path of Taghut (tyranny) and the way of Satan is failure. «انّ كید الشیطان كان ضعیفاً»

Ayah

أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى ٱلَّذِينَ قِيلَ لَهُمْ كُفُّوٓا۟ أَيْدِيَكُمْ وَأَقِيمُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَءَاتُوا۟ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ فَلَمَّا كُتِبَ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْقِتَالُ إِذَا فَرِيقٌ مِّنْهُمْ يَخْشَوْنَ ٱلنَّاسَ كَخَشْيَةِ ٱللَّهِ أَوْ أَشَدَّ خَشْيَةً ۚ وَقَالُوا۟ رَبَّنَا لِمَ كَتَبْتَ عَلَيْنَا ٱلْقِتَالَ لَوْلَآ أَخَّرْتَنَآ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍ قَرِيبٍ ۗ قُلْ مَتَـٰعُ ٱلدُّنْيَا قَلِيلٌ وَٱلْـَٔاخِرَةُ خَيْرٌ لِّمَنِ ٱتَّقَىٰ وَلَا تُظْلَمُونَ فَتِيلًا (۷۷)
Have you not regarded those who were told, ‘Keep your hands off [from warfare], and maintain the prayer and give the zakat’? But when fighting was prescribed for them, behold, a part of them feared the people as if fearing Allah, or were even more afraid, and they said, ‘Our Lord! Why did You prescribe fighting for us? Why did You not respite us for a short time?!’ Say, ‘The enjoyments of this world are trifle and the Hereafter is better for the Godwary, and you will not be wronged so much as a single date-thread. (77)

Key Points

The early Muslims, during the difficult period in Mecca, sought permission from the Prophet of Islam to fight the polytheists, saying: Before Islam, we were honored; now, because of Islam, we are under pressure. Let us fight so that we may regain our honor. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, who was not commanded to fight at that time, did not grant permission. But after the Hijra (migration), when the command for Jihad came, a group of those very people who had sought Jihad began to object and make excuses, and they considered the disbelievers of Mecca great and feared them intensely. Khashyah (خَشیة) refers to a fear that arises from the greatness and high regard given to the opposing party.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The intent of "كُفّوا ایدیكم" (Restrain your hands) is "كفّوا السنتكم" (Restrain your tongues). It seems they were giving empty slogans, so the Ayah was revealed, telling them to desist. [326]

Imam Baqir عليه السلام cited the peace treaty of Imam Hassan عليه السلام as one example, stating that that peace was better for the Islamic Ummah (community) than all that the sun shines upon. [327] Yes, the infallible leader sometimes commands a ceasefire, like Imam Hassan عليه السلام, and sometimes deems war and martyrdom necessary, like Imam Hussain عليه السلام.

In several narrations, we read that the "near term" (ajal qarīb) until which people requested the delay of Jihad, refers to the uprising of Hadrat Mahdi عليه السلام. [328] Meaning, some individuals say: The ruling for fighting should not come until the time of the uprising of Imam al-Zaman عليه السلام.

326) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn; Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 114. 327) Kafi, Vol. 8, p. 330. 328) Tafsir Ayyashi, Vol. 1, p. 257.

Messages

1- Attention to history is constructive. "ألم تر..."

2- At the beginning of any revolution, enduring difficulties and having patience is necessary. "كفوا ایدیكم"

3- Premature emotions and empty slogans must be controlled. "كفوا ایدیكم"

4- Allah's commands are based on real interests, not the demands of the people. "كُفّوا ایدیكم" (Considering the occasion of revelation)

5- The sword is not always effective everywhere. In critical conditions and lack of readiness, one should not give an excuse to a powerful enemy. "كُفّوا ایدیكم"

6- Do not precede the Messenger of Allah or the command of Allah, and do not express personal opinions in religious matters. "كفّوا ایدیكم"

7- Achieve inner peace through Prayer (Salat), which is the remembrance of Allah, and fill economic gaps through Zakat. "اقیموا الصلوة و آتواالزكاة"

8- The devotional matters of Islam are intertwined with its economic matters. "الصلوة, الزكاة"

9- Among acts of worship, Prayer and Zakat hold a special place. "اقیموا الصلوة و آتوا الزكاة"

10- Prayer precedes Zakat. (Wherever these two are mentioned, Prayer is named first.)

11- The command for Prayer and Zakat was issued and legislated before Jihad. "كُفّوا ایدیكم و اقیموا الصلوة..."

12- Self-purification precedes community building. He who is not devoted to Prayer and Zakat will not be devoted to sincerity and sacrifice either. "اقیموا الصلوة و آتواالزكاة"

13- Those who heat up quickly also cool down quickly. Yes, every hot thing cools down, but nothing that is cooked becomes raw again. "كفّوا ایدیكم... فلما كتب... لِمَ كتبت"

14- Being revolutionary is not important; remaining revolutionary is important. Sometimes there is a great distance between slogan and action. "فلما كتب...اذا فریق منهم یخشون"

15- The command for Jihad is a means of testing the people. "كتب... القتال... فریق منهم یخشون"

17- The important factor for abandoning Jihad is worldly attachments. "لولااخرتنا الى اجل"

16- The source of objection to the command of Jihad is fear. "یخشون... لِمَ كتبت"

18- Do not object to Allah's commands or the timing of their issuance. Set aside your own temporal and spatial calculations in the face of the Divine command. "لولا اخّرتنا الى اجل قریب"

19- Objection to Allah's command is a sign of lack of piety. "لِمَ كتبت... خیرٌ لمن اتّقى"

20- If you set your outlook wider than this material world, you will not become attached to the meager provisions of the world. "والاخرة خیر"

21- The successful ones in the Hereafter are only the people of piety. "خیر لمن اتّقى"

Ayah

أَيْنَمَا تَكُونُوا۟ يُدْرِككُّمُ ٱلْمَوْتُ وَلَوْ كُنتُمْ فِى بُرُوجٍ مُّشَيَّدَةٍ ۗ وَإِن تُصِبْهُمْ حَسَنَةٌ يَقُولُوا۟ هَـٰذِهِۦ مِنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَإِن تُصِبْهُمْ سَيِّئَةٌ يَقُولُوا۟ هَـٰذِهِۦ مِنْ عِندِكَ ۚ قُلْ كُلٌّ مِّنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ فَمَالِ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ ٱلْقَوْمِ لَا يَكَادُونَ يَفْقَهُونَ حَدِيثًا (۷۸)
Wherever you may be death will overtake you, even if you were in fortified towers.’ If any good befalls them, they say, ‘This is from Allah;’ and when an ill befalls them, they say, ‘This is from you.’ Say, ‘All is from Allah.’ What is the matter with these people that they would not understand any matter? (78)

Key Points

This verse is both an encouragement for Jihad (struggle) and a call not to fear death on the battlefield, and a response to the ill omens spread by the hypocrites, who, instead of properly analyzing and understanding issues, attribute the bitterness and defeats to the Prophet of Allah.

Messages

  1. Considering the certainty and inevitability of death, why flee from war? «اینما تكونوا...»

  2. Slandering the leader is one of the methods of the hypocrites. «هذه من عندك»

  3. One should not justify mistakes by absolving oneself of responsibility and blaming one's sins on others. «یقولوا هذه من عندك»

  4. Victory and defeat, death and life, bitterness and sweetness—all are within the scope of Allah's wise decrees. «كلّ من عنداللّه»

  5. The origin of all bitter and sweet events is Allah, not what dualism claims by proposing Ahriman (evil spirit) and Yazdan (good spirit). «كلّ من عنداللّه»

  6. The hypocrites are obstinate and scarcely grasp the truth. «لایكادون یفقهون حدیثاً»

  7. Considering Allah as the center of everything (Unity of Acts - Tawhid al-Af'al) requires precise and deep contemplation. «یفقهون»

  8. Whoever does not comprehend Tawhid (Unity) and the centrality of Allah will not comprehend any of the divine teachings (Ma'arif). «لایفقهون حدیثاً»

Ayah

مَّآ أَصَابَكَ مِنْ حَسَنَةٍ فَمِنَ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَمَآ أَصَابَكَ مِن سَيِّئَةٍ فَمِن نَّفْسِكَ ۚ وَأَرْسَلْنَـٰكَ لِلنَّاسِ رَسُولًا ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ شَهِيدًا (۷۹)
Whatever good befalls you is from Allah; and whatever ill befalls you is from yourself. We sent you as an apostle to mankind, and Allah suffices as witness. (79)

Key Points

In the divine perspective, everything is created by Allah. «اللّه خالق كلّ شى ء» (329) And Allah has created everything well. «احسن كلّ شى ء خلقه» (330) What relates to Allah is creation, which is inseparable from goodness (husn). Our misfortunes and afflictions are, firstly, the lack of those perfections, which are not created by Allah; and secondly, what causes deprivation from divine blessings is the action of the individual or the society. As one scholar put it, when the Earth revolves around the Sun, the part facing the Sun is always bright. If the other side is dark, it is because it has turned its back on the Sun; otherwise, the Sun always gives light. Therefore, one can say to the Earth: O Earth, wherever you are bright, it is from the Sun, and wherever you are dark, it is from yourself. In this Ayah too, man is addressed that whatever good reaches you is from Allah, and whatever evil reaches you is from yourself. Despite all the praise the Quran gives to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, it must be said that the intent of «من نفسك» (from yourself) is the human soul (nafs), not the person of the Prophet. And Allah knows best. We read in the previous Ayah that all good and evil are from Allah, and in this Ayah, we read that only good is from Him and evil is from man. This is because evil is attributed to man insofar as it originates from man, and it is attributed to Allah insofar as man himself and his will are under the divine dominion. Just as if a government employee commits a violation, this violation is attributed both to the employee himself and, because he is a government employee, to the government. Imam Reza عليه السلام says: Allah addresses man, saying that your will and desire are also from Me. (331) Yes, man can make decisions by the Will of Allah.

329) Az-Zumar, 63. 330) As-Sajdah, 7. 331) Tafsir Ayyashi, Vol. 1, p. 258.

Messages

1- In the divine worldview, every good and beauty is from Allah. «من حسنة فمن اللّه»

2- In the face of demoralization, morale must be strengthened. When the hypocrites attributed evils to the Messenger of Allah, this Ayah, in compensation, says: «من سیّئة فمن نفسك»

3- The mission of the Prophet of Islam is universal. «ارسلناك للناس»

4- Prophets are a means of goodness for all people. «للناس»

Ayah

مَّن يُطِعِ ٱلرَّسُولَ فَقَدْ أَطَاعَ ٱللَّهَ ۖ وَمَن تَوَلَّىٰ فَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَـٰكَ عَلَيْهِمْ حَفِيظًا (۸٠)
Whoever obeys the Apostle certainly obeys Allah; and as for those who turn their backs [on you]; We have not sent you to keep watch over them. (80)

Messages

1- The speech and conduct of the Prophet, like the Quran, are authoritative and mandatory to follow. «من یطع الرسول...»

2- Obedience to the governmental commands of the Prophet is obligatory. (The intent of obeying the Messenger is His ruling commands; otherwise, obedience to the Divine instructions conveyed by the Messenger is obedience to Allah, not the Messenger.) «من یطع الرسول»

3- The Prophets are infallible. «من یطع الرسول فقد اطاع اللّه»

4- The commands of the Prophet are a reflection of the Divine commands and are subordinate to them. «یطع الرسول فقد اطاع اللَّه»

5- Man is free-willed, not compelled. «من یطع... و من تولّى»

6- The duty of the Prophets is conveyance (of the message), not imposition. «و من تولى فما أرسلناك علیهم حفیظاً»

Ayah

وَيَقُولُونَ طَاعَةٌ فَإِذَا بَرَزُوا۟ مِنْ عِندِكَ بَيَّتَ طَآئِفَةٌ مِّنْهُمْ غَيْرَ ٱلَّذِى تَقُولُ ۖ وَٱللَّهُ يَكْتُبُ مَا يُبَيِّتُونَ ۖ فَأَعْرِضْ عَنْهُمْ وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ وَكِيلًا (۸۱)
They profess obedience [to you], but when they go out from your presence, a group of them conspire overnight [to do] something other than what you say. But Allah records what they conspire overnight. So disregard them and put your trust in Allah, for Allah suffices as trustee. (81)

Messages

1- Do not be reassured by every declaration of loyalty and faith, because the hypocrites present themselves as obedient through flattery. «یقولون طاعة»

2- The hypocrites hold secret night meetings and have organized groups. «بیّت طائفة»

3- Enemies utilize blind and dark spots; do not be heedless of their plots. «بیّت»

4- The Quran does not treat everyone equally in criticism. «طائفة منهم»

5- Allah will hold the hypocrites accountable for their plots and mischief. «یكتب ما یبیّتون»

6- Against the secret plots of the hypocrites, one must rely solely on Allah; reliance (Tawakkul) is the factor of success. «توكّل على اللّه»

7- Allah is the Protector of the Muslims and the Noble Messenger صلى الله علیه وآله; He lifts the veil from the plots of the hypocrites and aids them with unseen help. «و كفى بالله وكیلا»

Ayah

أَفَلَا يَتَدَبَّرُونَ ٱلْقُرْءَانَ ۚ وَلَوْ كَانَ مِنْ عِندِ غَيْرِ ٱللَّهِ لَوَجَدُوا۟ فِيهِ ٱخْتِلَـٰفًا كَثِيرًا (۸۲)
Do they not contemplate the Quran? Had it been from [someone] other than Allah, they would have surely found much discrepancy in it. (82)

Key Points

One of the accusations they leveled against the Prophet of Islam was that someone else had taught the Qur'an to Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله: «یُعلّمه بشر» [332]. This verse was revealed in response to them. Usually, changes, developments, and contradictions occur in the long run in the speeches and writings of human beings. However, the fact that the Qur'an, throughout the 23 years of its revelation, under various conditions of war and peace, obscurity and fame, strength and weakness, and through the ups and downs of time—and that too from the tongue of an unlettered person—has been expressed without any disagreement or contradiction, is proof that it is the Word of Allah, not something learned from a human being. The command to reflect upon the Qur'an (تدبّر) for everyone, in every era and generation, is the secret that every thinker, at any time, will arrive at a new point. Imam Ali عليه السلام said regarding the boundlessness of the Qur'anic concepts: «بَحراً لایُدرك قَعره» [333] The Qur'an is a sea whose depth cannot be fathomed.

332) An-Nahl, 103. 333) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 198.

Messages

  1. Failure to reflect upon the Qur'an is subject to Allah's reproach and blame. «افلا یتدبرون»
  2. Reflection upon the Qur'an is a healing remedy for hypocrisy. «و یقولون طاعة... افلا یتدبرون...»
  3. The path to inclination towards Islam and the Qur'an is reflection and contemplation, not blind imitation. «افلا یتدبرون»
  4. The Qur'an invites everyone to reflect, and human understanding is capable of grasping its knowledge. «افلا یتدبّرون القرآن»
  5. The notion that there are contradictions and disagreements in the Qur'an is the result of a superficial view and a lack of reflection and precision. «افلایتدبّرون»
  6. The Qur'an is the proof of the truthfulness of the Prophet's mission. «لو كان من عند غیر اللَّه...»
  7. The uniformity and lack of disagreement in the verses indicate that their source is an unchangeable Being. «لو كان من عند غیر اللّه»
  8. Whatever is from Allah is true, firm, and far removed from conflict, dispersion, and contradiction. «لو كان من عند غیر اللَّه لوجدوا...»
  9. Contradiction and inconsistency are always visible in non-Divine laws. «لو كان من عند غیر اللَّه لوجدوا...»
  10. Disagreement, change, and development are inherent necessities of human theories. «لوجدوا فیه اختلافاً»
  11. The best way to invalidate any school of thought is to discover and articulate its contradictions. «لوجدوا فیه اختلافاً كثیراً»

Ayah

وَإِذَا جَآءَهُمْ أَمْرٌ مِّنَ ٱلْأَمْنِ أَوِ ٱلْخَوْفِ أَذَاعُوا۟ بِهِۦ ۖ وَلَوْ رَدُّوهُ إِلَى ٱلرَّسُولِ وَإِلَىٰٓ أُو۟لِى ٱلْأَمْرِ مِنْهُمْ لَعَلِمَهُ ٱلَّذِينَ يَسْتَنۢبِطُونَهُۥ مِنْهُمْ ۗ وَلَوْلَا فَضْلُ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُۥ لَٱتَّبَعْتُمُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا (۸۳)
When a report of safety or alarm comes to them, they immediately broadcast it; but had they referred it to the Apostle or to those vested with authority among them, those of them who investigate would have ascertained it. And were it not for Allah’s grace upon you and His mercy, you would have surely followed Satan, [all] except a few. (83)

Key Points

The dissemination and spread of confidential news and rumors have always harmed Muslims. The spreading of secret news usually occurs due to naivety, revenge, causing harm, being manipulated, material greed, showing off, and displaying information. Islam, due to its comprehensiveness, has addressed this issue, and in this Ayah, it condemns the disclosure of military secrets, considering the reporting of news of victory or defeat before presenting it to the leaders as a cause for undue arrogance or unnecessary fear of the enemy. And were it not for the divine warnings and favors, Muslims would have fallen further into this satanic path (of revealing secrets). Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: "Whoever reveals our secrets is like one who has drawn a sword against us."[334] Imam Baqir عليه السلام says: The meaning of 'those who can deduce it' (أهل استنباط) in the Ayah refers to the Infallible Imams (A'immah Ma'sumeen).[335]

334) Wasā’il, Vol. 27, p. 197. 335) Wasā’il, Vol. 27, p. 200.

Messages

1- Secrecy and the preservation of security news are the duties of Muslims, and the disclosure of their secrets is forbidden (Haram). «اذا جاءهم امر... اذاعوا به»

2- Spreading rumors is one of the weapons of the hypocrites. «اذاعوا» (Considering the preceding Ayahs)

3- Frontline news and military secrets must reach a single point, and after evaluation and determination, be published only to the extent of the expediency (Maslahah). «ردّوه الى الرسول»

4- The common people must refer to those capable of deduction (Ahl al-Istinbat). «ردوه الى الرسول...»

5- The holders of authority (Ulu al-Amr) must be from among the believers themselves. «اولى الامر منهم»

6- Muslims must possess a government, organization, and leadership. The holders of authority (Ulu al-Amr) possess state, governance, and power. «الى اولى الامر منهم»

7- Military, political, and security issues must be under a management possessing Ijtihad (independent reasoning) and Istinbat (deduction). «لعلمه الّذین یستنبطونه»

8- Controlling and guiding security news and social secrets, establishing news organizations for gathering and evaluating crucial news, and presenting it to the people are among the responsibilities of leadership. «لعلمه الّذین یستنبطونه»

9- There is a close relationship between Wilayah (guardianship/authority) and Faqahah (jurisprudence). The holders of authority (Ulu al-Amr) must be capable of deduction (Ahl al-Istinbat). «اولى الامر... یستنبطونه»

10- Deduction (Istinbat) is not limited only to jurisprudential rulings (Ahkam Fiqhi). «امر من الامن او الخوف... یستنبطونه»

11- Neutralizing plots by placing competent authorities in charge of news and its investigation is an instance of Allah's favor and mercy. «ردّوه الى الرسول... فضل اللّه»

12- Appointing the Messenger of Allah (Rasul Allah) and the holders of authority (Ulu al-Amr) as the reference points for the people, and saving the people from following Satan, is the greatest favor and mercy of God. «و لولا فضل اللَّه علیكم و رحمته...»

13- Correct centralization prevents deviation, and a leaderless society falls into the trap of Satan. «لولا فضل اللّه لاتّبعتم»

14- Spreading military news and failing to refer to the divine leaders is following Satan. «لاتّبعتم الشیطان»

15- Most people are deceived by rumors. «لاتّبعتم الشیطان الا قلیلاً»

Ayah

فَقَـٰتِلْ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ لَا تُكَلَّفُ إِلَّا نَفْسَكَ ۚ وَحَرِّضِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ۖ عَسَى ٱللَّهُ أَن يَكُفَّ بَأْسَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ أَشَدُّ بَأْسًا وَأَشَدُّ تَنكِيلًا (۸٤)
So fight in the way of Allah: you are responsible only for yourself, but urge on the faithful [to fight]. Maybe Allah will curb the might of the faithless, for Allah is greatest in might and severest in punishment. (84)

Key Points

After the victory of the disbelievers at Uhud, Abu Sufyan proudly said: We will meet the Muslims again during the season of Badr Sughra (the location where a market is held in the land of Badr in the month of Dhul Qa'dah). When the appointed time arrived, the Prophet invited the people to Jihad. Some people lacked the morale to attend due to the defeat at Uhud. The above Ayah was revealed, and the Prophet invited the people to Jihad once again. Seventy people attended, but no conflict occurred, and the Muslims returned safely to Medina. Nukūl (نكول) means refusing out of fear. And Tankīl (تنكیل) is the performance of an action that deters the opponent, such as punishment or retribution. It is narrated from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام that no other Prophet was commanded to wage Jihad if he was left alone. [336] In our time too, Imam Khomeini قدس سره said: Let the superpowers know that even if Khomeini remains utterly alone, he will continue his path and his struggle against polytheism (shirk) and disbelief (kufr). Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: After the revelation of this Ayah, the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله personally commanded all the battles. [337]

336) Tafsir Safi. 337) Tafsir Burhan, vol. 1, p. 398 and Ayyashi, vol. 1, p. 261.

Messages

1- The leader must be the vanguard for others. «فقاتل»

2- If Muslims become indifferent to the cries of the oppressed (mustaḍ'afīn), the leader must act alone. «الاّ نفسك»

3- The leader must be so resolute that the people's participation or non-participation in Jihad has no effect on him. «الاّ نفسك»

4- Everyone is responsible for his own actions. «لاتكلّف الا نفسك»

5- One must rely on Allah, not the people. «الاّ نفسك»

6- The duty of the Prophets is encouragement and invitation, not compulsion. «حرّض المؤمنین»

7- The front line and Jihad require strong propagation. «حرّض المؤمنین»

8- Our duty is to rise up and wage Jihad; defeating the enemy is Allah's task. «فقاتل... عسى اللّه ان یكفّ»

9- Divine power is the supreme power, a source of encouragement for the believers and a threat to the transgressors. «واللّه اشد بأساً»

Ayah

مَّن يَشْفَعْ شَفَـٰعَةً حَسَنَةً يَكُن لَّهُۥ نَصِيبٌ مِّنْهَا ۖ وَمَن يَشْفَعْ شَفَـٰعَةً سَيِّئَةً يَكُن لَّهُۥ كِفْلٌ مِّنْهَا ۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ مُّقِيتًا (۸٥)
Whoever intercedes for a good cause shall receive a share of it, and whoever intercedes for an evil cause shall share its burden, and Allah is prepotent over all things. (85)

Key Points

  • «مُقیت» (Muqīt) means He who provides sustenance (qūt) to others and is the Preserver of their lives. Generally, it is used in the sense of Preserver and Reckoner (Accountant). In the previous Ayah, everyone was responsible for their own deeds, but this Ayah explains the role of invitation and mediation in good deeds, and how one shares in the reward and punishment. Admonition, reconciliation, teaching, encouraging participation in the front, and cooperation in good deeds are examples (manifestations) of «شفاعة حسنة» (good intercession). Just as in the Hadith, praying for others, enjoining good (Amr bil Ma'rūf), guidance, and even pointing toward a good deed are mentioned as examples of «شفاعة حسنة» (good intercession). [338] Backbiting, tale-bearing, obstruction of work, accusation, sedition, discouraging participation in the front, temptation, and conspiracy are examples of «شفاعة سیئة» (evil intercession).

338) Tafsir Safi.

Messages

1- Individualism and isolation are forbidden in Islam. «یشفع شفاعة حسنة» 2- Inviting others to good or evil results in sharing the reward or punishment. «نصیبٌ منها» 3- Due to limitations, one cannot directly intervene in every matter, but through good intercessions, one can benefit from them. «نصیب منها» 4- A broker (mediator) should be paid a right (fee); those who mediate in charitable affairs or commerce have a right. «یشفع شفاعة حسنة یكن له نصیب منها» 5 - In mediations, Allah must be kept in mind. «كان اللّه على كل شى ء مقیتاً»

Ayah

وَإِذَا حُيِّيتُم بِتَحِيَّةٍ فَحَيُّوا۟ بِأَحْسَنَ مِنْهَآ أَوْ رُدُّوهَآ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ حَسِيبًا (۸٦)
When you are greeted with a salute, greet with a better one than it, or return it; indeed Allah takes account of all things. (86)

Key Points

The meaning of tahiyyah (greeting) is offering salutations to others, or any other act [339] that is accompanied by the wish for the life, health, and happiness of others, such as giving a gift. For instance, when a slave girl presented a bouquet of flowers to Imam Hassan عليه السلام, the Imam freed her, and in response to the people's question, He recited this very Ayah. In Islam, there is encouragement to greet others, whether we know them or not. And the one who is stingy in offering greetings is considered a miser. The Prophet would greet everyone He met, even children. In the educational system of Islam, the greeting is not only from the younger to the elder; Allah, the Prophet, and the angels offer greetings to the believers. [340] (Refer to the narrations regarding the etiquette of greeting).

339) Tafsir Qummi, Vol. 1, p. 145. 340) Allah's greeting: «سلام على نوح فى العالمین». (As-Saffat, 79). The Prophet's greeting: «اذا جاءك الّذین یؤمنون بآیاتنا فقل سلام علیكم». (Al-An'am, 54). The angels' greeting: «تتوفّاهم الملائكة طیّبین یقولون سلام علیكم». (An-Nahl, 32).

Messages

1- Strengthen your emotional relationship with one another. «فحیّوا باَحسن»

2- Rejecting the kindness of people is improper; it must be accepted and compensated for in some way. «حیّوا باحسن منها»

3- The response to affection and gifts should not be delayed. (Indicated by) the letter fa in the word «فحیوا»

4- Offering a better reward is recommended (mustahabb) in Islam. «فحیّوا باحسن منها»

5- In responding to the good deeds of others, first seek to give a better response; if that is not possible, give a similar response. «باحسن منها او ردّوها»

6- Even in war, if the enemy proposes peace, accept it with greater mercy, or accept it with reciprocity. «حییتم بتحیّة فحیّوا باحسن منها»

7- Be concerned about unanswered greetings, for if people do not respond, Allah keeps account of it; and do not leave people's affections unanswered, for Allah will hold you accountable. «انّ اللّه كان على كلّ شیى ء حسیباً»

Ayah

ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۚ لَيَجْمَعَنَّكُمْ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ ۗ وَمَنْ أَصْدَقُ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ حَدِيثًا (۸۷)
Allah—there is no god except Him—will surely gather you on the Day of Resurrection, in which there is no doubt; and who is more truthful in speech than Allah? (87)

Messages

1- Both the Origin (Mabda') and the Return (Ma'ad) are in the hands of one God. «اللّه لااله الا هو لیجمعنكم...»

2- The Resurrection (Qiyamah) is the Day when everyone will be gathered for accountability, so one must strive to attain His pleasure and worship Him alone. «لیجمعنّكم الى یوم القیامة»

3- Despite all the proofs for the Return (Ma'ad) (such as God's justice, His wisdom, the signs of resurrection in nature and its life after death in winter, sleep and wakefulness, the renewal of body cells, etc.), there is no room for doubt regarding the Resurrection. «لاریب»

4- Wherever negligence is greater, the Educator must give more decisive warnings. (The signs of emphasis in the Ayah: the letter lām, the heavy nūn of emphasis (nūn at-tawkīd ath-thaqīlah), the phrase «لاریب», and the phrase «اصدق حدیثاً», are all due to man's negligence regarding the Resurrection.)

Ayah

۞ فَمَا لَكُمْ فِى ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقِينَ فِئَتَيْنِ وَٱللَّهُ أَرْكَسَهُم بِمَا كَسَبُوٓا۟ ۚ أَتُرِيدُونَ أَن تَهْدُوا۟ مَنْ أَضَلَّ ٱللَّهُ ۖ وَمَن يُضْلِلِ ٱللَّهُ فَلَن تَجِدَ لَهُۥ سَبِيلًا (۸۸)
Why should you be two groups concerning the hypocrites, while Allah has made them relapse [into unfaith] because of their deeds? Do you desire to guide someone Allah has led astray? Whomever Allah leads astray, you will never find any way for him. (88)

Key Points

The word irkās (اِركاس) means to overturn or invert, and the intent of arkasahum (اركسهم) in the Ayah is the intellectual downfall (inversion) of the hypocrites. In a few previous Ayahs, we read an Ayah concerning intercession (shafā'ah) in matters of good and evil. This Ayah presents an example of intercession for the hypocrites. A group in Mecca outwardly became Muslim but did not emigrate (hijrah) and practically remained allied with the polytheists. Later, when they did emigrate (perhaps for espionage), the believers held two opinions about them. Some said: Since they bear witness to Monotheism (Tawhīd) and the Resurrection (Ma'ād), we should treat them fraternally. Others, due to their failure to emigrate and their cooperation with the disbelievers (kuffār), favored a negative approach toward them. It was then that this Ayah was revealed. We should interpret the sentences of the Quran alongside one another. In this Ayah, we have both aḍalla Allāh (اضل اللّه) and yuḍlil Allāh (یضلل اللّه), but the phrase bimā kasabū (بما كسبوا) is also present. This means that divine misguidance is due to our own actions.

Messages

1- Despite having a leader like the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, divergence of opinions is undesirable. «فمالكم...»

2- Decisiveness and unity are necessary when dealing with hypocrites. «فمالكم...فئتین»

3- The factor leading to man's downfall is his own deeds. «اركسهم بما كسبوا»

4- Do not be kinder than the mother (i.e., overly sympathetic). Why show pity for someone who has prepared the grounds for his own downfall? «بماكسبوا...أتریدون»

5- When the measure of sin is full, Allah's wrath is certain. All factors become ineffective before the power of Allah. «ومن یضلل اللّه فلن تجد له سبیلاً»

6- For the one who, according to the divinely established system of law, is removed from the path due to his actions, even you, who are the Prophet, will find no way to save him. «فلن تجد له سبیلا»

Ayah

وَدُّوا۟ لَوْ تَكْفُرُونَ كَمَا كَفَرُوا۟ فَتَكُونُونَ سَوَآءً ۖ فَلَا تَتَّخِذُوا۟ مِنْهُمْ أَوْلِيَآءَ حَتَّىٰ يُهَاجِرُوا۟ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ فَإِن تَوَلَّوْا۟ فَخُذُوهُمْ وَٱقْتُلُوهُمْ حَيْثُ وَجَدتُّمُوهُمْ ۖ وَلَا تَتَّخِذُوا۟ مِنْهُمْ وَلِيًّا وَلَا نَصِيرًا (۸۹)
They are eager that you should disbelieve like they have disbelieved, so that you all become alike. So do not make friends [with anyone] from among them, until they migrate in the way of Allah. But if they turn their backs, seize them and kill them wherever you find them, and do not take from among them friends or helpers, (89)

Key Points

The method of dealing with the people of hypocrisy has stages. These severe rulings mentioned in this Ayah pertain to a group of hypocrites who cooperated with the polytheists in their conspiracies.

Messages

1- Allah unveils the secrets and inner desires of the hypocrites. «ودّوا...»

2- The hypocrites and enemies desire your thought and belief (i.e., they want you to disbelieve). «ودّوا لو تكفرون»

3- The Islamic society must be purified of hypocrites and cut off the bond of friendship and affection with them. «فلا تتخذوا منهم اولیاء»

4- Migration (Hijrah) from the region of disbelief, polytheism, and conspiracy is obligatory. «حتى یهاجروا»

5- The sign of true faith is migration in the way of Allah. «حتى یهاجروا فى سبیل الله»

6- The path of repentance is always open. «حتى یهاجروا»

7- The repentance for every sin is the compensation for that sin. The repentance for abandoning migration is migration itself. «حتى یهاجروا»

8- Obstructive and conspiring hypocrites must be executed and suppressed, and for this task, the Islamic society must be powerful. «خذوهم واقتلوهم»

9- Islam recognizes no boundaries in fighting the enemy and pursuing the corruptors. «خذوهم واقتلوهم حیث وجدتموهم»

10- One should not rely on the material resources and military strength of the hypocrites, and their help must be disregarded. «لاتتّخذوا منهم ولیّاً ولانصیراً»

11- Any kind of heartfelt, political, military, economic, or cultural connection with a hypocrite is condemned in Islam. «لاتتّخذوا منهم ولیّاً ولانصیراً»

Ayah

إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ يَصِلُونَ إِلَىٰ قَوْمٍۭ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُم مِّيثَـٰقٌ أَوْ جَآءُوكُمْ حَصِرَتْ صُدُورُهُمْ أَن يُقَـٰتِلُوكُمْ أَوْ يُقَـٰتِلُوا۟ قَوْمَهُمْ ۚ وَلَوْ شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ لَسَلَّطَهُمْ عَلَيْكُمْ فَلَقَـٰتَلُوكُمْ ۚ فَإِنِ ٱعْتَزَلُوكُمْ فَلَمْ يُقَـٰتِلُوكُمْ وَأَلْقَوْا۟ إِلَيْكُمُ ٱلسَّلَمَ فَمَا جَعَلَ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمْ عَلَيْهِمْ سَبِيلًا (۹٠)
excepting those who join a people between whom and you there is a treaty, or such as come to you with hearts reluctant to fight you, or to fight their own people. Had Allah wished, He would have imposed them upon you, and then they would have surely fought you. So if they keep out of your way and do not fight you, and offer you peace, then Allah does not allow you any course [of action] against them. (90)

Messages

1- Military covenants, even those made with disbelievers (kuffār), must be respected. «بینكم و بینهم میثاق»

2- In wars, do not attack those who declare neutrality. «حصرت صدورهم ان یقاتلوكم او یقاتلوا قومهم»

3- Muslims must be so powerful that opponents do not even contemplate attack. «حصرت صدورهم ان یقاتلوكم»

4- Pay attention to Divine power so that you do not fall into arrogance. «لوشاءالله لسلّطهم»

5- By the will and decree of Allah, the hypocrites and disbelievers were restrained from fighting you. Beware of attributing their weakness to your own strength. «و لو شاء اللَّه لَسلّطهم علیكم»

6- Islamic Jihad is not for domination or imposing belief, but rather for repelling evil and obstacles. Therefore, now that they have withdrawn from you, do not attack them; if you attack, it is possible that Allah may empower them and they will annihilate you. «و لو شاء اللَّه.... لسلطهم....»

7- Respect peace-seeking motives and requests for a ceasefire, because in Islam, peace is the principle, and war is only permissible out of necessity. «فان اعتزلوكم فلم یقاتلوكم... فما جعل الله لكم علیهم سبیلاً»

8- A mere proposal of peace from the enemy is not enough; for assurance, cessation of fighting and the offering of peace (salām) are necessary. «فلم یقاتلوكم والقوا الیكم السلم»

9- The criteria, standards, and regulations for war and peace must be determined by Allah. «فما جعل اللَّه لكم علیهم سبیلاً»

Ayah

سَتَجِدُونَ ءَاخَرِينَ يُرِيدُونَ أَن يَأْمَنُوكُمْ وَيَأْمَنُوا۟ قَوْمَهُمْ كُلَّ مَا رُدُّوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱلْفِتْنَةِ أُرْكِسُوا۟ فِيهَا ۚ فَإِن لَّمْ يَعْتَزِلُوكُمْ وَيُلْقُوٓا۟ إِلَيْكُمُ ٱلسَّلَمَ وَيَكُفُّوٓا۟ أَيْدِيَهُمْ فَخُذُوهُمْ وَٱقْتُلُوهُمْ حَيْثُ ثَقِفْتُمُوهُمْ ۚ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكُمْ جَعَلْنَا لَكُمْ عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطَـٰنًا مُّبِينًا (۹۱)
You will find others desiring to be secure from you, and secure from their own people; yet whenever they are called back to polytheism, they relapse into it. So if they do not keep out of your way, nor offer you peace, nor keep their hands off [from fighting], then seize them and kill them wherever you confront them, and it is such against whom We have given you a clear sanction. (91)

Key Points

A group of Makkans, in order to save their lives, came to the Prophet and hypocritically declared Islam. When they returned to Mecca, they resorted to idol worship so as not to be subjected to the torture of the disbelievers. Through this method, they benefited from the interests of both groups and were safe from the dangers of both factions. Of course, their inclination towards disbelief was greater.

Messages

1- Allah informs the Muslims about the future thoughts and actions of the enemy. «ستجدون»

2- Muslims must recognize the types of their enemies and deal with each one appropriately. «ستجدون ءاخرین...»

3- Do not trust everyone's declarations. «یریدون ان یأمنوكم»

4- The goal of the hypocrites is welfare and an easy life. «یریدون ان یأمنوكم ویأمنوا قومهم»

5- Suitable environments and contexts reveal the inner characteristics of individuals. «كلّما ردّوا الى الفتنة اُركسوا فیها»

6- The compass of the hypocrites' soul points towards disbelief, and when they find a favorable context, they plunge into it. «كلّما ردّوا الى الفتنة اُركسوا فیها»

7- Severe and suppressive action is reserved for hypocrites who engage in subversive movements against the Islamic system. «لم یعتزلوكم...یكفّوا ایدیهم»

8- Recognizing hypocrites requires awareness, research, and precise information. «ثقفتموهم» is derived from thaqāfah (ثقافه), meaning culture and knowledge.

9- Muslims must gain dominance over the disbelievers in every respect. «سلطاناً مبیناً»

10- The Islamic government has the authority and freedom to suppress plotting hypocrites and purify society. «جعلنا لكم علیهم سلطاناً مبیناً»

Ayah

وَمَا كَانَ لِمُؤْمِنٍ أَن يَقْتُلَ مُؤْمِنًا إِلَّا خَطَـًٔا ۚ وَمَن قَتَلَ مُؤْمِنًا خَطَـًٔا فَتَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ مُّؤْمِنَةٍ وَدِيَةٌ مُّسَلَّمَةٌ إِلَىٰٓ أَهْلِهِۦٓ إِلَّآ أَن يَصَّدَّقُوا۟ ۚ فَإِن كَانَ مِن قَوْمٍ عَدُوٍّ لَّكُمْ وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَتَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ مُّؤْمِنَةٍ ۖ وَإِن كَانَ مِن قَوْمٍۭ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُم مِّيثَـٰقٌ فَدِيَةٌ مُّسَلَّمَةٌ إِلَىٰٓ أَهْلِهِۦ وَتَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ مُّؤْمِنَةٍ ۖ فَمَن لَّمْ يَجِدْ فَصِيَامُ شَهْرَيْنِ مُتَتَابِعَيْنِ تَوْبَةً مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًا (۹۲)
A believer may not kill another believer, unless it is by mistake. Anyone who kills a believer by mistake should set free a believing slave, and pay blood-money to his family, unless they remit it in charity. If he belongs to a people that are hostile to you but is a believer, then a believing slave is to be set free. And if he belongs to a people with whom you have a treaty, the blood-money is to be paid to his family and a believing slave is to be set free. He who cannot afford [to pay the blood-money], must fast two successive months as a penance from Allah, and Allah is all-knowing, all-wise. (92)

Key Points

A Muslim named 'Ayyash ibn Abi Rabi'ah', who had been tortured for years in Mecca by 'Harith', one day after the Muslims migrated to Medina, saw Harith in Medina and, unaware of Harith's conversion to Islam, killed him as a torturing disbeliever. The news reached the Prophet, and this Ayah was revealed.

Paying blood money (Diyah) has effects: A: It is a balm for the victim's survivors. B: It prevents people from being careless, lest they say that accidental killing has no consequence. C: It is respect for the lives of individuals and social security. D: It compensates for the economic void created by the killing.

In cases where the victim's relatives are enemies of the Muslims, blood money is not given to them, so that the financial strength of the enemies of Islam is not bolstered. Furthermore, Islam has severed the connection between the believing victim and his disbelieving family, so there is no room for compensation.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Freeing a slave is paying the right of Allah, and paying the Diyah is paying the right of the victim's guardians (Awliya'). (341)

A full Diyah (blood money) is equivalent to the average income of an ordinary person: one thousand Mithqal of gold, or one hundred camels, or two hundred cows. (342)

The Ayah does not specify the amount of Diyah, so that the need for the true interpreters of the Quran, the Sunnah of the Prophet, and the Ahl al-Bayt is felt more strongly.

The Diyah paid to the family of a Muslim victim first mentions the freeing of a slave, then the blood money, «تحریر رقبة و دیة» (freeing a neck and Diyah), but regarding disbelieving relatives, the blood money is mentioned first, then the freeing of a slave. «فدیة مسلّمة... وتحریر رقبة» (a Diyah to be handed over... and freeing a neck). Perhaps this is because non-Muslim relatives pay more attention to material things, or because paying the Diyah is more effective in maintaining and preserving the covenant.

341) Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 530. 342) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

  1. Killing an innocent person is forbidden in Islam. «وما كان لمؤمن ان یقتل»
  2. Killing is incompatible with faith. «وما كان لمؤمن ان یقتل»
  3. A believer is fallible; he must be careful. «و من قتل مؤمناً خطأ»
  4. The sanctity of a Muslim's life is such that even accidental killing cannot be easily overlooked. «و من قتل مؤمناً خطأ فتحریر»
  5. Error (mistake) is one of the criteria for lifting a duty or reducing a penalty. «فتحریر رقبة»
  6. The loss of a believer is compensated by freeing a believing human being. «تحریر رقبة مؤمنة»
  7. Freedom is a type of life. When one person's life is taken, another person must be freed in compensation. (343) «تحریر رقبة»
  8. The freeing of slaves is incorporated within the programs of Islam. «فتحریر رقبةٍ»
  9. Handing over the Diyah is not conditional upon the request of the victim's family; rather, it is the duty of the killer to pay it. «مُسلّمة الى اهله»
  10. Diyah should not be given to just anyone; it must be given to the victim's family so that the right reaches its rightful owner. «مسلّمة الى اهله»
  11. The victim's family becomes the owner of the blood money. «مسلّمة الى اهله»
  12. The determination of Diyah for the wrongdoer is a symbol of the existence of penalties in the Islamic system. «دیةٌ مسلّمة»
  13. Even in emotional states, compassion and mercy should not be forgotten, and pardoning the wrongdoer is a commendable charity (Sadaqah). «الاّ ان یصّدقوا»
  14. The value of a covenant is such that it sometimes places a disbeliever on par with a believer. Blood money must be given to a disbeliever who is under a covenant. «فَدیةٌ مسلّمة... و تحریر رقبة»
  15. Penalties must, on the one hand, benefit believers, «فتحریر رقبة مؤمنة», and on the other hand, must not strengthen the community of disbelievers. Therefore, paying Diyah and blood money to a hostile enemy is forbidden. «فان كان من قوم عدوٍّ لكم و هو مؤمن فتحریر رقبة»
  16. The condition of faith for freeing a slave is an encouragement for slaves to embrace Islam. «رقبة مؤمنة»
  17. There is no deadlock in Islam and its laws. Islamic rulings are not suspended, but they offer mitigation. «فمن لم یجد»
  18. In Islam, the penalty is commensurate with the physical and financial ability of the individual. «فمن لم یجد»
  19. Consecutive fasts are a means for purifying the soul. «صیام شهرین متتابعین»
  20. Islam relies on the spiritual dimension of individuals. Freeing a slave and paying Diyah are visible acts, but fasting is not visible, and by this means, a person penalizes his religious and moral conscience. «فصیام شهرین متتابعین»
  21. The life of a believer is extremely important, and expiations (Kaffarat) are a form of divine mitigation and favor. «توبة من الله»
  22. Even unintentional killing distances a person from Allah's grace, and paying Diyah and freeing a slave is a way to restore divine grace to the killer. «توبة من اللَّه»
  23. Divine laws are based on knowledge and wisdom, taking into account all aspects of the matter. «كان الله علیماً حكیما»

343) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Ayah

وَمَن يَقْتُلْ مُؤْمِنًا مُّتَعَمِّدًا فَجَزَآؤُهُۥ جَهَنَّمُ خَـٰلِدًا فِيهَا وَغَضِبَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَلَعَنَهُۥ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُۥ عَذَابًا عَظِيمًا (۹۳)
Should anyone kill a believer intentionally, his requital shall be hell, to remain in it [forever]; Allah shall be wrathful at him and curse him and He shall prepare for him a great punishment. (93)

Key Points

During the critical conditions of the Battle of Uhud, one Muslim killed another Muslim due to personal hostilities dating back to the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah). The Noble Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) was informed through revelation, and upon returning from Uhud, in the neighborhood of "Quba," He ordered the killer Muslim to be executed in retribution (Qisas) for the murder of the believer, disregarding his regret. (344) Islam places great importance on the life of a Muslim and the preservation of individual security, and it has prescribed the punishment of eternal torment for it (murder) in order to prevent killing and serious crimes. The expressions used in this Ayah regarding the "intentional killing of a believer" have not been used concerning any other sin. Imam Sadiq (عليه السلام) said: The repentance of one who intentionally kills a believer is retribution (Qisas). (345) Ta'ammud (intentionality) derives from amud (pillar), and since intention is the pillar of actions, an act performed with prior motivation is called intentional (amdi). The gravity of the sin of murder is explicitly stated in several narrations, including: 1. On the Day of Judgment, the killing of the innocent is the first issue about which questions will be asked. 2. The most rebellious of people is he who kills or strikes a human being. 3. In addition to the sin of murder, the sins of the victim also fall upon the killer. 4. If the inhabitants of the heavens and the earth participate in the killing of a believer, they will all be punished. 5. If the entire world were to perish, it would be easier than the killing of one innocent person. (346)

344) Maghazi, Vol. 1, p. 304, quoted from: Payambari wa Jihad, p. 327. 345) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Kafi, Vol. 7, p. 276. 346) Refer to Wasa'il, Vol. 19, page 2 onwards.

Messages

  1. In the Islamic system, no authority has the right to unjustly kill or execute others. (347) «من یقتل... متعمّداً»
  2. The value of a human being lies in his correct thoughts and beliefs. «مؤمناً»
  3. In punishment, the reckoning for conscious and malicious errors is separate. «متعمداً»
  4. Murder and bloodshed are among the major sins (Kabirah). «فجزاؤه جهنّم»
  5. Severe punishment and penalty are factors that deter corruption in society and are agents for establishing and maintaining social security. «من یقتل... فجزاؤه جهنّم»

347) But in the tyrannical system (Nizam-e Taghuti), the slogan is «انَا اُحیى و اُمیت» (I give life and cause death). (Baqarah, 258).

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِذَا ضَرَبْتُمْ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ فَتَبَيَّنُوا۟ وَلَا تَقُولُوا۟ لِمَنْ أَلْقَىٰٓ إِلَيْكُمُ ٱلسَّلَـٰمَ لَسْتَ مُؤْمِنًا تَبْتَغُونَ عَرَضَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا فَعِندَ ٱللَّهِ مَغَانِمُ كَثِيرَةٌ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ كُنتُم مِّن قَبْلُ فَمَنَّ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْكُمْ فَتَبَيَّنُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرًا (۹٤)
O you who have faith! When you issue forth in the way of Allah, try to ascertain: do not say to someone who offers you peace, ‘You are not a believer,’ seeking the transitory wares of the life of this world. Yet with Allah are plenteous gains. You too were such earlier, but Allah did you a favour. Therefore, do ascertain. Allah is indeed well aware of what you do. (94)

Key Points

After the Battle of Khaybar, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله dispatched "Usamah ibn Zayd" along with a group of Muslims toward the Jews in one of the villages of Fadak to invite them to Islam or to accept the conditions of Dhimmah (protected status). One of the Jews named "Mirdas," upon learning of the arrival of the Islamic army, placed his possessions and family under the protection of a mountain, and while testifying to the Oneness of Allah (Tawhid) and the prophethood of the Prophet, he came forward to meet the Muslims. Usamah, thinking that he was declaring Islam out of fear, killed him and took his possessions as spoils of war. The Prophet of Allah was displeased by this action, which resulted in the killing of a Muslim.

Material motivation and the acquisition of spoils in Jihad is something that Islam has condemned, and it has criticized hasty action and killing without investigation, even if done under the guise of revolutionary movement and decisiveness.

The phrase «كذلك كنتم من قبل» (Thus were you before) can refer to the fact that you yourselves initially claimed Islam outwardly, and it can also (as mentioned in Al-Mizan) refer to the fact that you yourselves fought during the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah) with material motivations and for the sake of acquiring spoils, and Islam eliminated those motivations.

It is possible that the intent of «القى الیكم السلام» (offers you peace/salutation) is the declaration of Islam and the recitation of the two testimonies (Shahadatayn), which aligns with the occasion of revelation. Or, the intent of offering Salam (peace) might be a proposal for a ceasefire, cessation of fighting, and surrender, rather than conversion to Islam. In this case, the meaning of the Ayah becomes: Do not say to someone who proposes non-aggression and expresses surrender and cessation of fighting: "You do not believe your words and you are lying," so that you may kill him and acquire spoils.[348]

Of course, it is possible that the intent of the phrase «تبیّنوا» (investigate/verify) at the end of the Ayah is an investigation into the history of the Age of Ignorance. Meaning: You fought for worldly gain before Islam, so investigate—that is, examine history—how Islam elevated you. Therefore, do not have high expectations of new Muslims.[349]

348) Tafsir Rahnama. 349) Tafsir Rahnama under the Ayah.

Messages

1- Jihad must be based on information and awareness regarding the enemy's situation and goals. «فتبیّنوا»

2- Accept those who declare Islam with open arms. «لاتقولوا... لست مؤمناً»

3- Do not misuse your power on the front lines of Jihad. «لا تقولوا... لستَ مؤمناً»

4- Labeling others as irreligious and rushing to attribute disbelief (Kufr) to suspicious individuals is forbidden. «لا تقولوا... لستَ مؤمناً»

5- We are commanded regarding outward appearances, not the inner thoughts of individuals; if we do not accept their statement, we should not immediately reject it either. «لا تقولوا... لستَ مؤمناً»

6- Not only does a claim require proof, but the rejection of a matter also requires proof. «لاتقولوا... لستَ مؤمناً»

7- Do not declare others disbelievers (Takfir) based on material motivations. «تبتغون عرض الحیاة الدنیا»

8- In wars, set aside material goals and do not violate the sanctity of Jihad by seeking spoils. «تبتغون عرض الحیاة الدنیا»

9- The allure of the world does not spare even the self-sacrificing combatants. «اذا ضربتم فى سبیل اللّه... تبتغون عرض الحیاة الدنیا»

10- The goal of Jihad is the joining of others to Islam, not the acquisition of spoils. Therefore, do not accuse others of irreligion for the sake of spoils. «لا تقولوا... لستَ مؤمناًتبتغون عرض الحیاة الدنیا»

11- The danger of changing motivations in Jihad is very serious. «تبتغون»

12- The world is transient, unstable, and fleeting. «عرض الحیاة الدینا»

13- Asceticism (Zuhd) and indifference toward the world must be the character of the believing combatant. «تبتغون عرض الحیاة الدنیا»

14- Do not proceed without investigation or recklessly; Allah will resolve material matters. «فعنداللّه مغانم كثیرة»

15- Remembering the divine rewards minimizes the world in one's view. «فعنداللَّه مغانم كثیرة»

16- We should also seek material blessings from Allah. «فعندالله مغانم»

17- Man is instinctively seeking material benefits, and this need must be met somehow. How much better it is to prevent him from falling into the abyss of meager and unlawful gains by introducing better values and directing this innate desire. «فعندالله مغانم كثیرة»

18- Worldliness is a characteristic of the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah). «كذلك كنتم من قبل»

19- Put yourselves in the place of others before judging. You were once like that too. «كذلك كنتم من قبل»

20- Do not become arrogant, for your faith is from Allah. «فمنّ الله علیكم»

21- Divine guidance is a favor (Minnah) from Allah upon us. «فمنّ الله علیكم»

22- Blessings bring responsibility. «منّ اللهّ علیكم فتبیّنوا»

23- The danger and harm of wrongful killing outweigh the possibility of hypocrisy from enemies and the loss of spoils. Those responsible for the wealth, lives, and honor of the people must not forget the principle of caution and investigation. «فتبیّنوا... فتبیّنوا»

24- Be neither naive nor gullible. Be neither malicious nor resentful. «فتبیّنوا»

25- In wartime incidents, which provide grounds and excuses for killing the innocent, deal with them with reason and patience, not with emotions, for Allah is All-Aware and All-Seeing of what you do. «ان اللّه كان بما تعملون خبیراً»

Ayah

لَّا يَسْتَوِى ٱلْقَـٰعِدُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ غَيْرُ أُو۟لِى ٱلضَّرَرِ وَٱلْمُجَـٰهِدُونَ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ بِأَمْوَٰلِهِمْ وَأَنفُسِهِمْ ۚ فَضَّلَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمُجَـٰهِدِينَ بِأَمْوَٰلِهِمْ وَأَنفُسِهِمْ عَلَى ٱلْقَـٰعِدِينَ دَرَجَةً ۚ وَكُلًّا وَعَدَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْحُسْنَىٰ ۚ وَفَضَّلَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمُجَـٰهِدِينَ عَلَى ٱلْقَـٰعِدِينَ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا (۹٥)
Not equal are those of the faithful who sit back—excepting those who suffer from some disability—and those who wage jihad in the way of Allah with their possession and their persons. Allah has graced those who wage jihad with their possessions and their persons by a degree over those who sit back; yet to each Allah has promised the best reward, and Allah has graced those who wage jihad over those who sit back with a great reward: (95)

Key Points

Those who do not go to the front lines of Jihad (struggle) fall into several groups: the faithless hypocrites (munafiqūn), fearful Muslims, those seeking comfort/luxury, the doubters, and the unmotivated (the spiritless) who say: We support the fighters with our wealth, speech, pen, and prayers, and Jihad is not an individual obligation (wājib ‘aynī) for us. This Ayah relates to this last group and criticizes them. This is because the other groups have been severely criticized in multiple instances and possess no status or rank whatsoever for the Mujahidīn (striving fighters) to be one degree higher than them. It is mentioned in narrations (Riwāyāt) that those who cannot fight due to illness but whose hearts are with the fighters are equal to them in the reward (thawāb) of Jihad.[350] The Ayah relates to a collective obligation (wājib kifā’ī). If Jihad were an individual obligation (wājib ‘aynī), those who stayed behind (al-qā‘idūn) would have been referred to as transgressors (mutakhallifūn).

350) Tafsir Safi.

Messages

1- The method of propagation and encouragement must be gradual. (First, He said: «لایستوى» [they are not equal], then He said: «درجة» [a degree], and finally: «اجراً عظیما» [a great reward]).

2- One must speak to everyone in a specific manner: The Quran deals differently with believers who did not go to the front, hypocrites, the fearful, and the comfort-seekers. It only refers to the believers who did not go to the front using the word «قاعدین» (those who stayed behind).

3- Those who have physical difficulties are exempt from Jihad. «غیر اولى الضرر» (other than those who have a disability).

4- Financial Jihad is mentioned alongside Jihad with one's life, because without financial support, the front lines become weak. «باموالهم و انفسهم» (with their wealth and their lives).

5- Voluntary contributions from the people are among the sources for funding defense budgets. «باموالهو و انفسهم» (with their wealth and their lives).

6- Wherever the risk of people's disbelief (or lack of conviction) is probable, insistence and repetition are necessary. (In this Ayah and the following Ayah, the phrases «فضل اللَّه» [Allah's favor] and «درجة» [a degree] are repeated.)

7- The services and efforts of every person are respected, and the values of others should not be ignored because some participated on the front lines. The superiority of the Mujahidīn, yes, but the rejection of others, never. «كلاً وعدالله الحسنى» (To all, Allah has promised the best reward).

8- Everyone, regardless of their position, status, or service, must know that the rank of the sincere fighters is higher than theirs. «فضّل اللّه المجاهدین» (Allah has favored the Mujahidīn).

9- If Allah has granted superiority to the fighters, then society must also make special considerations for the Mujahidīn and the martyrs in social settings, selections, and interactions. (Of course, provided that the fighter does not develop undue expectations and does not suffer a bad end). «فضّل اللّه المجاهدین» (Allah has favored the Mujahidīn).

Ayah

دَرَجَـٰتٍ مِّنْهُ وَمَغْفِرَةً وَرَحْمَةً ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا (۹٦)
ranks from Him, forgiveness, and mercy, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (96)

Messages

1- The difference between the strivers and those who stay behind is not small; rather, the distance is vast. «درجاتٍ» (degrees/ranks).

2- The condition for receiving and benefiting from Divine Mercy is purity and purification. First «مغفرة» (forgiveness) is mentioned, then «رحمة» (mercy).

3- Allah is Forgiving (Ghafur) and Merciful (Rahim), but the condition for benefiting from this forgiveness and mercy is going to the front lines and engaging in Jihad. «مغفرة و رحمة... غفوراً رحیماً»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ تَوَفَّىٰهُمُ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ ظَالِمِىٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ قَالُوا۟ فِيمَ كُنتُمْ ۖ قَالُوا۟ كُنَّا مُسْتَضْعَفِينَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ قَالُوٓا۟ أَلَمْ تَكُنْ أَرْضُ ٱللَّهِ وَٰسِعَةً فَتُهَاجِرُوا۟ فِيهَا ۚ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ مَأْوَىٰهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ ۖ وَسَآءَتْ مَصِيرًا (۹۷)
Indeed, those whom the angels take away while they are wronging themselves, they ask, ‘What state were you in?’ They reply, ‘We were oppressed in the land.’ They say, ‘Was not Allah’s earth vast enough so that you might migrate in it?’ The refuge of such shall be hell, and it is an evil destination. (97)

Key Points

Before the Battle of Badr, the disbelievers (Kuffar) of Mecca summoned the people of that city to fight the Muslims and threatened that everyone must participate, and whoever failed to comply would have his house destroyed and his property confiscated. Some Muslims who were residents of Mecca and had not yet migrated, fearing for their lives, joined the disbelievers on the battlefield against the Muslims and were killed at "Badr." The Ayah was revealed, rebuking and holding accountable those who had wronged themselves by remaining in the environment of disbelief and abandoning migration (Hijra). It is narrated in traditions (Riwayat): Whoever migrates even one step for the sake of preserving his religion is among the people of Paradise and a companion of Prophet Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله and Abraham عليه السلام. (351)

351) Tafsir Safi concerning the Ayah.

Messages

1- The angels are aware of human actions. «الملائكه ... قالوا فیم كنتم قالوا»

2- Abandoning migration (Hijra) and losing one's goal and belief is oppression against oneself. (The slogan, "If you wish not to be disgraced, conform to the crowd," is rejected by the Quran.) «ظالمى انفسهم»

3- Accountability for the sin of "abandoning migration" begins before anything else. The angels assigned to seize the soul question this first at the moment of death. «فیم كنتم»

4- Migration from an environment of disbelief is obligatory, and becoming part of the disbelievers' army (mere numbers) is forbidden (Haram). «كنا مستضعفین... الم تكن ارض اللّه واسعة»

5- If you can, change the environment; otherwise, migrate from it so that you are not held accountable. «ألم تكن ارض اللّه واسعة فَتهاجروا فیها»

6- Justification for sin is not accepted. «كنا مستضعفین ...»

7- The environment and the atmosphere created by the majority influence a person; therefore, one must break the pressure of the environment through migration. «فتهاجروا»

8- The principle is the worship of Allah, not patriotism (love of homeland). «ألم تكن ارض اللَّه واسعة فتهاجروا»

9- Migration (Hijra) is one of the remedies for intellectual and ideological weakness (Istid'af). «كنا مستضعفین... ألم تكن ارض اللَّه واسعة»

Ayahs

إِلَّا ٱلْمُسْتَضْعَفِينَ مِنَ ٱلرِّجَالِ وَٱلنِّسَآءِ وَٱلْوِلْدَٰنِ لَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ حِيلَةً وَلَا يَهْتَدُونَ سَبِيلًا (۹۸)
Except the oppressed among men, women and children, who have neither access to any means nor are guided to any way. (98)
فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ عَسَى ٱللَّهُ أَن يَعْفُوَ عَنْهُمْ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَفُوًّا غَفُورًا (۹۹)
Maybe Allah will excuse them, for Allah is all-excusing, all-forgiving. (99)

Key Points

Those who have neither a plan to repel disbelief nor a way to submit to the Truth, are considered the oppressed/powerless (mustad'af), and the obligation is lifted from them. [352] Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: Men and women whose intellect is like that of children, are considered the oppressed/powerless. [353]

352) Tafsir Safi. 353) Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 537; Ma'ani al-Akhbar, p. 201.

Messages

1- Ability is a condition for obligation. «الاّ المستضعفین...»

2- Migration is obligatory even upon women and children who are under the dominion of disbelief but have the ability to migrate. «و النساء والولدان لا یستطعیون»

3- The truly oppressed/powerless is one who does not have the ability to migrate and escape from the dominion of the disbelievers and polytheists. «لایستطیعون حیلة و لایهتدون»

4- Ignorance of religious knowledge, despite the possibility of learning, is a sin and oppression against oneself. «ظالمى انفسهم.... الاّ المستضعفین.... لا یهتدون سبیلاً»

5- Genuine excuses are accepted, not fabricated excuses and pretexts. «لایستطیعون... لایهتدون»

6- Abandoning migration is so serious that the forgiveness of such afflicted and helpless individuals is presented with "perhaps," not definitively. «عسى اللّه»

Ayah

۞ وَمَن يُهَاجِرْ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ يَجِدْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مُرَٰغَمًا كَثِيرًا وَسَعَةً ۚ وَمَن يَخْرُجْ مِنۢ بَيْتِهِۦ مُهَاجِرًا إِلَى ٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ ثُمَّ يُدْرِكْهُ ٱلْمَوْتُ فَقَدْ وَقَعَ أَجْرُهُۥ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا (۱٠٠)
Whoever migrates in the way of Allah will find many havens and plenitude in the earth. And whoever leaves his home migrating toward Allah and His Apostle, and is then overtaken by death, his reward shall certainly fall on Allah, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (100)

Key Points

One of the Muslims in Mecca fell ill and said, "Take me out of Mecca so that I may be counted among the migrants (Muhajirun)." As soon as they took him out, he died on the way. Then this Ayah was revealed. All sacred migrations, such as migration for Jihad, seeking knowledge, propagation (Tabligh), etc., are instances of this Ayah. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: "Whoever leaves his home and city seeking the knowledge (Ma'rifah) of the Imam of his Age, but his appointed time (ajal) does not grant him respite, is an instance of this Ayah."[354]

354) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 378.

Messages

1- Migration is the groundwork for relief and expansion. «من یهاجر... یجد فى الارض مراغماً» Without enduring hardship, treasure cannot be attained.

2- We are responsible for the duty, not the result. What is important is leaving the home, not reaching the destination. «و من یخرج من بیته»

3- Follow the path of Allah and the Messenger; do not worry about death or life. «ثم یدركه الموت»

4- The reward of the 'migrant' is with Allah and is greater than Paradise. «وقع اجره على اللّه»

Ayah

وَإِذَا ضَرَبْتُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُنَاحٌ أَن تَقْصُرُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ إِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَن يَفْتِنَكُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ كَانُوا۟ لَكُمْ عَدُوًّا مُّبِينًا (۱٠۱)
When you journey in the land, there is no sin upon you in shortening the prayers, if you fear that the faithless may trouble you; indeed the faithless are your manifest enemies. (101)

Key Points

Following the discussion about Jihad and migration (Hijrah), this Ayah explains the ruling regarding the prayer of the traveler. "ضرب فى الارض" (traveling through the land) is a metaphor for travel, because the traveler treads upon the earth during his journey. (355) In the Qur'an, sometimes the expression "there is no blame" (or "it is permissible") is used instead of "obligation" (wujūb), and this Ayah is one such instance. It seems that the early Muslims were so devoted to prayer that they considered shortening it a sin. The shortened prayer (Salat al-Qasr) is not exclusive to situations involving fear of the enemy. However, because fear and anxiety usually accompany travel, or because the rule of Salat al-Qasr was initially applied in situations of fear and later generalized to all travel, the restriction "ان خفتم" (if you fear) is mentioned. In narrations (Riwāyāt), the distance of travel requiring the prayer to be shortened (Qasr) is specified as a one-day journey (eight farsakhs). (356) All prayers for the traveler, except the Maghrib prayer, are two rak'ahs. (357) And the meaning of shortening the prayer is reducing the four-rak'ah prayers to two rak'ahs.

355) Mufradāt Rāghib, entry "ḍarab." 356) Tahdhīb al-Aḥkām, Vol. 3, p. 207. 357) Man Lā Yaḥḍuruhu al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, p. 279.

Messages

1- Realism and flexibility are fundamental principles in Divine laws. «تَقصروا من الصّلوة ان خفتم...»

2- Do not be heedless of the enemy even during prayer. Politics and religion, worship and prudence, are inseparable. «إن خفتم أن یفتنكم»

3- Certainty is not required in all cases. The probability of conspiracy (plot) and mischief (fitnah) is sufficient for making a decision and changing a plan. «إن خفتم أن یفتنكم»

4- Establishing prayer is obligatory even when danger is probable. «إن خفتم أن یفتنكم...»

5- Disbelief (Kufr) and faith (Iman) are in opposition to each other. «ان الكافرین كانوا لكم عدوّا»

6- The disbelievers are united in their enmity toward you. (This is indicated by the use of) «عدوّ» (singular form meaning enemy/enmity) instead of «اعداء» (plural form meaning enemies).

7- The enmity of the disbelievers toward you is deep-rooted, long-standing, and permanent. «كانوا لكم عدوا»

Ayah

وَإِذَا كُنتَ فِيهِمْ فَأَقَمْتَ لَهُمُ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ فَلْتَقُمْ طَآئِفَةٌ مِّنْهُم مَّعَكَ وَلْيَأْخُذُوٓا۟ أَسْلِحَتَهُمْ فَإِذَا سَجَدُوا۟ فَلْيَكُونُوا۟ مِن وَرَآئِكُمْ وَلْتَأْتِ طَآئِفَةٌ أُخْرَىٰ لَمْ يُصَلُّوا۟ فَلْيُصَلُّوا۟ مَعَكَ وَلْيَأْخُذُوا۟ حِذْرَهُمْ وَأَسْلِحَتَهُمْ ۗ وَدَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَوْ تَغْفُلُونَ عَنْ أَسْلِحَتِكُمْ وَأَمْتِعَتِكُمْ فَيَمِيلُونَ عَلَيْكُم مَّيْلَةً وَٰحِدَةً ۚ وَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ إِن كَانَ بِكُمْ أَذًى مِّن مَّطَرٍ أَوْ كُنتُم مَّرْضَىٰٓ أَن تَضَعُوٓا۟ أَسْلِحَتَكُمْ ۖ وَخُذُوا۟ حِذْرَكُمْ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ أَعَدَّ لِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ عَذَابًا مُّهِينًا (۱٠۲)
When you are among them, leading them in prayers, let a group of them stand with you, carrying their weapons. And when they have done the prostrations, let them withdraw to the rear, then let the other group which has not prayed come and pray with you, taking their precautions and [bearing] their weapons. The faithless are eager that you should be oblivious of your weapons and your baggage, so that they could assault you all at once. But there is no sin upon you, if you are troubled by rain or are sick, to set aside your weapons; but take your precautions. Indeed Allah has prepared for the faithless a humiliating punishment. (102)

Key Points

In the sixth year of the Hijra, the Prophet, accompanied by a group, set out for Mecca. In the region of Hudaybiyyah, they encountered Khalid ibn al-Walid and two hundred men accompanying him, who had taken up positions to prevent the Prophet's entry. After Bilal's call to prayer (Adhan) and the establishment of the congregational prayer (Salat al-Jama'ah), Khalid ibn al-Walid saw an opportunity to launch a sudden attack during the afternoon prayer (Salat al-'Asr). The Ayah was revealed, informing (the Prophet) of the conspiracy. After witnessing this miracle of revelation, Khalid embraced Islam. [358] In this prayer, the first group, after completing the first unit (rak'ah), stands up and finishes the rest of their prayer individually, while the Imam waits for the other group to reach the second unit and join the congregational prayer while armed.

358) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Prayer is not suspended even on the battlefield, and a combatant is not excused from prayer. «اقمت لهم الصلوة....و لیأخذوا اسلحتهم»

2- Such is the importance of congregational prayer that it is established on the war front against the enemy, even if only for one unit (rak'ah). «و لتأت طائفة اُخرى لم یُصلّوا فلیُصلّوا»

3- When two duties arise (Jihad and prayer), one should not be sacrificed for the other. «فلتقم... ولیأخذوا»

4- Vigilance is necessary at all times; even prayer should not make Muslims oblivious to the danger posed by the enemy. «و لیأخذوا اسلحتهم»

5- The leader is the axis of unity and worship. «كنت فیهم ... اقمت لهم الصلوة»

6- Division of labor, cooperation, and involving others in good deeds, even in the most sensitive situations, are factors that promote social harmony. In this Ayah, the two units of congregational prayer are divided between two groups so that no discrimination occurs and everyone shares in the good. «و لتأت طائفة اُخرى...»

7- Divine commands vary according to circumstances. (This Ayah relates to the Prayer of Fear (Salat al-Khawf) when facing the enemy.) «فلتقم طائفة منهم»

8- Congregational prayer on the front line is a sign of love for the goal, for Allah, for the leader, and adherence to values. «اذا كنت فیهم فاقمت لهم الصلوة»

9- On the front line, the rotation of forces must have been possible within the duration of one unit of prayer. «و لتأت طائفة اُخرى لم یُصلّوا فلیُصلّوا»

10- The longer the prayer lasts on the front line, the greater the enemy's opportunity to attack. Therefore, greater protection is required. In the first unit, having weapons is sufficient, but in the second unit, both weapons and defensive measures are needed. «ولیأخذوا اسلحتهم... ولیأخذوا حذرهم و اسلحتهم»

11- Allah informs His Prophet of the enemy's conspiracies and hidden plans. (Considering the occasion of revelation, where Walid intended to attack during the congregational prayer, and this Ayah nullified his plan.)

12- Your sincere action leads to Divine assistance at the appropriate time. (The revelation of this Ayah and the command for the Prayer of Fear was a Divine aid against that conspiracy.)

13- On the front line, acts of worship should not be prolonged, and actions that hinder vigilance are forbidden. «ودّ الذین كفروا لو تغفلون»

14- Be aware of the enemy's desires. «ودّالذین»

15- Worship should not become a means of neglecting the enemy. «تغفلون»

16- The possibility of negligence among true Muslims is weak. (The word "لو" (law) in the phrase «لو تغفلون» is an indication of this meaning.)

17- With vigilance, military and economic resources must be protected. «اسلحتكم و امتعتكم»

18- The negligence of the Islamic Ummah will lead to a sudden raid by the disbelievers. «میلة واحدة»

19- Every word, plan, or action that makes Muslims negligent is a step toward the enemy's goals and desires. «ودّ الذین كفروا لو تغفلون»

20- The criterion and justification for exemption from the front line and military service are illness and harm. «بكم أذىً... او كنتم مرضى »

21- Rain is a justification for putting down weapons only if it is accompanied by harm or distress. In harmless rain, the combatant does not put down his weapon. «بكم أذىً من مطرٍ»

22- Under no circumstances should the combatant be without protective gear. Even if he does not have a weapon, he should wear armor. «خذوا حذركم»

23- Threatening the disbelievers and giving hope and encouragement to the believers are among the methods of the Quran. «أعدّ للكافرین عذاباً مهیناً»

24- In the future, humiliation belongs to the disbelievers. «للكافرین عذاباً مهیناً»

Ayah

فَإِذَا قَضَيْتُمُ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ فَٱذْكُرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ قِيَـٰمًا وَقُعُودًا وَعَلَىٰ جُنُوبِكُمْ ۚ فَإِذَا ٱطْمَأْنَنتُمْ فَأَقِيمُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ كَانَتْ عَلَى ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ كِتَـٰبًا مَّوْقُوتًا (۱٠۳)
When you have finished the prayers, remember Allah, standing, sitting, and lying down, and when you feel secure, perform the [complete] prayers, for the prayer is indeed a timed prescription for the faithful. (103)

Key Points

The word qiyāman (قِیاماً) is the plural of qā'im, meaning standing. The word qu'ūdan (قُعوداً) is the plural of qā'id, meaning sitting. The word junūb (جُنوب) is the plural of janb, meaning side (flank), and is a metaphor for lying down. Furthermore, the word kitāban (كتاباً) means something written and obligatory, and the word mawqūt (مَوقوت) means an action with a specified time.

Regarding the Ayah "اذكروا اللّه قیاماً و قعودا و على جنوبكم" (Remember Allah standing, sitting, and on your sides), Imam Ali عليه السلام said: This means a healthy person prays standing, a sick person prays sitting, and whoever cannot pray sitting, prays lying down. [359]

Imam Baqir عليه السلام said regarding kitāban mawqūtan (كتاباً موقوتاً): meaning prescribed and obligatory. [360]

359) Wasā'il, Vol. 5, p. 488. 360) Kāfī, Vol. 3, p. 394.

Messages

  1. The deficiencies of the Fear Prayer (Salat al-Khawf) are compensated for by the remembrance of Allah. "فاذا قضیتم الصلوة فاذكروااللَّه"
  2. Prayer and the remembrance of Allah are necessary in every state, whether on the battlefield or on a sickbed. "قیاماً و قعوداً و على جنوبكم"
  3. The weapon of the Muslim combatant is prayer and the remembrance of Allah. "فاذا قضیتم الصلوة فاذكروا اللّه"
  4. The Fear Prayer is an exception. After the state of necessity is removed, the prayer must be performed normally. "فاذا اطمأننتم فاقیموا الصلوة"
  5. Prayer is among the definite Divine obligations for all ages and all generations. "كانت على المؤمنین كتابا"
  6. The obligation of every prayer is at its specific time. "موقوتاً"
  7. Specifying times for prayer is the secret to its continuity, order, and the factor for maintaining it, which itself is a lesson in discipline and a factor for bringing order to the affairs of Muslims. "كتاباً موقوتاً"

Ayah

وَلَا تَهِنُوا۟ فِى ٱبْتِغَآءِ ٱلْقَوْمِ ۖ إِن تَكُونُوا۟ تَأْلَمُونَ فَإِنَّهُمْ يَأْلَمُونَ كَمَا تَأْلَمُونَ ۖ وَتَرْجُونَ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا يَرْجُونَ ۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًا (۱٠٤)
Do not slacken in the pursuit of these people. If you are suffering, they are also suffering like you, but you expect from Allah what they do not expect, and Allah is all-knowing, all-wise. (104)

Key Points

After the Battle of Uhud, Abu Sufyan went to the top of Mount Uhud and shouted a slogan that their victory was in exchange for the defeat they suffered the previous year in the Battle of Badr. The companions of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: Our martyrs are in Paradise, and your slain are in Hellfire. They shouted the slogan: «انّ لنا العزّى و لاعزّى لكم» (We have Al-Uzza and you have no Uzza). We have the idol Uzza, but you do not. The Muslims replied: «اللّه مولانا و لامولى لكم» (Allah is our Protector, and you have no protector). Allah is our Protector (Mawla), but you have no protector. Then the disbelievers shouted a slogan in the name of the idol Hubal: «اُعلُ هُبَل» (Hubal be exalted). May the idol Hubal be exalted. The Muslims shouted in response: «اللّه اعلى و اَجلّ» (Allah is Higher and more Glorious). Allah is Higher and more Glorious. Following the revelation of this Ayah, the Prophet issued a command for general mobilization (even for the wounded), so that this movement would remove the thought of the disbelievers returning.

Messages

1- Muslims must maintain strong morale, and isolated defeats (like Uhud) should not weaken them. «ولا تهنوا»

2- Instead of a defensive stance, be in pursuit of the enemy and in an offensive state. «لاتهنوا فى ابتغاء القوم»

3- Among the factors that boost the morale of the believers are comparing their pains with the suffering of others, having hope, paying attention to Divine aid, and being aware of His knowledge. «فانهم یألمون كماتألمون»

4- Despite all the harms and bitterness of war, you are victorious because you hope for Divine favors. «ترجون من الله»

5- Muslims have various hopes from Allah: hope for unseen aid, victory, the enemy being struck with fear, spiritual rewards, and the like. (The specific object of hope is not mentioned in the Ayah, thus encompassing every hope). «ترجون من الله»

6- Hope is the greatest lever for movement and the spiritual capital of the fighter: either martyrdom and bliss, or victory. «ترجون من الله ما لا یرجون»

7- The suffering and hardship of the believers in wars are within the scope of Allah’s knowledge and wisdom, and thus He does not prevent them. «علیماً حكیماً»

Ayahs

إِنَّآ أَنزَلْنَآ إِلَيْكَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ بِٱلْحَقِّ لِتَحْكُمَ بَيْنَ ٱلنَّاسِ بِمَآ أَرَىٰكَ ٱللَّهُ ۚ وَلَا تَكُن لِّلْخَآئِنِينَ خَصِيمًا (۱٠٥)
Indeed We have sent down to you the Book with the truth, so that you may judge between the people by what Allah has shown you; do not be an advocate for the traitors, (105)
وَٱسْتَغْفِرِ ٱللَّهَ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا (۱٠٦)
and plead to Allah for forgiveness; indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (106)

Key Points

A person from a well-known tribe committed a theft. When the matter reached the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, that thief blamed the crime on another person who lived in his house. The accused attacked him with a sword and demanded proof of this claim. They calmed him down, but sent one of the tribe's orators, along with a group, to the Prophet to clear his name. The Prophet, based on the apparent facts and their testimony, acquitted the real thief and reprimanded the person who reported the theft (named Qatadah). However, Qatadah, knowing their testimony was false, was extremely upset. This Ayah was revealed and clarified Qatadah's oppression, their staging of the scene, and their false testimony. Another narration regarding its occasion of revelation is that during one of the battles, one of the Muslims stole a coat of mail (armor). When he was on the verge of exposure, he threw the armor into the house of a Jew and instructed people to say: the Jew is the thief, not so-and-so. The Prophet, based on the apparent facts, ruled to acquit that Muslim. But the revelation of this Ayah acquitted that Jew. Khasīm (خصیم) refers to someone who advocates for a claim, and a traitor (khā'in) refers to someone who claims a falsehood in the presence of a judge. [361] The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: I judge based on the apparent facts, but if the truth is not with you, do not take anything from anyone based on My judgment, for it is a piece of the Fire (of Hell). Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The rightful successors of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله must judge based on the Quran, just as the Prophet Himself did. [362]

361) Tafsir Rahnama under the Ayah. 362) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 268.

Messages

  1. The Quran is the foundation of judgment and justice among people. «انا انزلنا الیك الكتاب»
  2. The judge must have complete awareness of the law of the Quran. «انزلنا الیك الكتاب لتحكم»
  3. Learning the Quran and the Sunnah is a prerequisite for becoming a judge. «انزلنا الیك الكتاب... لتحكم... اراك اللَّه»
  4. Since the revelation of the Quran is based on truth, judgments must also be based on truth, not on partisan, group, regional, or racial affiliations. «بما اریك اللَّه»
  5. Acting with justice is necessary even towards non-Muslims. (Considering the second occasion of revelation) «لتحكم... بما اریك اللّه»
  6. Judgment is among the duties of the Prophets. Merely conveying the rulings is not enough; implementing the rulings is also a duty. «لتحكم بین النّاس»
  7. The disbelief of individuals does not justify accusing them. (Considering the occasion of revelation)
  8. The Prophet, besides the law and the Book, benefits from Divine assistance. «اراك الله»
  9. Allah showed His Prophet other programs besides the Quran. «اراك اللَّه» and He did not say: (to judge among the people by it [the Book]).
  10. Allah protects His Prophet and exposes conspiracies and staged events. «اراك اللَّه»
  11. The law is respected, but a solution must be sought to prevent misuse. Here, Divine assistance became the solution. «بما اراك اللَّه»
  12. Defending and supporting a traitor is forbidden. «و لا تكن للخائنین خصیماً»
  13. Traitors should not hope for support from religious leaders. «و لاتكن للخائنین خصیماً»
  14. Judgment is a position that requires seeking forgiveness (Istighfār), even if the person holding it is infallible. «واستغفرالله»
  15. It is among the glories of Islam that even if a Jew is wronged, the foremost person in Islam must seek forgiveness for the Muslims who intended harm. «واستغفرالله»
  16. If the Quran were fabricated by the Prophet Himself, its verses would never command the Messenger of Allah to seek forgiveness. «و استغفر اللَّه»
  17. A judge must constantly seek forgiveness to avoid following personal desires. «استغفر اللَّه»
  18. Seeking forgiveness is the means of benefiting from the forgiveness and mercy of the Lord. «غفوراً رحیماً»
  19. Allah is merciful in addition to forgiving sins. «غفوراً رحیماً»

Ayah
