وَلَا تُجَـٰدِلْ عَنِ ٱلَّذِينَ يَخْتَانُونَ أَنفُسَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ مَن كَانَ خَوَّانًا أَثِيمًا (۱٠۷)
And do not plead for those who betray themselves; indeed Allah does not like someone who is treacherous and sinful. (107)

Key Points

Oppression and betrayal against people is betrayal against oneself. This is because it entails the following negative consequences: A: It causes the loss of the spirit of sincerity and justice-seeking. B: It is a factor in depriving the oppressed who will rise up tomorrow. C: It leads to preparing divine wrath and Hellfire for oneself. D: It pollutes society, and this pollution will also affect the betrayer himself.

Messages

1- Defending a betrayer is forbidden (haram), and consenting to betrayal means participation in the crime. «لاتجادل...»

2- The Islamic ruler must be a supporter of the oppressed, not a protector of the oppressor. «و لاتجادل...»

3- Titles, powers, threats, atmosphere, or public opinion—none of these justify cooperation with a betrayer. «و لا تجادل....»

4- The leader of the community and the judges must recognize the betrayers and know their schemes so that they can take appropriate positions. «و لاتجادل»

5- One must seek forgiveness for the past (previous Ayah) and also be vigilant regarding the future. «لاتجادل...»

6- In the culture of the Quran, members of society are like the limbs of a single body, and betrayal against society is tantamount to betrayal against oneself. «یختانون انفسهم»

7- The account of minor and unintentional betrayals is separate from the betrayal committed by corrupt plotters. «یختانون، خوّان، اثیم»

8- He who cuts off his affection from the people through betrayal will also have the love of Allah cut off from him. «انّ اللّه لایحبّ»

9- The instinct to be loved exists in humans, and this instinct must be used for education and training. «انّ اللَّه لایُحبّ»

10- The love (Hubb) and hatred (Bughd) of Allah are the criteria for the choices and actions of the Messenger of Allah and the believers. «انّ اللَّه لایُحبّ...»

Ayah

يَسْتَخْفُونَ مِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ وَلَا يَسْتَخْفُونَ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ وَهُوَ مَعَهُمْ إِذْ يُبَيِّتُونَ مَا لَا يَرْضَىٰ مِنَ ٱلْقَوْلِ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ مُحِيطًا (۱٠۸)
They try to hide [their real character] from people, but they do not try to hide from Allah, though He is with them when they conspire overnight with a discourse that He does not approve of. And Allah comprehends whatever they do. (108)

Messages

1- Utilizing secret night sessions for plotting is one of the methods employed by treacherous hypocrites. «یستخفون, یبیتون»

2- The most important factor for piety is faith in Allah's presence and awareness, and His encompassing knowledge of all sayings and deeds. «هو معهم, محیطاً»

3- Allah is closely observing our deeds. «هو معهم»

4- Speech is also considered part of deeds. Although the Ayah speaks about disapproved sayings, it uses the expression «بما یعملون» (what they do/work).

Ayah

هَـٰٓأَنتُمْ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ جَـٰدَلْتُمْ عَنْهُمْ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا فَمَن يُجَـٰدِلُ ٱللَّهَ عَنْهُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ أَم مَّن يَكُونُ عَلَيْهِمْ وَكِيلًا (۱٠۹)
Aha! There you are, pleading for them in the life of this world! But who will plead for them with Allah on the Day of Resurrection, or will be their defender? (109)

Key Points

In the three verses 107 to 109, three warnings are given to three groups: To the judge, He says: Be careful not to transgress the boundary of truth (justice). "بما اراك الله" To the traitor, He says: Allah is watching your deed. "هو معهم" To the defender of the traitor, He says: Your effort will be useless on the Day of Resurrection. "فمن یجادل الله عنهم یوم القیامة"

Messages

1- Traitors should not be pleased with their current supporters, for there is a tomorrow. All support for traitors is temporary and fleeting. "فمن یجادل اللَّه عنهم یوم القیمة"

2- The court ruling is enforceable in this world, but the criminal should prepare for the reckoning of the Day of Resurrection, for on that day he will be alone. "فمن یجادل اللَّه عنهم یوم القیمة"

3- Let us not become defense attorneys for traitors, and let us not be deceived by the attorney's fee. "فمن یجادل اللَّه عنهم"

4- The oppressed who cannot obtain their rights in this world should be reassured that they have a supporter on the Day of Resurrection, and the tyrant's account will be settled. "فمن یجادل اللَّه عنهم..."

Ayah

وَمَن يَعْمَلْ سُوٓءًا أَوْ يَظْلِمْ نَفْسَهُۥ ثُمَّ يَسْتَغْفِرِ ٱللَّهَ يَجِدِ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا (۱۱٠)
Whoever commits evil or wrongs himself and then pleads to Allah for forgiveness, will find Allah all-forgiving, all-merciful. (110)

Key Points

| Alongside the previous Ayah, which threatened the betrayer, this Ayah opens the door of repentance for him. The word su' (سوء) linguistically also means causing harm to others. Thus, the Ayah addresses both injustice towards people and oppression of oneself. Just as some have considered the meaning of su' (سوء) to be minor sins and the meaning of "oppression of the self" (zulm bi al-nafs) to be major sins. [363]

363) Tafsir Rahnama.

Messages

1- Man does not have the right to oppress even himself, and sin is, in reality, oppression of oneself. «یظلم نفسه»

2- The path of return is open for wrongdoers. «یستغفر اللَّه»

3- There is no distance between the servant's seeking forgiveness (istighfar) and Divine forgiveness (maghfirah). «یستغفراللّه یجداللّه غفوراً»

4- True repentance is so sweet that man feels Divine mercy within himself. «یجدالله غفوراً رحیماً»

5- Allah not only forgives evil deeds but is also Merciful. «غفوراً رحیماً»

Ayah

وَمَن يَكْسِبْ إِثْمًا فَإِنَّمَا يَكْسِبُهُۥ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِهِۦ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًا (۱۱۱)
Whoever commits a sin, commits it only against himself; and Allah is all-knowing, all-wise. (111)

Key Points

Sin, which is transgression of the boundary of Divine law, destroys the purity of the heart, the piety of the soul, and justice, and this is the greatest loss. Furthermore, in the cosmic order and Divine traditions, the result of oppression against people reaches the oppressor himself sooner or later. «فانّما یكسبه على نفسه»

Messages

1- Every sin has a direct effect on the human spirit and soul. «یكسبه على نفسه»

2- Man is not compelled; he commits sin through his own ill choice and will. «یكسب»

3- What has a negative effect on the soul is the evil intention that leads to the sin, not merely the action without intent. «یكسب»

4- In returning the negative consequences of sin to the sinner, no error occurs, and it is a wise action. «علیماً حكیماً»

5- A wise action is conditional upon awareness and knowledge. «علیماً حكیماً»

Ayah

وَمَن يَكْسِبْ خَطِيٓـَٔةً أَوْ إِثْمًا ثُمَّ يَرْمِ بِهِۦ بَرِيٓـًٔا فَقَدِ ٱحْتَمَلَ بُهْتَـٰنًا وَإِثْمًا مُّبِينًا (۱۱۲)
But someone who commits an iniquity or sin and then accuses an innocent person of it, is indeed guilty of calumny and a flagrant sin. (112)

Key Points

Khata (خطا) means a slip or error, whether intentional or unintentional, but ithm (اِثم) is an intentional sin. Slander (Tuhmat) is one of the very great sins that causes the melting away of faith, the erasure of justice and trust in society, and leads to punishment in the Hereafter. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Slander against an innocent person is heavier than massive mountains.[364] Buhtan (بهتان) refers to a lie that leaves a person stunned upon hearing it. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Backbiting (Gheebat) is when you reveal about your religious brother something that Allah has concealed for him, but if you say something about him that is not true of him, it is Buhtan.[365]

364) Safinat al-Bihar, entry "Baht"; Kafi Vol. 2, Chapter on Slander (al-Tuhmah). 365) Wasa'il, Vol. 13, p. 286.

Messages

1- Slander is the shooting of an arrow at people's honor. «یَرم به»

2- Slander against an innocent person is forbidden, regardless of their race, school of thought, age, or circumstances. «یرم به بریئا»

3- The slanderer carries a heavy burden of sin. «فقد احتمل»[366]

366) The tanwin (nunation) in the two words buhtānan (بهتاناً) and ithman (اثماً) indicates the magnitude of the sin.

Ayah

وَلَوْلَا فَضْلُ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكَ وَرَحْمَتُهُۥ لَهَمَّت طَّآئِفَةٌ مِّنْهُمْ أَن يُضِلُّوكَ وَمَا يُضِلُّونَ إِلَّآ أَنفُسَهُمْ ۖ وَمَا يَضُرُّونَكَ مِن شَىْءٍ ۚ وَأَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْكَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَٱلْحِكْمَةَ وَعَلَّمَكَ مَا لَمْ تَكُن تَعْلَمُ ۚ وَكَانَ فَضْلُ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكَ عَظِيمًا (۱۱۳)
Were it not for Allah’s grace and His mercy on you, a group of them were bent on leading you astray; but they do not mislead anyone except themselves, and they cannot do you any harm. Allah has sent down to you the Book and wisdom, and He has taught you what you did not know, and great is Allah’s grace upon you. (113)

Key Points

It is possible that this Ayah, like Ayah 105, relates to the incident of the accusation of theft against an innocent person and the false testimony of a group regarding the theft, based on which the Prophet judged according to their witness. And it is possible that it relates to a group who came to meet the Prophet and said: We are ready to pledge allegiance to you on two conditions: first, that we do not break the idols with our own hands, and second, that we worship the idol 'Uzza for another year. The Ayah was revealed and rejected this request.

Messages

1- Enemies have plans and programs even for the Prophet and for causing Him to slip; thus, leaders of society must be vigilant. «یضلوك»

2- The decision to mislead others is, in reality, the decision to mislead oneself. «و ما یضلون الا انفسهم»

3- Allah insures His Prophet, just as He has insured the Quran (367). «و ما یضرّونك من شى ء»

4- The teaching of wisdom and the sending down of the Book are clear examples of Allah’s grace and favor, and the secret of the infallibility (Ismah) of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. «انزل اللّه علیك الكتاب و الحكمة و علّمك»

5- Allah teaches His Prophet the knowledge of the unseen (Ilm al-Ghayb) and secrets. «علمك مالم تكن تعلم»

6- One of the signs of the Prophet's truthfulness is that He (Allah) says: You had no possibility of knowing certain matters without Divine teaching. Indeed, if the Quran were the Prophet's own creation, he would never have attributed ignorance to himself. «علّمك مالم تكن تعلم»

7- Allah is the Teacher of the Prophet, thus no error or mistake occurs in the teaching. «علّمك»

8- The scope of human knowledge, even that of the Prophet of Allah, is limited. «مالم تكن تعلم»

367) انّا نحن نزّلنا الذّكر و انّا له لحافظون» حجر، ۹.

Ayah

۞ لَّا خَيْرَ فِى كَثِيرٍ مِّن نَّجْوَىٰهُمْ إِلَّا مَنْ أَمَرَ بِصَدَقَةٍ أَوْ مَعْرُوفٍ أَوْ إِصْلَـٰحٍۭ بَيْنَ ٱلنَّاسِ ۚ وَمَن يَفْعَلْ ذَٰلِكَ ٱبْتِغَآءَ مَرْضَاتِ ٱللَّهِ فَسَوْفَ نُؤْتِيهِ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا (۱۱٤)
There is no good in much of their secret talks, excepting him who enjoins charity or what is right or reconciliation between people, and whoever does that, seeking Allah’s pleasure, soon We shall give him a great reward. (114)

Key Points

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The meaning of al-ma'ruf (good deed) in the Ayah is giving a benevolent loan (Qardh al-Hasanah). [368] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Creating optimism and reconciling disputes (Islah Dhat al-Bayn), even through a lie, is a praiseworthy action. [369]

368) Kāfī, Vol. 4, p. 34. 369) Kāfī, Vol. 2, p. 341.

Messages

  1. Whispering (Najwa), except in exceptional cases, is disliked because it causes suspicion among believers. «لا خیر فى كثیر من نجویهم»

  2. When expressing an opinion, one must act fairly. We should not question all whispers. «فى كثیر من نجواهم»

  3. Sometimes reconciling people necessitates speaking secretly with each of the parties involved. «اصلاح بین الناس»

  4. Sometimes enjoining good (Amr bi al-Ma'ruf) or giving open charity causes embarrassment and obstinacy in the recipient; one must act through whispering and secrecy. «اَو معروف»

  5. The importance of people's reputation (honor) is such that disliked whispering becomes praiseworthy for the sake of preserving it, and secret charity and enjoining good overshadow the ugliness of whispering. «الا مَن امر بصدقة...»

  6. The value of deeds lies in sincerity (Ikhlas). «ابتغاء مرضات الله»

  7. Performing every hidden and confidential good deed is not necessarily a sign of sincerity; it might itself be a form of showing off. «و من یفعل ذلك ابتغاء مرضات الله»

  8. Service to society, inviting to goodness, and peaceful coexistence lead to receiving a great reward. «نؤتیه اجراً عظیما»

Ayah

وَمَن يُشَاقِقِ ٱلرَّسُولَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُ ٱلْهُدَىٰ وَيَتَّبِعْ غَيْرَ سَبِيلِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ نُوَلِّهِۦ مَا تَوَلَّىٰ وَنُصْلِهِۦ جَهَنَّمَ ۖ وَسَآءَتْ مَصِيرًا (۱۱٥)
Whoever defies the Apostle, after the guidance has become manifest to him, and follows a way other than that of the faithful, We shall abandon him to his devices and We shall make him enter hell, and it is an evil destination. (115)

Key Points

This Ayah concerns those who, after accepting Islam and the prophethood of the Prophet, knowingly oppose His commands and separate their path from the path of the Muslim community (Jama'ah). "Mushāqqat ar-Rasūl" (opposition to the Messenger) means placing yourself on one side (shiqq) and the Noble Messenger صلى الله علیه وآله on the other side (shiqq). It means engaging in obstruction and undertaking conscious opposition accompanied by enmity. Schism (shiqaq) and opposition to the Messenger of Allah have various forms, such as: denying the entire religion, believing in some parts and disbelieving in others, distortion (tahrif), justification, and legal stratagems (hīlal shar'ī). It is narrated in traditions that two people, intending to mock, addressed a lizard and called it by the title Amīr al-Mu'minīn (Commander of the Faithful). Imam Ali عليه السلام said: «دَعْهما فهو امامهما یوم القیامة» (Leave them, for it [the lizard] will be their Imam on the Day of Resurrection). Meaning: Leave those two mockers alone, for this very lizard will be their Imam on the Day of Judgment. Then He recited this phrase: «نولّه ما تولّى» [370] (We shall turn him to what he himself has turned to). Imam Ali عليه السلام states in Letter 6 of Nahj al-Balaghah: If people unanimously agree upon a person and name him as Imam, the pleasure of Allah is in that. If anyone deviates from the sphere of his obedience through slander (ta'n) or innovation (bid'ah), bring him back to it, and if he refuses to return, fight him, for he has not followed the path of the believers. [371]

370) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 371) فانْ َاجمعوا على رَجل و سَمّوه اماماً كان ذلك للّه رضا فانْ خَرج عن اَمرِهم خارج بِطَعن او بِدعَة رُدّوه الى ما خَرج منه فانْ اَبى قاتلوا على اتّباعه غَیر سبیل المؤمنین ماتولّى». Nahj al-Balaghah, Letter 6.

Messages

1- Knowledge and recognition bring responsibility, and the punishment for opponents of the Prophet occurs when they have understood the truthfulness of the Noble Prophet. «و من یشاقق الرسول من بعد ما تبیّن له... نصله جهنّم»

2- The practical conduct (Sīrah) of Muslims is authoritative only if it does not contradict the Sunnah and the path of the Messenger of Allah. «من یشاقق الرسول.... یتبع غیر سبیل المؤمنین... نصله جهنّم»

3- The Prophet of Islam and the believers are on one front, and opposition to the Islamic community is opposition to the Prophet. «یشاقق الرسول... و یتبع غیر سبیل المؤمنین»

4- Submission to the Messenger of Allah is obligatory, and opposition to Him is forbidden (Harām). «و من یشاقق الرسول»

5- Creating division (tafriqah) among the ranks of Muslims leads to Hell. «یتبع غیر سبیل المؤمنین... نصله جهنّم»

6- Allah does not cast anyone into Hell without completing the proof (Ittmām al-Hujjah) against him. «بعد ما تبین له الهدى»

7- The reckoning of the negligent (qāṣir) and the oppressed (mustaḍ'af) who do not hear the voice of truth or lack the power of discernment is separate from the reckoning of the knowledgeable and stubborn individuals. «بعدماتبیّن له الهدى»

8- The preliminaries of man's downfall are in his own hands. «من یشاقق الرسول ... نصله جهنّم»

9- Every path other than the path of the true believers leads to Hell. «یتبع غیر سبیل المؤمنین... نصله جهنّم»

10- The result of opposing the Messenger of Allah and the Islamic Ummah is coming under the guardianship (wilāyah) of other than Allah and falling into Hell. «نولّه ما تولّى»

11- Allah does not cut off His blessings from the deviants, and He assists everyone on the path that he himself has chosen. «نولّه ما تولى»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَغْفِرُ أَن يُشْرَكَ بِهِۦ وَيَغْفِرُ مَا دُونَ ذَٰلِكَ لِمَن يَشَآءُ ۚ وَمَن يُشْرِكْ بِٱللَّهِ فَقَدْ ضَلَّ ضَلَـٰلًۢا بَعِيدًا (۱۱٦)
Indeed Allah does not forgive that any partner should be ascribed to Him, but He forgives anything besides that to whomever He wishes. And whoever ascribes partners to Allah has certainly strayed into far error. (116)

Key Points

This verse, with a slight difference, appeared in verse 48 of this same Surah. Yes, repetition is necessary in guidance and education. Until the root of shirk (polytheism/association), which is a deep spiritual disease, is severed, good morals and righteous deeds are of no benefit. Repentance (Tawbah) is the cure for shirk, and the polytheist (mushrik) must exit the orbit of shirk so that divine forgiveness and mercy may encompass him. This verse is the most hope-giving verse in the Quran. [372]

372) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Opposition to the Messenger of Allah and breaking the unity of Muslims (which was mentioned in the previous verse) is a type of shirk. «و من یشاقق... یشرك به»

2- Shirk is the greatest unforgivable sin. «لایغفر ان یشرك به»

3- Man must be in a state between fear and hope regarding divine forgiveness. «لمن یشاء»

4- Shirk is the cause of misguidance and distance from every kind of perfection. «ضلالاً بعیداً»

Ayah

إِن يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ إِلَّآ إِنَـٰثًا وَإِن يَدْعُونَ إِلَّا شَيْطَـٰنًا مَّرِيدًا (۱۱۷)
They invoke none but females besides Him, and invoke none but a froward Satan, (117)

Key Points

The previous Ayah considered the polytheists to be in error and misguidance. This Ayah states the reason for that is the worship of idols and the following of Satan. The meaning of the feminine deities in the Ayah, "اناثا" (anāthan), is either the idols to which the polytheists gave feminine names such as Lāt, Manāt, and ‘Uzzā, or the angels who, according to the polytheists, were the daughters of God. Perhaps the meaning of "اناث" (anāth) in the Ayah is a weak and impressionable being, and therefore, it is not worthy of worship.

Messages

1- Non-divine deities are either weak or rebellious. «الاّاناثاً... الاّ شیطاناً مریداً»

2- Fleeing from the True Deity leaves no path except seeking refuge in false deities and Satan. «الاّاناثاً... الاّ شیطاناً مریداً»

3- All paths of deviation lead to one path—the following of Satan. «ان یدعون الا شیطاناً مریداً»

4- The worship of any deity other than Allah is, in reality, the worship of Satan. «یدعون من دونه... شیطاناً مریداً»

Ayahs

لَّعَنَهُ ٱللَّهُ ۘ وَقَالَ لَأَتَّخِذَنَّ مِنْ عِبَادِكَ نَصِيبًا مَّفْرُوضًا (۱۱۸)
whom Allah has cursed, and who said, ‘I will surely take of Your servants a settled share, (118)
وَلَأُضِلَّنَّهُمْ وَلَأُمَنِّيَنَّهُمْ وَلَـَٔامُرَنَّهُمْ فَلَيُبَتِّكُنَّ ءَاذَانَ ٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ وَلَـَٔامُرَنَّهُمْ فَلَيُغَيِّرُنَّ خَلْقَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَمَن يَتَّخِذِ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنَ وَلِيًّا مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ فَقَدْ خَسِرَ خُسْرَانًا مُّبِينًا (۱۱۹)
and I will lead them astray and give them [false] hopes, and prompt them to slit the ears of cattle, and I will prompt them to alter Allah’s creation.’ Whoever takes Satan as a guardian instead of Allah has certainly incurred a manifest loss. (119)

Key Points

The phrase "لأُمنینّهم" means the instilling of vain and baseless hopes (or desires). Slitting the ear of an animal was one of the superstitious practices of the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah), after which they considered riding, slaughtering, or any other use of that animal forbidden (Haram). The numerous emphatic statements made by Satan regarding misleading people, which are mentioned in these two verses, serve as a warning to the people of faith. The phrase "من عبادك نصیباً مفروضاً" can be interpreted in two ways: A: He will mislead some of Your servants. B: He will make moments of every servant's life Satanic. In a narration, we read that the phrase "فلیغیّرنّ خلق اللّه" also includes changing the religion of Allah. [373]

373) Tafsir Burhan and Ayyashi.

Messages

1- Committing polytheism (Shirk) is falling into the trap of Satan. «ان یدعون من دونه الاّ اناثا... لاتّخذن من عبادك»

2- The root of all Satan's wretchedness is his being cursed. «لعنه الله»

3- Satan is the ancient enemy of man; let us be vigilant so as not to fall into his trap. «لاتّخذن»

4- Satan exerts his utmost effort to mislead man. (The entirety of Ayah 119)

5- Satan cannot mislead everyone. «لاتخذن من عبادك»

6- Satan accepts the Creatorship of Allah. «عبادك»

7- The danger of temptations (Waswasah) is serious and constant; we must know our enemy. (The letter Lām prefixed to all the verbs indicates the seriousness of the danger). «لاتخذنّ...»

8- Satan misleads everyone in a different way: innovation in religion, changing creation. «لأمنّینّهم، فلیبتّكنّ، فلیغیّرنّ»

9- Lengthy hopes (or excessive ambition) are among the instigations of Satan. «لأمنّینّهم»

10- Satan first influences man's thought and soul, and then makes him submit to him. (The phrases concerning misleading and lengthy hopes are mentioned first, followed by commanding). «لأضلنّهم و لأمنینّهم و لآمرنّهم»

11- Man is inherently upon the path of truth (Fitrah). The word Idhlāl (misleading) is used where there was previously a path of truth. «لأضلنّهم»

12- Satan invites people to forbid the lawful things (Halāl) ordained by Allah. «فلیبتّكنّ اذان الانعام»

13- Hopes (or desires) are the groundwork for obedience to Satan. «لأُمنینّهم و لآمرنّهم»

14- Superstitions are among the instigations of Satan. «فلیبتّكنّ اذان الانعام»

15- Islam does not permit an animal to remain useless, let alone human beings. «فلیبتّكنّ اذان الانعام»

16- Changing creation (such as transforming a woman into a man or a man into a woman) is a forbidden (Haram) and Satanic act. «فلیُغیّرنّ خلق اللَّه»

17- Obedience to Satan is, in reality, accepting his guardianship (Wilāyah). «یتّخذ الشیطان ولیّاً»

18- Only Allah is worthy of guardianship over man. «و من یتّخذ الشیطان ولیاً من دون اللَّه فقد خسر خسراناً مبیناً»

Ayahs

يَعِدُهُمْ وَيُمَنِّيهِمْ ۖ وَمَا يَعِدُهُمُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ إِلَّا غُرُورًا (۱۲٠)
He makes them promises and gives them [false] hopes, yet Satan does not promise them anything but delusion. (120)
أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ مَأْوَىٰهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ وَلَا يَجِدُونَ عَنْهَا مَحِيصًا (۱۲۱)
The refuge of such shall be hell, and they will not find any escape from it. (121)

Key Points

Mahīs (مَحیص) is derived from hīs (حِیص), meaning to deviate or turn away. When Ayah 135 of Al Imran (regarding the forgiveness of sins by Allah) was revealed, Iblis gathered His followers with a cry and said: "With the repentance of man, all our efforts will be frustrated." Each one offered a suggestion. One of the devils said: "Whenever someone decides to repent, I will trap him with hopes and promises so that he delays the repentance." Iblis was satisfied. (374) Both Allah and Satan have made promises, but the Divine promise is true: «و لن یخلف الله وعده» (375), while the promises of Satan are nothing but lies and deception. «و ما یعدهم الشیطان الا غروراً» The sign of Satan's promises is the invitation to stinginess and corruption. In another Ayah, we read: «الشیطان یعدكم الفقر و یأمركم بالفحشاء» (376)

374) Tafsir Safi. 375) Hajj, 47. 376) Baqarah, 268.

Messages

1- Attachment to hopes (false aspirations) is falling into the trap of Satan's deception. «یعدهم و یمنّیهم»

2- Those who give false promises to others—even to children—are performing a satanic act. «و ما یعدهم...»

3- Hell is the eternal and everlasting abode for a group (of people). «مأواهم جهنّم و...»

4- Threatening punishment is one of the methods of preventing corruption. «مأواهم جهنّم»

5- One can escape all the hardships of this world, but never the punishment of the Hereafter! «لا یجدون عنها محیصاً»

6- In the Hereafter, return (to life or repentance) is impossible; therefore, let us turn away from evil before we leave this world. «لایجدون...»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ سَنُدْخِلُهُمْ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَآ أَبَدًا ۖ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقًّا ۚ وَمَنْ أَصْدَقُ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ قِيلًا (۱۲۲)
But those who have faith and do righteous deeds, We will admit them into gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them forever—a true promise of Allah, and who is truer in speech than Allah? (122)

Messages

1- Faith is inseparable from action. «آمنوا و عملوا»

2- If all deeds are righteous, they will be effective. The word «الصالحات» (al-Salihat), marked by the definite article alif and lam, signifies all good deeds.

3- Encouragement alongside warning is among the educational methods of the Quran. (The previous Ayah contained a warning; this one contains encouragement). «مأویهم حهنم... سندخلهم جنّات»

4- The Divine promise originates from His sacred, powerful, and needless Essence. «سندخلهم جنات» But the promises of others are either due to ignorance and need, or they arise from weakness and inability.

5- The pleasures of this world carry the worry of being lost, but the blessings of Paradise are eternal. «خالدین فیها»

6- By comparing the false promises of Satan and the true promises of Allah, we should attach our hearts to the Divine promise. «وعداللّه حقّاً»

Ayah

لَّيْسَ بِأَمَانِيِّكُمْ وَلَآ أَمَانِىِّ أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ ۗ مَن يَعْمَلْ سُوٓءًا يُجْزَ بِهِۦ وَلَا يَجِدْ لَهُۥ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَلِيًّا وَلَا نَصِيرًا (۱۲۳)
It will be neither after your hopes nor the hopes of the People of the Book: whoever commits evil shall be requited for it, and he will not find for himself any guardian or helper besides Allah. (123)

Key Points

Muslims prided themselves on the finality of the Prophet of Islam (Khatamiyyah) and on being the best nation (Ummah). [377] The People of the Book also boasted of their history and claimed: We will not be in the Fire except for a few numbered days. [378] This Ayah, in correcting the misconception of both groups, established "action" (amal) as the criterion. In the two preceding Ayahs, He attributed vain desires (amāniyy) to Satan; in this Ayah, He rejects false hopes. The phrase "یُجزَ به" (he will be recompensed for it) includes worldly recompense, or otherworldly recompense, or both. Narrations have identified the recompense for a believer's transgression as the afflictions and hardships of this world and the Barzakh (intermediate realm). [379] Some Muslims expected the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله to side with the Muslims in disputes between them and the People of the Book, whereas the principle is justice, not favoritism. [380] The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, in response to someone who was terrified by the phrase "من یعمل سوء یجز به" (whoever does evil will be recompensed for it), said: Physical illnesses and afflictions are a type of punishment for human deeds. [381]

377) Āl ‘Imrān, 110. 378) Al-Baqarah, 80. 379) Tafsīr Atyab al-Bayān. 380) Tafsīr al-Mīzān. 381) Mustadrak, Vol. 2, p. 60.

Messages

1- Avoid baseless fantasies and hopes, as they are ineffective. «لیس بامانیكم»

2- In every group and class, there are individuals who are overly demanding and seek privilege. «لیس بامانیكم، اهل الكتاب»

3- Act fairly in speech and judgment, and if you criticize, mention the weaknesses of all parties. «لیس بأمانیّكم و لاأمانىّ أهل الكتاب»

4- When criticizing or correcting a fault, start with yourselves. The phrase «لیس بأمانیّكم» precedes the phrase «أمانى اهل الكتاب».

5- Appropriate decisiveness and resistance against baseless demands for privilege are among the best methods of education (tarbiyah). «لیس بأمانیّكم»

6- Islam is founded upon realities, not upon fantasies or the inclinations of individuals. «لیس بامانیّكم...»

7- Allah is Just, and His reward and punishment are based on action. «من یعمل»

8- In the school of Revelation, everyone is equal before the law. «من یعمل» (Misuse and seeking privilege based on name or symbol are forbidden.)

9- The principle is action, not slogans; it is regulation (dābitah), not connection (rābitah)! «من یعمل»

10- In the execution of legal penalties (Hudūd) against a criminal, mediation and intercession (shafā'ah) are forbidden. «لایجد... ولیّاً و لانصیراً»

Ayah

وَمَن يَعْمَلْ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ مِن ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنثَىٰ وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ يَدْخُلُونَ ٱلْجَنَّةَ وَلَا يُظْلَمُونَ نَقِيرًا (۱۲٤)
And whoever does righteous deeds, whether male or female, should he be faithful—such shall enter paradise and they will not be wronged [so much as] the speck on a date-stone. (124)

Key Points

The word "Naqīr" (نقیر) is derived from pecking and refers to the hollow of a date stone, which looks as if it has been pecked. In the previous Ayah, it was "من یعمل سوء" (whoever does evil); here, it is "یعمل من الصالحات" (does righteous deeds). This means that both punishment and reward are based on "action" (عمل). In this Ayah, Paradise (al-Jannah) is mentioned, and in Ayah 97 of Sūrat An-Naḥl, in addition to that, the "pure life" (ḥayāt ṭayyibah) is stated: «من عمل صالحاً من ذكر او انثى و هو مؤمن فلنحیینه حیاة طیبة ولنجزینهم اجرهم باحسن ماكانوا یعملون»

Messages

1- The factor for entering Paradise is faith and righteous deeds, not race, claims, or mere wishes (which were discussed in the previous Ayah). «لیس بأمانیّكم... من یعمل من الصالحات...»

2- All races, colors, nations, and classes are equal in benefiting from Allah's grace. «من یعمل»

3- Have hope for Paradise by performing even a portion of righteous deeds, because perfection is infinite, and human capacity for acquisition is limited. «من الصالحات»

4- Men and women are equal in attaining spiritual perfection. «من ذكر و أنثى» This is contrary to the beliefs of some groups among the Jews and Christians. (382)

5- Faith is a condition for the acceptance of deeds. The services rendered by faithless individuals are compensated for in this world and hold no value in the Hereafter. (383) «من یعمل... و هو مؤمن»

6- The reward for the righteous believer is Paradise. «فاؤلئك یدخلون الجنّة»

7- Good deeds, even if they are few, hold value. «ولایظلمون نقیراً»

Ayah

وَمَنْ أَحْسَنُ دِينًا مِّمَّنْ أَسْلَمَ وَجْهَهُۥ لِلَّهِ وَهُوَ مُحْسِنٌ وَٱتَّبَعَ مِلَّةَ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ حَنِيفًا ۗ وَٱتَّخَذَ ٱللَّهُ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ خَلِيلًا (۱۲٥)
Who has a better religion than him who submits his will to Allah, being virtuous, and follows the creed of Abraham, a Hanif? And Allah took Abraham for a dedicated friend. (125)

Key Points

In the two preceding verses, the principle was established upon 'action' ('amal), not affiliation with Islam or Christianity. This verse prevents the assumption that all religions and beliefs are identical. It should be noted that submission (taslīm) to Allah is higher than contentment (riḍā) with His decree. This is because in contentment, the content person still considers himself someone, but in submission, a person attributes no value to himself before Allah. (384)

Ḥanīf means someone who has turned toward the Truth from a path of deviation, contrary to Janīf, which is said of someone who has turned from the Truth toward falsehood.

Khalīl is either derived from the root khullah meaning friendship, or from khallah meaning need, but the first meaning is more appropriate. According to narrations, the station of Khalīl Allāh (Friend of Allah) attained by Abraham was due to his frequent prostrations (sujūd), feeding the poor, night prayers (Salāt al-Layl), never turning away a beggar, and his hospitality. (385)

The Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله follows the religion of Abraham. "اوحینا الیك ان اتبع ملة ابراهیم حنیفاً" (386)

Abraham is Khalīl Allāh (Friend of Allah) and Muhammad is Ḥabīb Allāh (Beloved of Allah). (387)

Those who make their focus divine are the winners, because "كل شى ءهالك الا وجهه" (Everything will perish except His Face). (388) Everything except the Divine Face is transient.

384) Tafsir Atyab al-Bayān. 385) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn. 386) An-Naḥl, 123. 387) Durr al-Manthūr, Vol. 2 p. 230. 388) Al-Qaṣaṣ, 88.

Messages

1- The best religion is following the faith of Abraham. "و من احسن دیناً"

2- Submission to Allah and benevolence toward people are among the principles of the Divine religions. "اَسلم... و هو محسن"

3- The school (of thought/religion) is founded upon both faith and action, and each is incomplete without the other. "اسلم...و هو محسن"

4- The best role models for people are those whom Allah has chosen for Himself. "و اتّبع ملّة ابراهیم حنیفاً و اتّخذ اللَّه ابراهیم خلیلاً"

5- Whoever turns away from falsehood prepares himself for the station of Khalīl Allāh. "حنیفاً و اتّخذ اللَّه ابراهیم خلیلاً"

Ayah

وَلِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ مُّحِيطًا (۱۲٦)
To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, and Allah comprehends all things. (126)

Key Points

Allah has complete encompassment over all His creatures: the encompassment of might and subjugation, the encompassment of knowledge and planning, and the encompassment of creation and change.

Messages

1- Since everything belongs to Him, one must submit only to His wise Will. (Reference to the previous Ayah) «و من اسلم وجهه للّه... للّه ما فى السموات»

2- The selection of Abraham as a friend (the previous Ayah) is not due to any need on the part of Allah. Even the chosen people are His possessions. «ابراهیم خلیلاً و لله ما فى السموات...»

3- Absolute ownership, coupled with complete knowledge and encompassment over the owned, belongs exclusively to Allah. «بكل شى ء محیط»

Ayah

وَيَسْتَفْتُونَكَ فِى ٱلنِّسَآءِ ۖ قُلِ ٱللَّهُ يُفْتِيكُمْ فِيهِنَّ وَمَا يُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ فِى ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ فِى يَتَـٰمَى ٱلنِّسَآءِ ٱلَّـٰتِى لَا تُؤْتُونَهُنَّ مَا كُتِبَ لَهُنَّ وَتَرْغَبُونَ أَن تَنكِحُوهُنَّ وَٱلْمُسْتَضْعَفِينَ مِنَ ٱلْوِلْدَٰنِ وَأَن تَقُومُوا۟ لِلْيَتَـٰمَىٰ بِٱلْقِسْطِ ۚ وَمَا تَفْعَلُوا۟ مِنْ خَيْرٍ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ بِهِۦ عَلِيمًا (۱۲۷)
They seek your ruling concerning women. Say, ‘Allah gives you a ruling concerning them and what is announced to you in the Book concerning girl orphans—whom you do not give what has been prescribed for them, and yet you desire to marry them—and about the weak among children: that you should maintain the orphans with justice, and whatever good you do, indeed Allah knows it well. (127)

Messages

1- The rights of women were a subject of discussion and debate among the Muslims in the early days of Islam. «یستفتونك فى النساء»

2- The mention of defending the rights of women, children, and orphans together is a sign of the oppression they have faced throughout history. «النساء، یتامى ، الولدان»

3- Supporting the rights of women is an inviolable decree from Allah. «قل الله یفتیكم»

4- The legislation of the ruling is from Allah, and its articulation is the responsibility of the Prophet. «قل اللّه یفتیكم»

5- The traditions of the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah) must be broken by explicit religious rulings (fatwas). «قل اللّه یفتیكم»

6- Do not deprive women of inheritance due to the thoughts of the Age of Ignorance. «لاتؤتونهنّ ماكتب لهنّ»

7- Allah considers women owners and has assigned them a share of the inheritance. «كتب لهن»

8- Unwillingness to marry orphan girls is equivalent to depriving them of their inheritance. Similarly, the desire to marry them solely to seize their inheritance is a form of oppression against them. «ترغبون» (This word means both desiring and not desiring.)

9- Protecting the rights of orphans is the duty of everyone. «و ان تقوموا للیتامى»

10- The Islamic society must rise up to establish justice among the orphans. «تقوموا للیتامى بالقسط»

11- Dealing justly with the orphan is an example of a good deed (khayr). «و ما تفعلوا من خیر»

12- Service and support for the deprived will not be forgotten. «فانّ اللّه كان به علیماً»

13- Belief in Divine knowledge is the best encouragement for performing good deeds. «كان به علیما»

14- Allah’s knowledge of events precedes their realization. «تفعلوا... كان به علیما»

Ayah

وَإِنِ ٱمْرَأَةٌ خَافَتْ مِنۢ بَعْلِهَا نُشُوزًا أَوْ إِعْرَاضًا فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْهِمَآ أَن يُصْلِحَا بَيْنَهُمَا صُلْحًا ۚ وَٱلصُّلْحُ خَيْرٌ ۗ وَأُحْضِرَتِ ٱلْأَنفُسُ ٱلشُّحَّ ۚ وَإِن تُحْسِنُوا۟ وَتَتَّقُوا۟ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرًا (۱۲۸)
If a woman fears from her husband misconduct or desertion, there is no sin upon the couple if they reach a reconciliation between themselves; and reconcilement is better. The souls are prone to greed; but if you are virtuous and Godwary, Allah is indeed well aware of what you do. (128)

Key Points

Nushūz (نشوز) originates from nashz (نشز) – meaning high ground – and its meaning is rebellion/insubordination. In Ayah 34, the discussion was about the wife's nushūz ("تخافون نشوزهنّ" - if you fear their insubordination), and here the discussion is about the husband's nushūz and rebellion. Shuḥḥ (شُحّ) (stinginess/avarice) in a wife is defined as not wearing good clothes and abandoning adornment. And in a husband, it is not paying the dowry (mahriyyah) and maintenance (nafaqah) and not showing affection. Rāfi' ibn Khadīj had two wives, one old and one young. He divorced the elderly wife due to some disagreements, and before her waiting period (‘iddah) ended, he told her: If you agree to reconcile with me on the condition that you be patient if I prioritize my young wife, [I will reconcile]; otherwise, after the waiting period ends, we will separate. The wife accepted the reconciliation. Then this Ayah was revealed. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding this Ayah: This Ayah relates to a man who does not derive pleasure from his wife and intends to divorce her. The wife says: Do not divorce me; if you wish, take another wife, but keep me as well. [389]

389) كافى، ج ۶، ص ۱۴۵ ؛ وسائل، ج ۲۱، ص ۳۵۱.

Messages

1- The remedy for an event must be sought before it occurs. In family matters, a solution must be considered before the situation leads to divorce. (khāfat)

2- Waiving a right in order to achieve a higher interest and preserve the family is permissible and is better than insisting on obtaining that right. (falā junāḥ)

3- In family matters, the first stage of reform is between the couple themselves, without the interference of others. If that fails, then others should intervene. (an yuṣliḥā baynahumā)

4- A spouse's share of the other spouse's time/attention is a waivable right. It is not a ruling that cannot be changed. (lā junāḥ ‘alayhimā an yuṣliḥā)

5- The right of the family is greater than the personal right of the wife or the husband. For the sake of preserving the family, personal rights must be waived. (waṣ-ṣulḥu khayr)

6- Reconciliation is not always achieved by paying money. Sometimes waiving a right paves the way for compromise and reconciliation. And stinginess prevents it. (wa uḥḍirat al-anfusu ash-shuḥ)

7- The secret behind the instability of many families is the narrow-mindedness, stinginess, and greed that encompass the human soul. (wa uḥḍirat al-anfusu ash-shuḥ)

8- A husband's waiving of sexual desires and observing justice between two wives are examples of piety (taqwā) and excellence (iḥsān). (in tuḥsinū wa tattaqū)

9- The legal system of Islam is intertwined with its ethical system. (yuṣliḥā... tuḥsinū)

10- Whether it is waiving one's own right or showing kindness to one's spouse, all are in the presence of Allah. (fa inna Allāha kāna bimā ta‘malūna khabīrā)

11- Let the lustful man know that if, for the sake of his own gratification, he pressures his former wife and she is forced to accept a condition, Allah is aware of his actions. (kāna bimā ta‘malūna khabīrā)

Ayah

وَلَن تَسْتَطِيعُوٓا۟ أَن تَعْدِلُوا۟ بَيْنَ ٱلنِّسَآءِ وَلَوْ حَرَصْتُمْ ۖ فَلَا تَمِيلُوا۟ كُلَّ ٱلْمَيْلِ فَتَذَرُوهَا كَٱلْمُعَلَّقَةِ ۚ وَإِن تُصْلِحُوا۟ وَتَتَّقُوا۟ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا (۱۲۹)
You will not be able to be fair between wives, even if you are eager to do so. Yet do not turn away from one altogether, leaving her as if in a suspense. But if you are conciliatory and Godwary, Allah is indeed all-forgiving, all-merciful. (129)

Key Points

The divine law does not contradict human nature (Fitrah). A person has more affection for a younger wife than an older wife. Therefore, the command for justice relates only to the treatment of the two, not to the affection of the heart. Since justice of the heart is not possible, justice in action must be maintained. [390] In Islam, obligation is not beyond capacity. Man does not have the power to moderate affection, but he must at least maintain justice in his conduct. It is narrated in traditions: The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, even during his illness on the verge of passing away, would justly move His bed to the rooms of His wives, and Imam Ali عليه السلام, on a day belonging to one wife, would not even perform ablution (Wudu) in the house of another wife. [391]

390) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 558; Kafi, Vol. 5, p. 362. 391) Tafsir Namuneh and Tibyan.

Messages

1- Leaving the wife in a state of uncertainty (unresolved status) is forbidden (Haram). «فتذروها كالمعلقة»

2- Through reconciliation (Sulh) and piety (Taqwa), both past shortcomings and today's unintentional neglects are forgiven. «و ان تصلحوا و تتقوا فانّ اللَّه كان غفوراً رحیماً»

Ayah

وَإِن يَتَفَرَّقَا يُغْنِ ٱللَّهُ كُلًّا مِّن سَعَتِهِۦ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ وَٰسِعًا حَكِيمًا (۱۳٠)
But if they separate, Allah will suffice each of them out of His bounty, and Allah is all-bounteous, all-wise. (130)

Messages

1- In Islam, there is no deadlock for the family (contrary to current Christianity); if forgiveness, reconciliation, and the implementation of limits and warnings are ineffective, divorce exists. «و ان یتفرّقا...»

2- When a knot appears in life, one should not despair. Rather, the path must be changed. «وان یتفّرقا یغن الله...»

3- The first step is reform and piety (the previous Ayah); the final step is separation and divorce. «وان یتفّرقا»

4- Divorce and separation are not always bad; sometimes they are a solution. This is because family disputes may lead to suicide or illicit relations. Divorce promises the hope of forming a new life. «یغن اللّه كلاً من سعته»

5- Allah is the Guarantor of resolving difficulties. «یغن الله كلا من سعته»

6- The path to the expansion of life is different for everyone. Sometimes it is through marriage «ان یكونوا فقراء یغنهم اللّه»(392), and sometimes it is through divorce «و ان یتفّرقا یغن الله», depending on what the duty is. One should neither fear marriage, because God has promised sufficiency; nor fear divorce, because Allah has also promised abundance of provision there. «یغن اللّه»

7- Provision is in the hand of Allah. «واسعاً حكیماً»

8- The legislation of divorce is a divine favor and based on wisdom. «واسعاً حكیماً»

9- Utilizing vastness and power is valuable if accompanied by wisdom; otherwise, it is dangerous. «واسعاً حكیماً»

Ayahs

وَلِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ وَلَقَدْ وَصَّيْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ وَإِيَّاكُمْ أَنِ ٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ۚ وَإِن تَكْفُرُوا۟ فَإِنَّ لِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ غَنِيًّا حَمِيدًا (۱۳۱)
To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. We have certainly enjoined those who were given the Book before you, and you, that you should be wary of Allah. But if you are faithless, [you should know that] to Allah indeed belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, and Allah is all-sufficient, all-laudable. (131)
وَلِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ وَكِيلًا (۱۳۲)
To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, and Allah suffices as trustee. (132)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, Allah promised that if a husband and wife separate, He will enrich both of them from His vast bounty. The repetition of the phrase "و لِلّه ما فى السموات" (And to Allah belongs what is in the heavens) in these verses is to dispel the illusion that someone might imagine that Allah cannot provide sufficiency for the divorced spouses, or that the command for piety is for Allah's benefit, or that Allah is compelled to keep you. Because what is in the heavens and the earth belongs to Him, and if He wills, He will take you away and replace you with others. Ali عليه السلام said regarding the relationship between man and Allah: «غنیاً عن طاعتهم لاتضره معصیة من عصاء و لاتنفعه طاعة من اطاعه»[۳۹۳] The sin of those who sin does not harm Him, nor does the worship of the worshippers benefit Him.

393) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 193.

Messages

1- Divine power and ownership guarantee the fulfillment of His promises. «و للّه ما فى السموات»

2- The command for piety is the command of all Prophets, and the repetition of this advice in all religions is one of the important principles of education. «وصینا... من قبلكم و ایاكم»

3- The commonality of Divine commands across all religions makes acting upon them easier. «وصینا... من قبلكم و ایّاكم ان اتقوا اللَّه»

4- Fear must be reserved for Him Who owns the heavens and the earth, not for anyone else. «و لله ما فى السموات...اتقواالله»

5- Lack of piety paves the way for disbelief (kufr). «ان اتقوا اللّه و ان تكفروا»

6- Allah is Self-Sufficient (Ghani) and, possessing the ownership of the heavens and the earth, He has no need for our worship or faith. «ان تكفروا فان لله ما فى السموات»

7- Disbelief does not remove people from under the power of Allah. «ان تكفروافانّ للّه مافى السموات»

8- Since Allah is absolutely Self-Sufficient, He must therefore be praised. «غنیاً حمیداً»

9- His Self-Sufficiency is eternal. «كان اللَّه غنیّاً»

10- Allah has no need for the faith and piety of people. «ان اتقوااللَّه وان تكفروا... كان اللَّه غنیاً»

11- Rely upon Him Who owns the heavens and the earth. «كفى باللّه وكیلا»

Ayah

إِن يَشَأْ يُذْهِبْكُمْ أَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ وَيَأْتِ بِـَٔاخَرِينَ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ ذَٰلِكَ قَدِيرًا (۱۳۳)
If He wishes, He will take you away, O mankind, and bring others [in your place]; Allah has the power to do that. (133)

Key Points

When this Ayah was revealed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله placed His blessed hand on the back of Salman al-Farsi and said: That community which Allah will bring forth is from the people of Persia (Fars).[394] The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, through this statement, both prophesied the Islam of the Iranians and their services to Islam.

394) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Allah granting respite to the disbelievers is not due to His inability, but due to His wisdom. «ان یشأ یذهبكم»

2- The resources and status you possess are not permanent, but depend on the Will of Allah. Do not be arrogant. «ان یشأ یذهبكم و یأت باخرین»

Ayah

مَّن كَانَ يُرِيدُ ثَوَابَ ٱلدُّنْيَا فَعِندَ ٱللَّهِ ثَوَابُ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ سَمِيعًۢا بَصِيرًا (۱۳٤)
Whoever desires the reward of this world, [should know that] with Allah is the reward of this world and the Hereafter, and Allah is all-hearing, all-seeing. (134)

Key Points

| When man is capable of asking Allah for both this world (dunya) and the Hereafter (akhirah) and benefiting from the blessings of both realms, if he contents himself only with this world and pursues material goals in his good deeds, he is severely mistaken. One of the causes of inclination towards disbelief (kufr) and impiety is the assumption of attaining worldly gains, which this Ayah refutes.

Messages

1- The instinct for seeking profit exists in man. The Quran utilizes this instinct in the education of human beings. «من كان یرید ثواب الدنیا»

2- Islam is a comprehensive religion that desires the happiness of people in this world and the Hereafter, and their attainment of both. «فعنداللّه ثواب الدنیا و الاخرة»

3- This world and the Hereafter are all in His hands; therefore, we should broaden our perspective and free ourselves from materialism and narrow-mindedness. «فعنداللَّه ثواب الدنیا و الاخرة»

4- Even if we desire this world, we should ask Allah for it, as everything is in His hands. «فعنداللَّه ثواب الدنیا و الاخرة»

5- Believers who seek both this world and the Hereafter from Allah are the winners. (395) «فعنداللَّه ثواب الدنیا و الاخرة»

6- Divine reward is based on His knowledge and awareness of our deeds. «فعنداللَّه ثواب الدنیا و الاخرة و كان اللَّه سمیعاً بصیرا»

7- One must be a believer and sincere in reality, not just outwardly, because Allah is All-Hearing and All-Seeing. «كان اللَّه سمیعاً بصیرا»

(395) With attention to the Ayah: «ربّنا آتنا فى الدنیا حسنة و فى الاخرة حسنة» (Baqarah, 205).

Ayah

۞ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ كُونُوا۟ قَوَّٰمِينَ بِٱلْقِسْطِ شُهَدَآءَ لِلَّهِ وَلَوْ عَلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِكُمْ أَوِ ٱلْوَٰلِدَيْنِ وَٱلْأَقْرَبِينَ ۚ إِن يَكُنْ غَنِيًّا أَوْ فَقِيرًا فَٱللَّهُ أَوْلَىٰ بِهِمَا ۖ فَلَا تَتَّبِعُوا۟ ٱلْهَوَىٰٓ أَن تَعْدِلُوا۟ ۚ وَإِن تَلْوُۥٓا۟ أَوْ تُعْرِضُوا۟ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرًا (۱۳٥)
O you who have faith! Be maintainers of justice and witnesses for the sake of Allah, even if it should be against yourselves or [your] parents and near relatives, and whether it be [someone] rich or poor, for Allah has a greater right over them. So do not follow [your] desires, lest you should be unfair, and if you distort [the testimony] or disregard [it], Allah is indeed well aware of what you do. (135)

Key Points

The word talwū (تلووا) is derived from the root layy (لىّ), meaning to twist or bend. Its meaning in this Ayah is either twisting the tongue unjustly and distorting testimony, or bending the implementation of justice and causing delay in it. All the words and phrases in this Ayah are aimed at encouraging and emphasizing justice. Upholding justice and testifying truthfully, while avoiding any form of individualism, racism, or bias toward the weak, is the standard for truth and justice, not poverty or wealth, nor kinship or relatives.

Human testimony has several states: sometimes it is against the wealthy due to an anti-arrogance sentiment. Sometimes it is in favor of the wealthy due to greed for their wealth and status. Sometimes it is against the poor due to indifference toward them. Sometimes it is in favor of the poor out of sympathy for them. This Ayah invalidates all these motives and considers only the upholding of justice to be the truth.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: A believer has seven rights over another believer, the most obligatory of which is speaking the truth, even if it results in harm to himself and his relatives. [397]

397) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Establishing justice is both obligatory and a requirement of faith. «یا ایها الذین امنوا كونوا قوّامین بالقسط»

2- Upholding justice must become the habit and characteristic of the believer. The word qawwām (قوّام) means one who constantly establishes equity and justice.

3- Justice must be observed even toward non-Muslims, based on the generality of «كونوا قوّامین بالقسط».

4- Justice must be present in all aspects of life. No specific instance is mentioned regarding the establishment of justice. «قوّامین بالقسط»

5- Becoming a witness and testifying truthfully is obligatory. «كونوا... شهداء للّه»

6- The goal of giving testimony should not be the attainment of worldly objectives. «شهداء للَّه»

7- A person's confession against himself is valid. «شهداء للّه و لو على انفسكم»

8- Regulations (principles) take precedence over relationships. «ولو على انفسكم او الوالدین و الاقربین»

9- Considering the interests of oneself, relatives, and the poor must be overshadowed by the truth and the pleasure of Allah. «شهداء للَّه و لو على انفسكم...»

10- The true benefit and interest of the poor and the rich will not be secured through false testimony. «ان یكن غنیّاً او فقیراً فاللَّه اولى بهما»

11- Everyone is equal before the law. «غنیاً او فقیراً»

12- Following personal desires prevents the implementation of justice, and considering family, poverty, or wealth is a sign of following desires. «فلا تتّبعوا الهوى ان تعدلوا»

13- Giving testimony is obligatory, and concealing or distorting it is forbidden. «ان تلووا او تعرضوا»

14- Any form of obstruction, delay, or prevention of the implementation of justice is forbidden. «ان تلووا»

15- The guarantee for the implementation of justice is faith in Divine knowledge. «فان الله كان بما تعملون خبیراً»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ ءَامِنُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ وَٱلْكِتَـٰبِ ٱلَّذِى نَزَّلَ عَلَىٰ رَسُولِهِۦ وَٱلْكِتَـٰبِ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنزَلَ مِن قَبْلُ ۚ وَمَن يَكْفُرْ بِٱللَّهِ وَمَلَـٰٓئِكَتِهِۦ وَكُتُبِهِۦ وَرُسُلِهِۦ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ فَقَدْ ضَلَّ ضَلَـٰلًۢا بَعِيدًا (۱۳٦)
O you who have faith! Have faith in Allah and His Apostle and the Book that He has sent down to His Apostle and the Book He had sent down earlier. Whoever disbelieves in Allah and His angels, His Books and His apostles and the Last Day, has certainly strayed into far error. (136)

Key Points

The meaning of the Ayah is either: O believers! Take a step further and attain higher ranks, or that you remain steadfast in your faith forever and do not deviate from it even slightly. Faith possesses ranks, just as He says in other verses: «والذین اهتدوا زادهم هدى» (398) those who are guided, Allah increases their guidance, «لیزدادوا ایماناً مع ایمانهم» (399) so that they may add faith to their faith.

398) Muhammad, 17. 399) Al-Fath, 4.

Messages

1- The believer must strive to raise himself to a higher rank of faith. (400) «یا ایها الذین آمَنوا آمِنوا»

2- Heavenly religions have a single goal and truth, like the classes of a single school supervised by one principal. «الكتاب الذى نزل على رسوله والكتاب الذى انزل من قبل»

3- Belief in all Prophets and heavenly Books is necessary. «امنوا... و من یكفر... فقد ضلّ»

400) Tafsir Qummi, Vol. 1, p. 31.

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ثُمَّ كَفَرُوا۟ ثُمَّ ءَامَنُوا۟ ثُمَّ كَفَرُوا۟ ثُمَّ ٱزْدَادُوا۟ كُفْرًا لَّمْ يَكُنِ ٱللَّهُ لِيَغْفِرَ لَهُمْ وَلَا لِيَهْدِيَهُمْ سَبِيلًۢا (۱۳۷)
As for those who believe and then disbelieve, then believe [again] and then disbelieve and then increase in disbelief, Allah shall never forgive them, nor shall He guide them to any way. (137)

Key Points

In the early days of Islam, a group from the People of the Book (Ahl al-Kitab) would declare their faith in the presence of Muslims, and then become disbelievers again in order to sow doubt, claiming the reason for their disbelief was the falsehood of Islam, just as the Jewish people first believed in Moses, then became calf-worshippers, then repented and became monotheists, then disbelieved in Jesus (عليه السلام), and finally, by disbelieving in the Prophet of Islam, they added to their disbelief. A similar verse has passed previously, stating that the repentance of such people is not accepted, and they are the truly misguided ones. «انّ الّذین كفروا بعد ایمانهم ثمّ ازدادوا كفراً لن تقبل توبتهم و اولئك هم الضالّون» (401) Changing colors every day is either due to a lack of investigation when accepting the religion, or it is a conspiracy and a plot intended to destabilize the belief of the faithful. Verse 72 of Surah Aal-i-Imran also exposes this plot, stating that they believe in the morning and become disbelievers by the end of the day in order to destabilize the Muslims. Among the clear examples of this verse are individuals like "Shabath ibn Rib'i," about whom history records: He became Muslim during the time of the Prophet, apostatized after the Prophet, then repented and became a companion of Hadrat Ali. However, he later became the commander of the Khawarij and instigated the Battle of Nahrawan. He repented again and became a companion of Imam Hassan and Imam Hussain (علیهما السلام), and was among those who wrote letters inviting Imam Hussain (عليه السلام), but he betrayed Muslim in Kufa. At Karbala, he joined Yazid's army and, in gratitude for the killing of Imam Hussain (عليه السلام), he built a mosque in Kufa. (402)

401) Aal-i-Imran, 90. 402) Kitab Muslim ibn Aqil by Ayatollah Kamare'i, p. 178; Qamus al-Rijal by Tustari; Mu'jam Rijal al-Hadith by Ayatollah Khoei.

Messages

  1. The danger of apostasy and turning away from religion threatens every believer. «آمنوا ثمّ كفروا»
  2. Instability in belief leads to ultimate misguidance and the lack of Divine forgiveness. «آمنوا ثمّ كفروا... لم یكن لیغفر لهم و لالیهدیهم»

Ayahs

بَشِّرِ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقِينَ بِأَنَّ لَهُمْ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا (۱۳۸)
Inform the hypocrites that there is a painful punishment for them (138)
ٱلَّذِينَ يَتَّخِذُونَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ أَوْلِيَآءَ مِن دُونِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ۚ أَيَبْتَغُونَ عِندَهُمُ ٱلْعِزَّةَ فَإِنَّ ٱلْعِزَّةَ لِلَّهِ جَمِيعًا (۱۳۹)
—those who take the faithless for allies instead of the faithful. Do they seek honour with them? [If so,] indeed all honour belongs to Allah. (139)

Key Points

The content of this Ayah, which is a severe criticism of taking disbelievers as friends and guardians, has also been stated in Ayah 28 of Aal-i-Imran. In the early days of Islam, the hypocrites maintained relations with the Jews of Medina and the polytheists of Mecca so that if the Muslims were defeated, they would not suffer harm or lose their position. Honor belongs solely to Allah. Because the source of honor is either knowledge or power, and others are devoid of knowledge and power. In the Munajat Sha'baniyyah (Whispered Prayer of Sha'ban), we read: «الهى بیدك لا بید غیرك زیادتى و نقصى» O God! My increase or decrease is only in Your hand, not in the hand of another.

Messages

1- Seeking honor through dependence on disbelievers is a hypocritical trait. «الذین یتخذون الكافرین اولیاء...»

2- Disbelievers do not have the right to guardianship over the believers, and believers should not accept the shame of the disbelievers' dominance. «یتخذون الكافرین اولیاء»

3- In foreign policy, instead of expanding relations with disbelieving countries, we should focus on establishing relations with Islamic countries. «یتخذون الكافرین اولیاء من دون المؤمنین»

Ayah

وَقَدْ نَزَّلَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِى ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ أَنْ إِذَا سَمِعْتُمْ ءَايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ يُكْفَرُ بِهَا وَيُسْتَهْزَأُ بِهَا فَلَا تَقْعُدُوا۟ مَعَهُمْ حَتَّىٰ يَخُوضُوا۟ فِى حَدِيثٍ غَيْرِهِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّكُمْ إِذًا مِّثْلُهُمْ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ جَامِعُ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقِينَ وَٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ فِى جَهَنَّمَ جَمِيعًا (۱٤٠)
Certainly He has sent down to you in the Book that when you hear Allah’s signs being disbelieved and derided, do not sit with them until they engage in some other discourse, or else you [too] will be like them. Indeed Allah will gather the hypocrites and the faithless in hell all together (140)

Key Points

Similar to this Ayah is Ayah 68 of Surah Al-An'am, which addresses the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, saying: If you see people indulging in vain talk concerning Our verses, turn away from them until they change the subject of their discussion. «واذا رأیت یخوضون فى آیاتنا فاعرض عنهم یخوضوا فى حدیث غیره»

Messages

1- A person should not be indifferent toward the unjust statements of others. «اذا سمعتم... فلاتقعدوا معهم»

2- Complacency and silence in a gathering of sin is a sin. «یكفر بها... فلا تقعدوا»

3- Either change the corrupt environment or leave that environment. «فلاتقعدوا معهم»

4- Merely refraining from sin and being upright is not enough; one must dismantle the setting of sin and prevent the deviation of others. «فلاتقعدوا معهم حتّى یخوضوا فى حدیث غیره»

5- One must not remain silent in the face of mockery of religion under the pretext of freedom of expression, broad-mindedness, tolerance, good manners, leniency, modesty, sociability, and the like. «سمعتم... یستهزأ بها فلا تقعدوا معهم»

6- Religious zeal and fervor, taking a stand, and confronting [wrongdoing] are conditions for true faith. «فلا تقعدوا معهم»

7- Encouraging and strengthening disbelief (kufr) and mocking religion, in any form, even merely by being present (as part of the crowd), is forbidden (haram). «فلا تقعدوا معهم»

8- The condition for associating with others is that it must not cause intellectual or ideological harm. «سمعتم... یكفر بها یستهزأ بها فلا تقعدوا معهم»

9- Both maintaining relations and cutting off relations are necessary in their proper place; both attraction and repulsion are required. «فلا تقعدوا معهم»

10- Whoever is pleased with the sins of others is a partner in that sin. «انّكم اذاً مثلهم»

11- Silence in the face of the vain talk of the disbelievers is a type of hypocrisy. «ان الله جامع المنافقین»

12- Associations in the Hereafter are the recompense for associations in this world. «ان الله جامع المنافقین والكافرین فى جهنّم»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يَتَرَبَّصُونَ بِكُمْ فَإِن كَانَ لَكُمْ فَتْحٌ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ قَالُوٓا۟ أَلَمْ نَكُن مَّعَكُمْ وَإِن كَانَ لِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ نَصِيبٌ قَالُوٓا۟ أَلَمْ نَسْتَحْوِذْ عَلَيْكُمْ وَنَمْنَعْكُم مِّنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ۚ فَٱللَّهُ يَحْكُمُ بَيْنَكُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۗ وَلَن يَجْعَلَ ٱللَّهُ لِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ عَلَى ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ سَبِيلًا (۱٤۱)
—those who lie in wait for you: if there is a victory for you from Allah, they say, ‘Were we not with you?’ But if the faithless get a share [of victory], they say, ‘Did we not prevail upon you and defend you against the faithful?’ Allah will judge between you on the Day of Resurrection, and Allah will never provide the faithless any way [to prevail] over the faithful. (141)

Key Points

The Quran refers to the victory of the Muslims using the word fath (فتح) and the success of the disbelievers using the word nasīb (نصیب), to make it understood that the prosperity of disbelief is fleeting, and the good outcome and true victory belong to the truth (Haqq). Nastaḥwidh (نَستَحوذ) is derived from the root ḥawdh (حوذ), meaning the back of the thigh. This is because the camel driver strikes that area to drive and move the camel. Here, the intent is to encourage the disbelievers to fight against the Muslims. Jurists (Fuqahā) rely on the phrase lan yaj‘al Allāh (لن یجعل الله) in various jurisprudential issues to prove the lack of dominance of the disbelievers over the believers. Of course, travel, gaining information, receiving education, and cultural and economic exchange are permissible, provided they do not lead to the dominance of the disbelievers and the humiliation of the believers. Because we read in narrations: Seek knowledge, even if it means traveling to the country of China. And the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: I will free any captive disbeliever who teaches ten Muslims. And during the time of the Infallibles علیهم السلام, transactions took place between Muslims and others.

Messages

1- Hypocrites are opportunists and exploit every incident for their own benefit. «یتربّصون بكم...»

2- Hypocrites are spies for the disbelievers and encourage them to fight the Muslims. «نستحوذ»

3- On the Day of Resurrection, through Allah’s just judgment, all deceits and hypocrisies will be revealed. «فالله یحكم بینكم یوم القیامة»

4- Believers do not have the right to accept the dominance of the disbelievers, and accepting dominance is a sign of lacking true faith. «لن یجعل الله للكافرین على المؤمنین سبیلا»

5- Actions must be taken so that the disbelievers are permanently despairing of dominating the believers. «لن یجعل الله...»

6- Any plan, treaty, interaction, or contract that opens the way for the disbelievers to infiltrate the Muslims is forbidden (ḥarām). Therefore, Muslims must enjoy complete independence in all political, military, economic, and cultural aspects. «لن یجعل الله للكافرین...»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقِينَ يُخَـٰدِعُونَ ٱللَّهَ وَهُوَ خَـٰدِعُهُمْ وَإِذَا قَامُوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ قَامُوا۟ كُسَالَىٰ يُرَآءُونَ ٱلنَّاسَ وَلَا يَذْكُرُونَ ٱللَّهَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا (۱٤۲)
The hypocrites indeed seek to deceive Allah, but it is He who outwits them. When they stand up for prayer, they stand up lazily, showing off to the people and not remembering Allah except a little, (142)

Key Points

A hypocrite (munafiq) is someone who outwardly performs religious duties, but his motivations are not divine. He performs the prayer (Salat), but out of hypocrisy, laziness, and heedlessness. He imagines that by this method he is deceiving Allah, while he does not know that he has deceived himself, and Allah is aware of his inner self and will give him the recompense for his deception. Imam Reza عليه السلام says: Since Allah gives them the recompense for their deceit (khid'ah), this divine punishment is called "deceit" (khid'ah).[403] The desired prayer is loving, sincere, and continuous. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: Those who delay the prayer until the end of its time and perform it hastily are performing the prayer of the hypocrites.[404] The Quran uses the phrase «اقامواالصلوة» (they established the prayer) regarding the believers, but concerning the hypocrites, it uses the phrase «قاموا الى الصلوة» (they stood up for the prayer). The difference between «اقاموا» and «قاموا» is the difference between establishing (it) and merely standing up (for it).

403) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 404) Durr al-Manthur, Vol. 2, p. 237.

Messages

1- Deception, lack of enthusiasm in prayer, hypocrisy, and heedlessness of the remembrance of Allah are signs of hypocrisy. «ان المنافقین یخادعون، یراؤون، لا یذكرون»

2- Hypocrites must know that they are dealing with Allah. «و هو خادعهم»

3- Allah's punishment is commensurate with the deed. «یخادعون الله و هو خادعهم»

4- The prayer of the hypocrites lacks spirit, motivation, and quantity. «كسالى، یراؤون، قلیلاً»

Ayah

مُّذَبْذَبِينَ بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ لَآ إِلَىٰ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ وَلَآ إِلَىٰ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ ۚ وَمَن يُضْلِلِ ٱللَّهُ فَلَن تَجِدَ لَهُۥ سَبِيلًا (۱٤۳)
wavering in between: neither with these, nor with those. And whomever Allah leads astray, you will never find any way for him. (143)

Key Points

The term «تَذَبذُب» (tadhbadhub) refers to the movement of something suspended in the air, which has no firm or stable position or base on the ground.

Messages

1- Hypocrites lack independence of thought and belief; they turn with every movement, are dependent on others, and wander aimlessly. «مذبذبین بین ذلك»

2- The hypocrite lacks peace. This is because he constantly has to adopt a new position and make immediate and hasty decisions. «مذبذبین بین ذلك»

3- Decisiveness is necessary in matters of belief and intellectual insight. «مذبذبین بین ذلك»

4- The hypocrite is subject to Allah's wrath. «من یضلل الله»

5- Hypocrisy is an incurable ailment. «فلن تجدله سبیلاً»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَتَّخِذُوا۟ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ أَوْلِيَآءَ مِن دُونِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ۚ أَتُرِيدُونَ أَن تَجْعَلُوا۟ لِلَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ سُلْطَـٰنًا مُّبِينًا (۱٤٤)
O you who have faith! Do not take the faithless for friends instead of the faithful. Do you wish to give Allah a clear sanction against yourselves? (144)

Key Points

Believers do not have the right to accept the guardianship (wilayah) of the disbelievers, but the hypocrites have a close relationship with the disbelievers. The Quran introduces the disbelievers as the devils (shayatin) of the hypocrites «خلوا الى شیاطینهم»(405) and the brothers of the hypocrites «الذین نافقوا یقولون لاخوانهم الذین كفروا»(406). The expressions of the Quran in verses 139 and 141 clarified the nature of their relationship with the disbelievers.

405) Al-Baqarah, 17. 406) Al-Hashr, 11.

Messages

1- Accepting the guardianship (wilayah) of the disbelievers is incompatible with faith. One heart cannot hold two loves. «یا ایها الّذین آمنوا لاتتّخذوا...»

2- Tawalli and Tabarri (loving the believers and disassociating from the disbelievers) are prerequisites of faith. «لاتتّخذوا الكافرین اولیاء من دون المؤمنین»

3- Every type of mixing, friendship, and contract that results in harm to the Muslims must be avoided. «لاتتّخذوا الكافرین اولیاء»

4- In foreign policy, political and economic relations, selections, and appointments/dismissals, any action that leads to the domination of the disbelievers over the Muslims is forbidden (haram) and condemned. «لاتتّخذوا الكافرین اولیاء»

5- A Muslim who accepts humiliation has no answer before Allah. «اتریدون ان تجعلوالله ...»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلْمُنَـٰفِقِينَ فِى ٱلدَّرْكِ ٱلْأَسْفَلِ مِنَ ٱلنَّارِ وَلَن تَجِدَ لَهُمْ نَصِيرًا (۱٤٥)
Indeed the hypocrites will be in the lowest reach of the Fire, and you will never find any helper for them, (145)

Key Points

Darak (دَرك) is the deepest point of the sea. A matter whose depth is understood is also said to be idrāk (understood/perceived). Steps that go downward are called darak, and steps that go upward are called darajah (degree). Just as Paradise has darajāt (degrees), Hell also has darakāt (depths/levels).

Messages

1- The position of the hypocrites in Hell is worse than that of the disbelievers (kuffār). «فى الدرك الاسفل»

2- Hell has ranks and levels. «فى الدرك الاسفل»

3- On the Day of Judgment, there is no way for the hypocrites to be saved. «لن تجدلهم نصیراً»

4- Intercession (shafā'ah) does not include the hypocrites. «لن تجدلهم نصیراً»

Ayah

إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ تَابُوا۟ وَأَصْلَحُوا۟ وَٱعْتَصَمُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ وَأَخْلَصُوا۟ دِينَهُمْ لِلَّهِ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ مَعَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ۖ وَسَوْفَ يُؤْتِ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا (۱٤٦)
except for those who repent and reform, and hold fast to Allah and dedicate their religion [exclusively] to Allah. Those are with the faithful, and soon Allah will give the faithful a great reward. (146)

Messages

1- The path of repentance (Tawbah) is open to everyone, even the hypocrites, and it raises them from the "lowest abyss" (al-dark al-asfal) to the highest paradise. «الاّ الّذین تابوا»

2- Man is free and can consciously change direction. «تابوا»

3- Alongside the severest threats, give people hope as well. «فى الدرك الاسفل... الا الذین تابوا»

4- Repentance is not merely an expression of regret; rather, it is comprehensive reconstruction. «اصلحوا، اعتصموا، اخلصوا»

5- The repentance for every sin takes a specific form. The repentance for hypocrisy is reform instead of destruction, holding fast to Allah instead of dependence on this or that person, and sincerity instead of impurity. «اصلحوا و اعتصموا و اخلصوا»

6- Syncretism (eclecticism) in beliefs and thoughts is forbidden. «اخلصوا دینهم لله»

7- Repentant hypocrites should not feel alienated when separating from their former associates, because they will find better friends. «فاولئك مع المؤمنین»

8- Believers should welcome true penitents and consider them as part of themselves. «فاولئك مع المؤمنین»

Ayah

مَّا يَفْعَلُ ٱللَّهُ بِعَذَابِكُمْ إِن شَكَرْتُمْ وَءَامَنتُمْ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ شَاكِرًا عَلِيمًا (۱٤۷)
Why should Allah punish you if you give thanks and be faithful? And Allah is appreciative, all-knowing. (147)

Messages

1- Divine punishments are based on His justice, not on revenge or a display of power. The cause of the torment is humanity itself. «مایفعل الله بعذابكم ان شكرتم» 2- Recognizing His blessings and being grateful for them is the groundwork for accepting the invitation and having faith. «شكرتم و آمنتم» 3- Faith in Allah is the most prominent manifestation of gratitude. «ان شكرتم و آمنتم» 4- Gratitude is both a means of salvation from wrath and a cause for receiving greater favor. «مایفعل اللّه بعذابكم، كان الله شاكراً» 5- If you are grateful, Allah is also grateful towards you. «كان الله شاكراً» 6- Although Allah is Self-Sufficient, He thanks His small servant. Why should we not render thanks to Him?! «ان شكرتم، كان الله شاكراً»

Ayah

۞ لَّا يُحِبُّ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْجَهْرَ بِٱلسُّوٓءِ مِنَ ٱلْقَوْلِ إِلَّا مَن ظُلِمَ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ سَمِيعًا عَلِيمًا (۱٤۸)
Allah does not like the disclosure of [anyone’s] evil [conduct] in speech except by someone who has been wronged, and Allah is all-hearing, all-knowing. (148)

Key Points

This Ayah grants the oppressed permission to seek justice and cry out, similar to Ayah 41 of Surah Ash-Shura, which states: «و لِمَن انتَصر بعد ظلمِه فَاولئك ما عَلیهم مِن سبیل» (And whoever defends himself after being wronged—for them there is no cause for blame). And whoever seeks help and takes revenge after being wronged, there is no path of reproach upon him.

The Qur'an has repeatedly forbidden the disclosure of evil deeds, considering it among the major sins and promising punishment for it. This includes Ayah 19 of Surah An-Noor, which considers the desire to spread the evil deeds of believers a sin: «انّ الذین یحبّون ان تشیع الفاحشة فى الّذین آمنوالهم عذاب الیم فى الدنیا والاخرة» (Indeed, those who love that immorality should be spread among those who have believed will have a painful punishment in this world and the Hereafter).

There are several possibilities regarding the interpretation of the phrase «من ظُلم»: 1. It means «ممن ظلم» (from one who was wronged), meaning the outcry of the person who has been oppressed is permissible. 2. It means «لمن ظلم» (for one who was wronged), meaning crying out in favor of the oppressed, even if the speaker himself is not oppressed. 3. It means «على من ظلم» (against one who was wronged), meaning an outcry of protest against an oppressed person who remains silent and accepts humiliation. Of course, the first possibility is the closest interpretation.

What is forbidden is the disclosure of people's faults in front of others, not the recounting of individuals' faults to the individuals themselves. This is because the Prophet of Allah has said: The believer is the mirror of the believer. (407)

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding this Ayah: A person who hosts guests but treats them inappropriately is among those who have committed oppression, and there is no blame upon the guest if they say something about him. (408)

407) Bihar, Vol. 74, p. 233. 408) Wasā’il, Vol. 12, p. 289.

Messages

1- The general and fundamental rule is the prohibition of disclosure and the mention of people's faults, except in specific cases. «لایحبّ اللّه الجهر بالسوء»

2- The disclosure of people's faults is forbidden, regardless of the method (through poetry, satire, explicit statement, implication, narration, complaint, etc.). «لایحب الله الجهر بالسوء من القول»

3- A characteristic of an Islamic society is that the oppressed person can cry out against the oppressor with complete freedom. «الجهر بالسوء من القول الا من ظُلم»

4- Backbiting (Gheebah) by the oppressed against the oppressor is permissible. «الجهر بالسوء من القول الا من ظُلم»

5- Islam is the protector of the wronged. «الاّ من ظُلم»

6- Misuse of the law is forbidden. Oppressors should know that the prohibition of backbiting does not open the door to oppression for them. «الا من ظُلم»

7- Only the oppressed person is permitted to backbite the oppressor, and only concerning the oppression committed, not his other faults. (409) «الاّ من ظُلم»

8- The oppressor has no respect in an Islamic society; he must be condemned and introduced to the people. «لایحبّ الله الجهر بالسوء... الا من ظُلم»

9- When human values conflict, priorities (the more important over the important) must be observed. The value of defending the oppressed outweighs the value of modesty and silence. «لایحبّ الله الجهربالسوء... الاّ من ظُلم»

10- In cases where the disclosure of faults is permissible, do not transgress the bounds of truth, for Allah is Hearing and Knowing. «سمیعاً علیماً»

409) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Ayah

إِن تُبْدُوا۟ خَيْرًا أَوْ تُخْفُوهُ أَوْ تَعْفُوا۟ عَن سُوٓءٍ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ كَانَ عَفُوًّا قَدِيرًا (۱٤۹)
Whether you disclose a good [deed that you do] or hide it, or excuse an evil [deed], Allah is indeed all-excusing, all-powerful. (149)

Key Points

Wherever the oppressed person gains a position of power, and forgiving the oppressor has an educational effect, forgiveness must be granted. And wherever silence leads to the humiliation of the oppressed and the strengthening of oppression, one must cry out. Vengeance and retribution (Qisas) are a "right" (Haqq), while pardon and forgiveness are a "favor" (Fadl). According to the statement of Imam Ali عليه السلام, forgiving the enemy is gratitude for having overcome him. «اذا قدرت على عدوّك فاجعل العفو عنه شكراً للقدرة علیه»[410]

410) Nahj al-Balaghah, Qisar 10.

Messages

1- One must be encouraging towards good deeds and forgiving towards bad deeds. «ان تبدوا خیراً... او تعفوا عن سوء»

2- Sometimes fighting oppression and crying out is valuable, and sometimes pardon and forgiveness are valuable. «تعفوا عن سوء»

3- Forgiveness granted from a position of power is valuable. «عفوّاً قدیراً»

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَكْفُرُونَ بِٱللَّهِ وَرُسُلِهِۦ وَيُرِيدُونَ أَن يُفَرِّقُوا۟ بَيْنَ ٱللَّهِ وَرُسُلِهِۦ وَيَقُولُونَ نُؤْمِنُ بِبَعْضٍ وَنَكْفُرُ بِبَعْضٍ وَيُرِيدُونَ أَن يَتَّخِذُوا۟ بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ سَبِيلًا (۱٥٠)
Those who disbelieve in Allah and His apostles and seek to separate Allah from His apostles, and say, ‘We believe in some and disbelieve in some’ and seek to take a way in between (150)
أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ حَقًّا ۚ وَأَعْتَدْنَا لِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ عَذَابًا مُّهِينًا (۱٥۱)
—it is they who are truly faithless, and We have prepared for the faithless a humiliating punishment. (151)

Key Points

Ayah 150 serves as the subject (Mubtada) and Ayah 151 serves as its predicate (Khabar). Jews and Christians, due to whims, ignorant prejudices, and baseless narrow-mindedness, accepted some prophets while disbelieving in others.

Messages

1- Prophethood is a continuous divine tradition and flow, like an interconnected chain. Therefore, one must believe in this entire flow. (411) «یریدون ان یفرّقوا بین اللّه و رسله»

2- God (Allah) and the Messenger constitute a single front, and there is no separation between them. «یفرّقوا بین اللّه ورسله»

3- Disbelief in Allah and His Messengers, or belief in Allah coupled with disbelief in all prophets, or belief in Allah coupled with disbelief in some prophets, is forbidden. The path of truth is belief in Allah and all the prophets. «یكفرون، یقرّقوا، یقومون، هم الكافرون حقّا»

4- All heavenly religions must be recognized (of course, within their historical boundaries). «یقولون نؤمن ببعض و نكفر ببعض»

5- Accepting laws contrary to Islam is a form of discrimination in religion and is forbidden. «نؤمن ببعض و نكفر ببعض»

6- Syncretism (Al-Iltiqat) and opening a new path contrary to the path of the prophets is disbelief (Kufr). «یتخذوا بین ذلك سبیلاً اولئك هم الكافرون»

7- Hell (Jahannam) is already existent and created. «اعتدنا»

(411) Regarding the 'Imamate' as well, belief in all the Imams is necessary. Accepting some and denying others, or stopping (at a certain point), is tantamount to disbelief in all the Imams.

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ وَرُسُلِهِۦ وَلَمْ يُفَرِّقُوا۟ بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِّنْهُمْ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ سَوْفَ يُؤْتِيهِمْ أُجُورَهُمْ ۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا (۱٥۲)
But those who have faith in Allah and His apostles and make no distinction between any of them—them He will soon give their rewards, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (152)

Messages

1- One must believe in Allah and all His messengers. Although the ranks of the prophets and the scope of their missions differ, belief in all of them is necessary. «آمنوا باللّه و رسله»

2- Although our existence, our successes, and all our means and tools are from Him, He still rewards us. «اجورهم»

3- In His reward, Allah does not take into account our shortcomings and faults. «یؤتیهم اجورهم و كان اللّه غفوراً رحیما»

Ayah
