يَسْـَٔلُكَ أَهْلُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ أَن تُنَزِّلَ عَلَيْهِمْ كِتَـٰبًا مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ ۚ فَقَدْ سَأَلُوا۟ مُوسَىٰٓ أَكْبَرَ مِن ذَٰلِكَ فَقَالُوٓا۟ أَرِنَا ٱللَّهَ جَهْرَةً فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ ٱلصَّـٰعِقَةُ بِظُلْمِهِمْ ۚ ثُمَّ ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ ٱلْعِجْلَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا جَآءَتْهُمُ ٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتُ فَعَفَوْنَا عَن ذَٰلِكَ ۚ وَءَاتَيْنَا مُوسَىٰ سُلْطَـٰنًا مُّبِينًا (۱٥۳)
The People of the Book ask you to bring down for them a Book from the sky. Certainly they asked Moses for [something] greater than that, for they said, ‘Show us Allah visibly,’ whereat a thunderbolt seized them for their wrongdoing. Then they took up the Calf [for worship], after all the manifest proofs that had come to them. Yet We excused that, and We gave Moses a manifest authority. (153)

Key Points

The demand of the Jews from the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was that the Qur'an should be revealed all at once, like the Torah, but this was nothing more than an excuse. Allah says in response to them: Even if We were to send down the Qur'an all at once in a tangible scroll, the disbelievers would still consider it magic, «ولو نزّلنا علیك كتاباً فى قِرطاس فلمسوه بایدیهم لقال الّذین كفروا انْ هذا الاّ سحرٌ مبین»[412] The excuses of the disbelievers for not believing are similar. The polytheists also made the same request to the Messenger of Allah, saying: «لن نؤمن لرقیّك حتّى تنزّل علینا كتاباً نقرؤه»[413]

412) Al-An'am (The Cattle), 7. 413) Al-Isra (The Night Journey), 93.

Messages

1- The undesirable deeds, thoughts, and morals of ancestors become a negative precedent for future generations. «فقد سألوا...»

2- The Children of Israel were not seeking the truth. Otherwise, after all those miracles, why did they worship the calf? «اتخذوا العجل من بعد ما جائتهم البینات»

3- Do not be worried or distressed by the disbelievers' lack of faith, because stubborn individuals have existed against all the prophets. «سألوا موسى اكبر من ذلك»

4- Allah cannot be seen with the eye, and requesting to see Him in this manner is oppression and tyranny. «ارنا اللّه جهرة فاخذتهم الصاعقة بظلمهم»

5- Denying the truth and intellectual deviation lead to Allah's wrath in this very world. «اخذتهم الصاعقة بظلمهم»

6- Divine mercy forgives even the sin of calf-worship. «فعفونا»

7- The prophets are supported by the Lord. «و آتینا موسى سلطاناً مبیناً»

Ayah

وَرَفَعْنَا فَوْقَهُمُ ٱلطُّورَ بِمِيثَـٰقِهِمْ وَقُلْنَا لَهُمُ ٱدْخُلُوا۟ ٱلْبَابَ سُجَّدًا وَقُلْنَا لَهُمْ لَا تَعْدُوا۟ فِى ٱلسَّبْتِ وَأَخَذْنَا مِنْهُم مِّيثَـٰقًا غَلِيظًا (۱٥٤)
And We raised the Mount above them for the sake of their covenant, and We said to them, ‘Enter the gate prostrating’ and We said to them, ‘Do not violate the Sabbath,’ and We took from them a solemn covenant. (154)

Key Points

Similar to this Ayah, it appears in Surah Al-Baqarah, Ayahs 63 and 93, and also in Al-A'raf, Ayah 171. The intent of Allah's covenant with the Children of Israel is what is mentioned in Surah Al-Baqarah, Ayahs 40 and 84, and Al-Ma'idah, Ayah 12. Sacred places have specific etiquette: "ادخلوا الباب سجّدا" (Enter the gate prostrating). Regarding Mount Tur, it is mentioned in the Quran: "فاخلع نعلیك"[414] (Remove your sandals). Regarding the houses of the Prophet: "لاتدخلوا بیوت النبى حتّى یؤذن لكم"[415] (Do not enter the houses of the Prophet unless permission is given to you). Regarding mosques: "خذوا زینتكم عند كل مسجد"[416] (Take your adornment at every place of prostration/mosque). Regarding Al-Masjid Al-Haram: "فلایقربوا المسجد الحرام"[417] (So let them not approach Al-Masjid Al-Haram). Regarding the Ka'bah: "طهّرا بیتى"[418] (Purify My House). And other etiquette concerning mosques mentioned in jurisprudential books and Hadith sources.

414) Taha, 12. 415) Al-Ahzab, 53. 416) Al-A'raf, 31. 417) At-Tawbah, 28. 418) Al-Baqarah, 125.

Messages

1- In education, it is sometimes necessary to use the leverage of fear and intimidation. "و رفعنا فوقهم الطور"

2- Engaging in work during a holiday or time of worship is a form of transgression beyond the limits. "لاتعدوا فى السّبت"

Ayah

فَبِمَا نَقْضِهِم مِّيثَـٰقَهُمْ وَكُفْرِهِم بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ وَقَتْلِهِمُ ٱلْأَنۢبِيَآءَ بِغَيْرِ حَقٍّ وَقَوْلِهِمْ قُلُوبُنَا غُلْفٌۢ ۚ بَلْ طَبَعَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهَا بِكُفْرِهِمْ فَلَا يُؤْمِنُونَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا (۱٥٥)
Then because of their breaking their covenant, their defiance of Allah’s signs, their killing of the prophets unjustly and for their saying, ‘Our hearts are uncircumcised’… Indeed, Allah has set a seal on them for their unfaith, so they do not have faith except a few. (155)

Key Points

The Children of Israel (Bani Israel) during the time of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله had not killed the prophets, but their fathers and ancestors had committed such a crime. However, because the descendants were pleased with the actions of their ancestors, the crimes have also been attributed to them. [419]

419) Tafsir Qummi, Vol. 1, p. 157.

Messages

1- Breaking the covenant is a sin on par with disbelief (Kufr), or rather, it paves the way for it. «نقضهم میثاقهم و كفرهم»

2- Ingratitude for blessings reaches the point where those freed by the prophets become the killers of the prophets. «قتلهم الانبیاء بغیر حقّ»

3- Man prepares the ground for his own downfall through disbelief (Kufr) and obstinacy. «طبع... بكفرهم»

4- The belief of a few individuals despite the disbelief (Kufr) of the majority proves that man possesses the power of choice and decision. Therefore, friends, environment, and a corrupt system should not change a person's will regarding the path of truth. «فلا یؤمنون الا قلیلا»

Ayah

وَبِكُفْرِهِمْ وَقَوْلِهِمْ عَلَىٰ مَرْيَمَ بُهْتَـٰنًا عَظِيمًا (۱٥٦)
And for their faithlessness, and their uttering a monstrous calumny against Mary, (156)

Key Points

The slander against Lady Maryam (Mary) is also mentioned in Ayah 27 of Surah Maryam. Attributing fornication to Lady Maryam was, in reality, attributing illegitimacy to Prophet Isa (Jesus) and implying His unworthiness for guidance and leadership. This very slander was the reason for their disbelief in Isa عليه السلام. The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said to Ali عليه السلام: "You are like Isa عليه السلام. A group attributed falsehoods to the mother of Isa, and another group considered Him God." [420] Perhaps slander (Tuhmat) is called Buhtan (a shocking falsehood) because chaste individuals are struck with astonishment (Baht) upon hearing such accusations. A great slander (Buhtan Azim) incurs a great punishment (Azab Azim). In Surah An-Noor, Ayah 23 states: "Indeed, those who accuse chaste women incur a great punishment."

420) Durr al-Manthur, Vol. 2, p. 238.

Messages

1- Slander (Buhtan) is placed in the same category as disbelief (Kufr). «بكفرهم و قولهم... بهتانا»

2- In a corrupt society, sometimes the vilest accusations are leveled against the purest individuals. «قولهم على مریم بهتانا»

Ayahs

وَقَوْلِهِمْ إِنَّا قَتَلْنَا ٱلْمَسِيحَ عِيسَى ٱبْنَ مَرْيَمَ رَسُولَ ٱللَّهِ وَمَا قَتَلُوهُ وَمَا صَلَبُوهُ وَلَـٰكِن شُبِّهَ لَهُمْ ۚ وَإِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱخْتَلَفُوا۟ فِيهِ لَفِى شَكٍّ مِّنْهُ ۚ مَا لَهُم بِهِۦ مِنْ عِلْمٍ إِلَّا ٱتِّبَاعَ ٱلظَّنِّ ۚ وَمَا قَتَلُوهُ يَقِينًۢا (۱٥۷)
and for their saying, ‘We killed the Messiah, Jesus son of Mary, the apostle of Allah’—though they did not kill him nor did they crucify him, but so it was made to appear to them. Indeed those who differ concerning him are surely in doubt about him: they do not have any knowledge of that beyond following conjectures, and certainly, they did not kill him. (157)
بَل رَّفَعَهُ ٱللَّهُ إِلَيْهِ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَزِيزًا حَكِيمًا (۱٥۸)
Indeed, Allah raised him up toward Himself, and Allah is all-mighty, all-wise. (158)

Key Points

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding the phrase, «و ما قتلوه و ما صلبوه و لكن شبّه» (and they did not kill Him, nor did they crucify Him, but [the matter] was made dubious to them): The occultation (Ghaybah) of Hadrat Qa'im عليه السلام is also like this. Indeed, the Ummah will deny it due to its prolonged duration. Some will say: Al-Qa'im has not been born. Some will say: He was born and has died. And others will say: Our eleventh [Imam] was barren, and so on... [421]

The factors and signs that led to the matter becoming dubious and another person being crucified instead of Hadrat Isa (Jesus) were as follows: 1- The arresting officers were Roman and foreign, and they did not know Isa عليه السلام. [422] 2- The attempt to arrest Isa was carried out at night. 3- The person arrested complained to God, which is inconsistent with the station of prophethood (Nubuwwah). 4- All the Christian Gospels that enthusiastically narrate the sacrifice and crucifixion of Isa were written many years after Isa, and the possibility of error in them is very high. 5- Groups of Christians do not accept the issue of the cross, and there are contradictions in the Gospels that make the subject ambiguous.

421) Al-Ghaybah by Tusi, p. 170. 422) It is probable that the person who intended treachery and introduced Isa to His opponents in exchange for money was himself transformed into the likeness of the Prophet and was arrested. Tafsir Furqan.

Messages

1- Sometimes the moral decline of man reaches a point where he boasts about killing a prophet. «انا قتلنا المسیح»

2- Clarity is necessary in the face of false claims. «و ما قتلوه و ماصلبوه»

3- Hadrat Isa's birth and His departure from this world were both unnatural. He ascended so that He might be a reserve for the future. «بل رَفعه اللّه الیه»

4- Ascension (Mi'raj) has occurred for others besides the Prophet of Islam. «بل رَفعه اللّه الیه»

5- Isa is Allah's guest. «رفعه الله الیه»

6- It is possible for humans to live in the heavens. «بل رفعه الله»

7- The Divine Will neutralizes all conspiracies. «كان الله عزیزاً حكیماً»

Ayah

وَإِن مِّنْ أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ إِلَّا لَيُؤْمِنَنَّ بِهِۦ قَبْلَ مَوْتِهِۦ ۖ وَيَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ يَكُونُ عَلَيْهِمْ شَهِيدًا (۱٥۹)
There is none among the People of the Book but will surely believe in him before his death; and on the Day of Resurrection, he will be a witness against them. (159)

Key Points

Perhaps the meaning is this: Before the death of Christ (عليه السلام), all the People of the Book will believe in Him. Because, according to Shi'a and Sunni narrations, Jesus (عليه السلام) will descend from the heavens at the End of Time, pray behind the Imam Zaman (عليه السلام), live for a period, and then pass away. On that day, Christians will bring true faith to Him, not the faith that considers Him the son of God. [423]

423) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- All disbelievers and deviants, at the threshold of death when the veils are lifted, become aware and believe, but it is of no benefit. «و ان من اهل الكتاب...»

2- Death is a definite tradition (Sunnah) for everyone, even for Jesus, who has been the guest of Allah and the angels for centuries under the best conditions. [424] «قبل موته»

3- Prophets are witnesses and testifiers over mankind. «یكون علیهم شهیدا»

424) If the intent of «قبل موته» (before his death) is the death of Prophet Jesus.

Ayah

فَبِظُلْمٍ مِّنَ ٱلَّذِينَ هَادُوا۟ حَرَّمْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ طَيِّبَـٰتٍ أُحِلَّتْ لَهُمْ وَبِصَدِّهِمْ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ كَثِيرًا (۱٦٠)
Due to the wrongdoing of the Jews, We prohibited them certain good things that were permitted to them [earlier], and for their barring many [people] from the way of Allah, (160)

Key Points

Perhaps the meaning of the good things (al-tayyibāt) that were forbidden to the Jews is the same as what is mentioned in Ayah 146 of Surah Al-An'am: «و على الّذین هادوا حَرّمنا كلّ ذى ظُفرٍ و من البقر و الغنم» This prohibition also exists in the current Torah. (425) The conditions and actions of the individual and society play a primary role in benefiting from divine blessings or in their removal. In Surah Al-Fajr, Ayah 17, we read that sometimes the reason for deprivation is neglect of orphans. «كلاّ بل لاتكرمون الیتیم» And in Ayah 96 of Surah Al-A'raf, we read: If the people of the towns had faith and piety, they would benefit from the blessings of the heavens. «و لو ان اهل القرى آمنوا و اتقوا لفتحنا علیهم بركات من السماء» Just as we read in a hadith: Those whose agricultural products become poor quality, it is because of the injustice (zulm) that they have committed. (426)

425) Torah, Book of Leviticus, Chapter 11. 426) Mustadrak, Vol. 13, p. 473.

Messages

1- Injustice (zulm) is the basis for deprivation of blessings. «فبظلمٍ... حرّمنا»

2- Sometimes economic deprivations and material hardships are signs of divine wrath and punishment. «فبظلمٍ... حرّمنا»

3- Prohibitions (harām) are not always due to health considerations. «فبظلمٍ... حرّمنا»

4- Although the main punishment is in the Hereafter, worldly punishments serve as a warning, a penalty for wrongdoers, and a test for the righteous. «فبظلمٍ... حرّمنا»

5- Blocking the path of Allah by any means (distortion, concealment, innovation, corruption, deviation, etc.) is a factor leading to deprivation of blessings. «و بصدهم عن سبیل الله»

Ayah

وَأَخْذِهِمُ ٱلرِّبَوٰا۟ وَقَدْ نُهُوا۟ عَنْهُ وَأَكْلِهِمْ أَمْوَٰلَ ٱلنَّاسِ بِٱلْبَـٰطِلِ ۚ وَأَعْتَدْنَا لِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ مِنْهُمْ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا (۱٦۱)
and for their taking usury—though they had been forbidden from it—and for eating up the wealth of the people wrongfully. And We have prepared for the faithless among them a painful punishment. (161)

Key Points

The ruling prohibiting usury (riba) is mentioned in the Torah, Deuteronomy, Chapter 23, verses 19 and 20.(427)

427) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- Although usury (riba) appears outwardly to be a source of income and a factor for prosperity, it is actually the cause of deprivation and punishment. «حرّمنا علیهم طیّبات... اخذهم الربا... عذاباً الیما»

2- All divine religions speak about and are sensitive regarding the financial relationships of people and how they earn and spend. «اخذهم الربا و قد نهوا عنه و اكلهم... بالباطل»

3- As long as oppression, usury, and consuming unlawful wealth have not placed a person within the sphere of disbelief (kufr), the path of return is easy. Otherwise, these types of sins are the foundation for disbelief and punishment. «اخذهم الربا... اعتدنا للكافرین»

Ayah

لَّـٰكِنِ ٱلرَّٰسِخُونَ فِى ٱلْعِلْمِ مِنْهُمْ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِمَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ وَمَآ أُنزِلَ مِن قَبْلِكَ ۚ وَٱلْمُقِيمِينَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ ۚ وَٱلْمُؤْتُونَ ٱلزَّكَوٰةَ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ سَنُؤْتِيهِمْ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا (۱٦۲)
But as for those who are firmly grounded in knowledge from among them, and the faithful, they believe in what has been sent down to you, and what was sent down before you—those who maintain the prayer, give the zakat, and believe in Allah and the Last Day—them We shall give a great reward. (162)

Key Points

From the fact that the phrase "و ما انزل من قبلك" (and what was sent down before you) has been mentioned repeatedly in the Qur'an, while the phrase "من بعدك" (after you) has never appeared, the finality (Khatamiyyah) of the religion of Islam can be understood. It is stated in a Hadith: The prayer (Salat) of one who does not pay Zakat is not accepted. The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله also used to expel from the mosque those who performed prayer but were not people of paying Zakat. And according to the statement of the Qur'an, the true worshipper is not stingy. «اذا مسّه الخیر منوعاً، الاّ المُصلّین»[428]

428) Al-Ma'arij, 21-22.

Messages

1- Fairness must be maintained in criticism. In this Ayah, the believers among the Jews are exempted from the oppressors and those who consume unlawful gain. «لكن الراسخون... منهم»

2- Knowledge is valuable when it is rooted and established in the soul. «الراسخون فى العلم»

3- Shared goals and beliefs are important, not race, tribe, or language. «الراسخون فى العلم منهم و المؤمنون»

4- Belief in all principles across all divine religions is necessary. «ما انزل الیك و ما انزل من قبلك»

5- Although prayer (Salat) and Zakat existed in all religions, they are mentioned separately due to their importance. «ما انزل الیك و ما انزل من قبلك و المقیمین الصلاة و...»

6- Prayer (Salat) is not separate from Zakat, and those who pray must also be people of financial charity. «المقیمین الصلوة و المؤتون الزكوة»

7- Prayer (Salat) holds a special status. (All phrases in the Ayah use the waw and nun ending [the nominative plural]: «راسخون، مؤمنون، مؤتون», but concerning prayer, the word «مقیمین» [the accusative/genitive plural] is used instead of «مقیمون», which in the Arabic language is a sign of its prominence.)

Ayah

۞ إِنَّآ أَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَيْكَ كَمَآ أَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰ نُوحٍ وَٱلنَّبِيِّـۧنَ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ ۚ وَأَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰٓ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَإِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَإِسْحَـٰقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ وَٱلْأَسْبَاطِ وَعِيسَىٰ وَأَيُّوبَ وَيُونُسَ وَهَـٰرُونَ وَسُلَيْمَـٰنَ ۚ وَءَاتَيْنَا دَاوُۥدَ زَبُورًا (۱٦۳)
We have indeed revealed to you as We revealed to Noah and the prophets after him, and [as] We revealed to Abraham and Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and the Tribes, Jesus and Job, Jonah, Aaron, and Solomon—and We gave David the Psalms— (163)

Key Points

The names of 25 prophets are mentioned throughout the entire Quran. The names of eleven of them are mentioned in this verse, and the names of the rest are: Adam, Idris, Hud, Salih, Lut, Yusuf, Shu'ayb, Dhul-Kifl, Musa, Ilyas, Al-Yasa', Zakariyya, Yahya, and 'Uzayr. It is stated in a hadith: What was revealed to the previous prophets has been revealed to the Prophet of Islam. [429] Asbāt (أسباط) is the plural of sibt (سِبط), meaning grandson, and is the title given to the descendants of Ya'qub (Jacob) who attained prophethood. Zabūr (زبور) literally means 'book,' but technically refers to the Book of Prophet Dawud (David). The "Psalms of David" (Mazāmīr Dāwūd) are among the books of the Old Testament (Torah) and contain 150 chapters, each of which is a "Psalm" (Mazmūr).

429) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

  1. Revelation (wahy) and prophethood are an uninterrupted process and an inviolable tradition in human history. "أَوْحَيْنَا إِلَىٰ نُوحٍ وَ..." (We revealed to Noah and...)

  2. Presenting historical examples of a subject (such as revelation to previous prophets) is one of the methods of proving a claim, and awareness of these examples prepares the ground for acceptance. "أَوْحَيْنَا إِلَيْكَ كَمَا أَوْحَيْنَا إِلَىٰ نُوحٍ..." (We revealed to you just as We revealed to Noah...)

  3. The goal, method, and general content of the divine religions are one, because the source of all of them is Allah. "إِنَّا أَوْحَيْنَا..." (Indeed, We revealed...)

  4. Despite the fact that divine revelation has been sent down repeatedly throughout history, why do the People of the Book not accept the prophethood of the Prophet, differentiating between him and other prophets and having excessive expectations? "أَوْحَيْنَا إِلَيْكَ كَمَا أَوْحَيْنَا إِلَىٰ نُوحٍ..." (We revealed to you just as We revealed to Noah...)

Ayah

وَرُسُلًا قَدْ قَصَصْنَـٰهُمْ عَلَيْكَ مِن قَبْلُ وَرُسُلًا لَّمْ نَقْصُصْهُمْ عَلَيْكَ ۚ وَكَلَّمَ ٱللَّهُ مُوسَىٰ تَكْلِيمًا (۱٦٤)
and apostles We have recounted to you earlier and apostles We have not recounted to you—and to Moses Allah spoke directly— (164)

Key Points

Among the chapters (Surahs) of the Quran, Surahs Hud and Shu'ara possess greater comprehensiveness in narrating the stories of the Prophets (Qisas al-Anbiya). All Prophets are addressed by God, but among them, only Prophet Musa عليه السلام was designated as "Kalim Allah" (The one who spoke to Allah). Perhaps this was because Prophet Musa, in his struggle against Pharaoh and in enduring the stubbornness and obstinacy of the Children of Israel, needed to be connected with Allah more frequently and continuously to receive messages. Therefore, He became Kalim Allah.

Messages

1- Familiarity with the history of the Prophets is so beneficial and instructive that Allah Himself has addressed it. «رسلاً قد قصصناهم علیك»

2- The number of Prophets is greater than those mentioned in the Quran. «و رسلاً لم نقصصهم»

3- Neither is human life long enough to hear all of history, nor is there a need to hear all of history. One should hear only what provides a lesson (or warning). «رسلاً لم نقصصهم»

4- Sometimes the narration of a single event is repeated many times in the Quran, while some events are not mentioned at all. This indicates that the Quran is a book of guidance and education, not mere storytelling. «لم نقصصهم»

Ayah

رُّسُلًا مُّبَشِّرِينَ وَمُنذِرِينَ لِئَلَّا يَكُونَ لِلنَّاسِ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ حُجَّةٌۢ بَعْدَ ٱلرُّسُلِ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَزِيزًا حَكِيمًا (۱٦٥)
apostles, as bearers of good news and warners, so that mankind may not have any argument against Allah, after the [sending of the] apostles; and Allah is all-mighty, all-wise. (165)

Key Points

This Ayah explains the general principles of the prophets' invitation (Da'wah), their method of work, and the goal of their mission (Bi'that). Reason (Aql), like Revelation (Wahy), is a Divine proof (Hujjah), but because the scope of its perceptions is limited, it is not sufficient alone and is not the complete proof (Hujjah Tammah). Therefore, the prophets convey knowledge of the unseen (Ghaybi), the celestial realm (Malakuti), and the Hereafter (Akhirawi), which is beyond the reach of reason. The Quran states elsewhere: «و لو انا اهلكناهم بعذاب من قبله لقالوا ربنا لولا ارسلت الینا رسولاً»[430] If We had destroyed them with a punishment before sending the messengers, they would have said: Why did You not send us an articulate and warning messenger?

430) Taha, 134.

Messages

1- The method of the prophets' propagation (Tabligh) is based on two axes: fear (warning) and hope (good tidings). «مبشّرین و منذرین»

2- The philosophy behind the mission (Bi'that) of the prophets is the completion of Allah's proof (Hujjah) upon the people, so that they cannot say: We did not have a leader or guide, and we did not know. «لئلا یكون للناس على اللّه حجّة»

3- The prophets, in terms of personal and physical attributes, life history, social, political, and familial standing, as well as possessing miracles (Mu'jizah), clear articulation, and benefiting from unseen Divine aid, must be at a level where no one can find any excuse against them in any area. «لئلایكون للناس على اللّه حجّة بعد الرسل»

4- Since Allah is Almighty (Aziz) and Wise (Hakim), no one has any proof (Hujjah) against Him. «عزیزاًحكیما»

Ayah

لَّـٰكِنِ ٱللَّهُ يَشْهَدُ بِمَآ أَنزَلَ إِلَيْكَ ۖ أَنزَلَهُۥ بِعِلْمِهِۦ ۖ وَٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ يَشْهَدُونَ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ شَهِيدًا (۱٦٦)
But Allah bears witness to what He has sent down to you—He sent it down with His knowledge—and the angels bear witness [too], and Allah quite suffices as witness. (166)

Key Points

The fact that the sublime teachings of the Quran were expressed by an unlettered person, in a region characterized by ignorance, polytheism, and enmity, and that people were transformed—moving from division to unity, from stinginess to altruism, from polytheism to monotheism, from ignorance to knowledge, and from decline to spiritual ascent, culminating in the formation of the "Islamic Nation" (Ummah Islamiyyah)—is Allah's testimony to the favor He has bestowed upon the Prophet and His religion.

Messages

1- In the face of unjust weakening (or undermining), appropriate strengthening must take place. «لكن الله یشهد»

2- Even the Prophets need Allah's support in the path of their mission (Da'wah). «لكن الله یشهد»

3- The best reliance and point of hope for every religious preacher (Muballigh) must be Allah. «لكن اللّه یشهد»

4- The source of revelation (Wahy) is the infinite Divine knowledge. «انزله بعلمه» Therefore, with the advancement of sciences, a new layer is unveiled from the teachings of the Quran every day.

5- Even if stubborn sinners make excuses in one corner of the earth, all the angels of existence bear witness in your favor. «والملائكه یشهدون»

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَصَدُّوا۟ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ قَدْ ضَلُّوا۟ ضَلَـٰلًۢا بَعِيدًا (۱٦۷)
Indeed those who are faithless and bar [others] from the way of Allah, have certainly strayed into far error. (167)
إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَظَلَمُوا۟ لَمْ يَكُنِ ٱللَّهُ لِيَغْفِرَ لَهُمْ وَلَا لِيَهْدِيَهُمْ طَرِيقًا (۱٦۸)
Indeed those who are faithless and do wrong, Allah shall never forgive them, nor shall He guide them to any way, (168)

Key Points

|

Ayah

إِلَّا طَرِيقَ جَهَنَّمَ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَآ أَبَدًا ۚ وَكَانَ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ يَسِيرًا (۱٦۹)
except the way to hell, to remain in it forever, and that is easy for Allah. (169)

Key Points

The meaning of "ضلال بعید" (far-reaching error) is the compounded deviation of the disbelievers. Disbelief is one deviation, and preventing others from believing is another deviation. Disbelief is one deviation, and considering oneself to be upon the truth is a higher deviation. Disbelief is oppression against oneself, and blocking the path of others is oppression against history and the human race. Perhaps the meaning of "ظلم" (oppression) in Ayah 168 is precisely the act of preventing others from guidance. For what oppression is greater than intellectual, cultural, and ideological oppression? The severest punishments and the worst descriptions are mentioned for those disbelievers who, through various forms of propaganda, accusations, and threats, prevent the message of truth from reaching the ears of those seeking truth. (Lack of forgiveness, lack of guidance, being consigned to Hellfire, and eternal Hell).

Messages

1- Those who prevent people from guidance are themselves in severe deviation. «ضلّوا ضلالا بعیدا»

2- Disbelief and oppression are side by side, and their result is deprivation from divine forgiveness and guidance. «كفروا و ظلموا... لا لیهدیهم»

3- The punishment for the oppressive disbelievers who prevent people from guidance is eternal Hellfire. «جهنّم خالدین فیها»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ قَدْ جَآءَكُمُ ٱلرَّسُولُ بِٱلْحَقِّ مِن رَّبِّكُمْ فَـَٔامِنُوا۟ خَيْرًا لَّكُمْ ۚ وَإِن تَكْفُرُوا۟ فَإِنَّ لِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًا (۱۷٠)
O mankind! The Apostle has certainly brought you the truth from your Lord. So have faith! That is better for you. And if you are faithless, [you should know that] to Allah indeed belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth, and Allah is all-knowing, all-wise. (170)

Key Points

The People of the Book and even the polytheists were awaiting the advent of a Prophet and had a mental readiness. Therefore, in this Ayah, instead of saying rasūlan (a prophet), He says: الرسول (the Prophet), meaning that the very Prophet they were awaiting has now come, so believe in Him.

Messages

  1. The call of Islam is universal and is not exclusive to the Arab nation. یا ایّها النّاس (O mankind).
  2. The factor for the spread of the Prophets' call is its truthfulness. بالحق (with the truth).
  3. Faith is for the benefit of the people themselves, and they should not feel they are doing Allah a favor by their faith. Rather, Allah has conferred a favor by guiding us. فآمنوا خیرلكم (so believe, it is better for you).
  4. Neither the disbelief of the people harms Allah, nor does their faith benefit Him. فآمنوا خیر لكم و ان تكفروا فانّ للّه ما فى السموات... (so believe, it is better for you; and if you disbelieve, then indeed, to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens...).
  5. The mission of the Prophets and the content of their call are based on Divine knowledge and wisdom. كان اللّه علیماً حكیما (Allah is ever Knowing, Wise).

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَهْلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لَا تَغْلُوا۟ فِى دِينِكُمْ وَلَا تَقُولُوا۟ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ إِلَّا ٱلْحَقَّ ۚ إِنَّمَا ٱلْمَسِيحُ عِيسَى ٱبْنُ مَرْيَمَ رَسُولُ ٱللَّهِ وَكَلِمَتُهُۥٓ أَلْقَىٰهَآ إِلَىٰ مَرْيَمَ وَرُوحٌ مِّنْهُ ۖ فَـَٔامِنُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ وَرُسُلِهِۦ ۖ وَلَا تَقُولُوا۟ ثَلَـٰثَةٌ ۚ ٱنتَهُوا۟ خَيْرًا لَّكُمْ ۚ إِنَّمَا ٱللَّهُ إِلَـٰهٌ وَٰحِدٌ ۖ سُبْحَـٰنَهُۥٓ أَن يَكُونَ لَهُۥ وَلَدٌ ۘ لَّهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ وَكَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ وَكِيلًا (۱۷۱)
O People of the Book! Do not exceed the bounds in your religion, and do not attribute anything to Allah except the truth. The Messiah, Jesus son of Mary, was only an apostle of Allah, and His Word that He cast toward Mary and a spirit from Him. So have faith in Allah and His apostles, and do not say, ‘[God is] a trinity.’ Relinquish [such a creed]! That is better for you. Allah is but the One God. He is far too immaculate to have any son. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, and Allah suffices as trustee. (171)

Key Points

"The Word of Allah" (Kalimatullah) is applied to the Prophets and the Awliya (saints/successors), because just as a word expresses the thoughts in the speaker's mind, the Prophets and the Awliya express the perfections of the Lord. As stated in a Hadith: «نحن كلمات اللّه التّامات» (We are the complete Divine Words).[431] Perhaps the intent of the word (Kalimah) is the phrase «كن فیكون» (Be, and it is), which is the saying of Allah, and like any other speech, its product is that word.[432] Just as elsewhere, Allah likens the creation of Jesus عليه السلام to the creation of Adam عليه السلام, saying: «انّ مثل عیسى عنداللّه كمثل آدم خلقه من تراب ثمّ قال له كن فیكون» [433] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was asked about «روح منه» (a spirit from Him). The Imam replied: The intent is the Spirit of Allah, which Allah placed in Adam and Jesus علیهما السلام.[434]

Christians believe in the Trinity and consider Allah as God the Father, Christ as God the Son, and Gabriel as the intermediary between these two gods, which is a form of disbelief (Kufr). The Quran also states: «لقد كفر الّذین قالوا انّ اللّه ثالث ثلاثة» [435] (Those who say that Allah is one of three gods are disbelievers). Of course, a group among them also considers Lady Mary, instead of Gabriel, as one of the three gods, concerning which the Quran states: «یا عیسى بن مریم ءانت قلت للناس اتّخذونى و امّى الهَین من دون اللّه» [436] (O Jesus, son of Mary! Did you say to the people, 'Take me and my mother as two gods besides Allah'?).

In narrations, those who commit exaggeration (Ghuluw) have been cursed, and they are counted among the worst types of disbelievers (Kuffar) and polytheists (Mushrikun).[437] If lacking a father were proof of divinity, then Prophet Adam should possess divinity a fortiori, since he had neither a father nor a mother.

431) Tafsir Atayeb al-Bayan. 432) Tafsir Al-Mizan and Fi Zilal al-Quran. 433) Al Imran, 59. 434) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 133. 435) Ma'idah, 73. 436) Ma'idah, 115. 437) Mizan al-Hikmah.

Messages

1- Exaggeration (Ghuluw) is a sign of misplaced fanaticism and is forbidden in all religions. The Jews considered 'Uzayr (Ezra) the son of God, and the Christians considered 'Isa (Jesus) the son of God. «لاتغلوا فى دینكم» (Do not exaggerate in your religion).

2- One of the afflictions of heavenly religions is exaggeration concerning religious leaders. «لاتغلوافى دینكم»

3- Moderation and balance are necessary everywhere, and love for the Awliya of Allah should not lead to exaggeration. «لاتغلوا فى دینكم»

4- Exaggerating concerning human beings, even if they are Prophets, is an insult to Allah and attributing falsehood to Him. «لاتقولوا على الله الاالحقّ» (Say nothing concerning Allah except the truth).

5- In the Quran and the Gospel, Prophet Jesus is introduced as the Messenger of Allah, not as God or the son of God.[438] «عیسى بن مریم رسول اللّه» (Jesus, son of Mary, is the Messenger of Allah).

6- The unnatural creation of Jesus is an Ayah (sign) and proof of Allah, just as a word is a signpost and indicator of meaning. «كلمته القاها» (His Word which He conveyed).

7- Jesus is not a part of God, but rather a reflection of the Divine life-giving power. «روح منه» (A spirit from Him).

8- The Quran expresses the correct belief and negates false beliefs. «لاتغلوا...لاتقولوا ثلاثة...» (Do not exaggerate... Do not say three...).

9- Allah is not a body that He should have a son. «سبحانه ان یكون له ولد» (Glory be to Him that He should have a son). (Expressions such as Tharullah, Yadullah, Kalimatullah found in religious texts are metaphorical expressions).

10- The God to Whom the heavens belong, what need does He have for a son or a partner? «سبحانه ان یكون له ولد له ما فى السموات» (Glory be to Him that He should have a son. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens).

11- Everything belongs to Allah, so one must be His servant and rely only upon Him. «له ما فى السموات و ما فى الارض و كفى باللّه وكیلا» (To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth. And sufficient is Allah as a Trustee).

438) In the Quran, Jesus is attributed to Mary 17 times to negate the idea of Him being the son of God. In the Gospels, He is also referred to as the Messenger of God, not God. In the Gospels, Prophet Jesus عليه السلام introduced Himself 80 times as the servant and messenger of God. Tafsir Furqan.

Ayah

لَّن يَسْتَنكِفَ ٱلْمَسِيحُ أَن يَكُونَ عَبْدًا لِّلَّهِ وَلَا ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ ٱلْمُقَرَّبُونَ ۚ وَمَن يَسْتَنكِفْ عَنْ عِبَادَتِهِۦ وَيَسْتَكْبِرْ فَسَيَحْشُرُهُمْ إِلَيْهِ جَمِيعًا (۱۷۲)
The Messiah would never disdain being a servant of Allah, nor would the angels brought near [to Him]. And whoever disdains His worship and is arrogant, He will gather them all toward Him. (172)

Key Points

Imam Reza عليه السلام said to the Catholicos (Jathaliq), the leader of the Christians at that time: Everything about Jesus was good, except that he was not devoted to worship. The Catholicos became upset and said: He performed the most worship. The Imam asked: Whom did he worship? The Catholicos fell silent, realizing that the Imam's goal was to make him understand that a "worshipper" ('abid) cannot be the "Worshipped" (ma'bud).

People have three attitudes towards the worship of Allah: a group that is arrogant (haughty), a group that is submissive, and a group that is seeking (eager). Imam Ali عليه السلام says: «كفى بى عزّاً ان اكون لك عبداً و كفى بى فخراً ان تكون لى ربّا» It is sufficient honor for me that I am Your servant. [439]

439) Bihar, Vol. 94, p. 92.

Messages

1- Do not be more zealous than necessary in religious matters (literally: do not be a bowl hotter than the soup). Jesus considers himself a servant of Allah, so why do you recognize him as the son of God?! «لن یستنكف المسیح ان یكون عبداً لِلّه»

2- Christ not only worships, but He is a complete "servant" ('abd) and entirely devoted to Him. «عبداً لِلّه»

3- The closest angels (including the Holy Spirit) perform servitude to Allah. Why do you consider the Holy Spirit one of the three gods? «ولاالملائكة المقرّبون»

4- The source of abandoning worship is arrogance, and if the spirit becomes arrogant, it entails all kinds of danger. «و من یستنكف عن عبادته و یستكبر...»

5- The return of everyone is towards Allah, so let us fear the Day of Judgment and not be arrogant. «فسیحشرهم الیه جمیعا»

Ayah

فَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ فَيُوَفِّيهِمْ أُجُورَهُمْ وَيَزِيدُهُم مِّن فَضْلِهِۦ ۖ وَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَنكَفُوا۟ وَٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ فَيُعَذِّبُهُمْ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا وَلَا يَجِدُونَ لَهُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَلِيًّا وَلَا نَصِيرًا (۱۷۳)
As for those who have faith and do righteous deeds, He will pay them in full their rewards, and He will enhance them out of His grace. But those who are disdainful and arrogant, He will punish them with a painful punishment, and they will not find besides Allah any guardian or helper. (173)

Key Points

The People of the Book either considered their prophet superior and exaggerated concerning him, or they considered themselves superior and said: «نحن ابناء اللّه» [440], or they belittled others. «قالت الیهود ليست النصارى على شى ء...» [441]. However, on the Day of Judgment, none of these things will be effective; faith and righteous deeds are necessary.

440) Ma'idah, 18. 441) Baqarah, 11.

Messages

1- The path to salvation is faith and righteous deeds. «آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات فیوفّیهم اجورهم»

2- Faith precedes action, and deeds without faith are like currency without backing. «آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات فیوفّیهم اجورهم»

3- If you wish to possess Divine ethics, pay the worker's wage in full and give something extra as well. «فیوفّیهم اجورهم و یزیدهم من فضله»

4- Without faith and deeds, do not expect any intercession from Christ or others. «لایجدون لهم من دون اللّه ولیّاً ولانصیراً»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ قَدْ جَآءَكُم بُرْهَـٰنٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ وَأَنزَلْنَآ إِلَيْكُمْ نُورًا مُّبِينًا (۱۷٤)
O mankind! Certainly, a proof has come to you from your Lord, and We have sent down to you a manifest light. (174)

Key Points

According to narrations, the meaning of al-Burhān (the Proof) is the person of the Prophet, and the meaning of Nūran Mubīnan (Manifest Light) is the Quran. And truly, the Prophet is the Proof of the religion, because an unlettered person brought such profound knowledge and a Book, that the more time passes and sciences develop, the clearer the truthfulness of the religion and the depth of its teachings become.

Messages

1- Islam speaks to all people, all generations, and all eras, and its message is universal. «یا ایهاالناس»

2- Sending down light and proof is among the affairs of Allah's Lordship. «من ربّكم»

3- The Quran is a book of reasoning, guidance, and light. «نوراً مبینا»

Ayah

فَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱعْتَصَمُوا۟ بِهِۦ فَسَيُدْخِلُهُمْ فِى رَحْمَةٍ مِّنْهُ وَفَضْلٍ وَيَهْدِيهِمْ إِلَيْهِ صِرَٰطًا مُّسْتَقِيمًا (۱۷٥)
As for those who have faith in Allah, and hold fast to Him, He will admit them to His mercy and grace, and He will guide them on a straight path to Him. (175)

Key Points

It is possible that the meaning of holding fast to Allah (اعتصام به خداوند) in this Ayah is holding fast to the Divine Guardians and leaders who prevent division and dispersion. In Ziyarat Jami'ah, we read: "Whoever holds fast to you, has certainly held fast to Allah." In the two preceding Ayahs, faith and righteous deeds were mentioned as the means of receiving Divine favor, and in this Ayah, faith and holding fast to Allah are mentioned. This indicates that the path to holding fast to Allah and joining Him is through righteous deeds.

Messages

1- Divine rewards are His mercy and grace, not our entitlement. [442] «فى رحمةمنه وفضل»

2- Although Allah guides whomever He wills, «یهدى من یشاء» [443] we must ourselves prepare the ground and means for guidance through faith and holding fast. «یهدیهم الیه»

3- The need for Divine guidance remains even after faith and holding fast; we are not independent of His guidance for a single moment. «یهدیهم الیه»

4- The straight path is the path that leads man to Allah. «یهدیهم الیه صراطاً مستقیماً»

442) And the fact that He considers this mercy and grace of His in other Ayahs as the reward and recompense for our deeds is itself another favor; otherwise, the wage for a few minutes of imperfect work would not be all this Paradise, palaces, and mercy.

443) Ibrahim, 4.

Ayah

يَسْتَفْتُونَكَ قُلِ ٱللَّهُ يُفْتِيكُمْ فِى ٱلْكَلَـٰلَةِ ۚ إِنِ ٱمْرُؤٌا۟ هَلَكَ لَيْسَ لَهُۥ وَلَدٌ وَلَهُۥٓ أُخْتٌ فَلَهَا نِصْفُ مَا تَرَكَ ۚ وَهُوَ يَرِثُهَآ إِن لَّمْ يَكُن لَّهَا وَلَدٌ ۚ فَإِن كَانَتَا ٱثْنَتَيْنِ فَلَهُمَا ٱلثُّلُثَانِ مِمَّا تَرَكَ ۚ وَإِن كَانُوٓا۟ إِخْوَةً رِّجَالًا وَنِسَآءً فَلِلذَّكَرِ مِثْلُ حَظِّ ٱلْأُنثَيَيْنِ ۗ يُبَيِّنُ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمْ أَن تَضِلُّوا۟ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمٌۢ (۱۷٦)
They ask you for a ruling. Say, ‘Allah gives you a ruling concerning the kalalah: If a man dies and has no children [or parents], but has a sister, for her shall be a half of what he leaves, and he shall inherit from her if she has no children. If there be two sisters, then they shall receive two-thirds of what he leaves. But if there be [several] brothers and sisters, then for the male shall be the like of the share of two females. Allah explains [the laws] for you lest you should go astray, and Allah has knowledge of all things.’ (176)

Key Points

In Ayah 12 of this same Surah, it was mentioned that a sister and brother inherit one-sixth from each other, but here (in this Ayah) it says: half. This is because there, the reference was to maternal siblings (sister and brother from the mother's side), and here it refers to paternal siblings, or full siblings (paternal and maternal).[444] Siblings inherit from the deceased only when the deceased has no children or parents. Otherwise, the siblings do not inherit (it is not their turn). Surah An-Nisa began with family matters and concluded with family matters.

444) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- It is the duty of the people to refer to religious leaders to learn religious issues and rulings. «یستفتونك»

2- Religion has a program for the worldly life of the people. The subject of inheritance is an economic matter from one perspective and a family matter from another, and religion has established rulings for it considering both aspects. «قل اللّه یفتیكم»

3- Observing priorities in the division of inheritance indicates priorities in attending to family members. Parents and children are in one rank, but sisters and brothers are in the next stage. «لیس له ولد و له اخت»

4- The share of inheritance for a man being double that of a woman is based on Divine knowledge, not on specific social conditions during the time of the Prophet when women were considered weak. «واللّه بكل شى ء علیم»

End of Nisa
